prayers.text:
The Departed
O Lord, O Thou whose mercy hath encompassed all, Whose forbearance is transcendent, Whose munificence is sublime, Whose clemency and generosity encompass all things, and the lights of Whose forgiveness are diffused throughout the world! O Lord of glory!

I implore Thee, with fervour and tears in mine eyes, to turn the glance of Thine eye of mercy towards Thy handmaid, who hath ascended unto Thee.

Clothe her with the robe of Thy grace, which shineth with the adornments of the celestial Paradise, and, beneath the Tree of Thy oneness, illumine her countenance with the light of Thy mercy and compassion.

O God! Confer upon Thy heavenly handmaid the holy fragrances proceeding from the spirit of Thy forbearance.

Make her to abide in a blessed abode, heal her sorrows with the balm of reunion with Thee, and, according to Thy will, suffer her to enter Thy holy Paradise.

Cause the angels of Thy loving-kindness to descend upon her in succession, and shelter her beneath Thy blessed Tree.

Thou art, verily, the Ever-Forgiving, the Most Generous, the Bountiful.
prayers.name: Difuntos (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O Kind Lord! This dearly beloved servant was drawn towards Thee, and through reflection and discernment, yearned to attain Thy presence and enter Thy dominions. With eyes full of tears, she fixed her gaze upon the Kingdom of Mysteries. Many nights she spent in close communion with Thee, and many days she lived with the intimate remembrance of Thee. Every morn she remembered Thee, and every eve she centered her thoughts upon Thee. Like a melodious nightingale, she sang Thy sacred verses, and, like a mirror, she aspired to reflect Thy light.

O Forgiver of sins! Open the way for this awakened soul to enter into Thy Kingdom, and permit this bird, trained by Thy hand, to wing its flight in the eternal rose garden. She is aflame with the desire to draw nigh unto Thee; permit her to attain Thy presence. She is sorrowful and grieved at her separation from Thee; cause her to be admitted into Thy Celestial Mansion.

O Lord! We are sinners, but Thou art the Forgiver. We are submerged in the ocean of imperfections, but Thou art the Indulgent, the Kind. Forgive our sins and bless us with Thy abundant grace. Grant us the privilege of beholding Thy Countenance and give us the cup of joy and gladness. We are captives of our own transgressions, and Thou art the King of Bountiful Favors. We are submerged in a sea of iniquities, and Thou art the Lord of Infinite Mercy. Thou art the Giver, the Glorious, the Eternal, the Munificent; and Thou art the Most Kind, the All-Merciful, the Omnipotent, Who bestoweth gifts and forgiveth sins. Verily, unto Thee do we turn for the remission of our faults, the Lord of Lords.
prayers.name: The Departed (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O Thou merciful God! All praise be unto Thee, for Thou hast awakened me and made me conscious. Thou hast given me a seeing eye and a hearing ear, hast led me to Thy kingdom and guided me to Thy path. Thou hast shown me the right way and granted me to enter the ark of salvation. O God! Cause me to remain steadfast and make me strong and faithful. Protect me from grievous afflictions, and preserve and guard me within Thy covenant and Thy testament's strongly fortified fortress. Thou art the Mighty. Thou art the Seeing. Thou art the Hearing.
 
O thou merciful God! Grant me a heart which, like unto a glass, may be illumined by the light of Thy love, and bestow upon me thoughts that may change the world into a rose garden through the outpourings of heavenly grace.
 
Thou art the Merciful, the Compassionate. Thou art the Great, Benevolent God.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Now a new Message has come, causing dark places to receive light. This Message causes the Holy House of God to be established, the Scriptures to be made clear, and a new Path to be opened. I ask Thee to grant me, and all who are near unto me, whatsoever will help us to ascend unto the heaven of Thy glory, which is most exalted. And graciously bestow upon us that which will cleanse us from the evil imaginings that cause some to disbelieve in Thy unity.

## O my Lord! I am one who has clung to the cord of Thy love and Thy tenderness.
And I have grasped the hem of the garment of Thy mercy and Thy grace. I ask Thee to grant me, and them whom I love, the good things of this world and of the world to come. There is a hidden Gift which Thou dost wish to bestow upon Thy chosen ones. Graciously grant this Gift unto us!

## O my Lord! These days are as the time when Thou didst command Thy servants to abstain from food and drink.
Blessed indeed is he who abstains, for he loves Thee and renounces all else but Thee alone. Aid me, and aid all, O my Lord, that we may heed Thee and follow Thy laws. Verily, Thou hast the power to do whatsoever Thou wilt.

There is no other God but Thee. Thou art unique. Thou knowest all things. Thou art exceeding wise. Praises be to God, the Lord of all worlds.
prayers.name: Praises be to Thee, O God, my Lord! (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O Thou, merciful Lord! Forgive the sins of my dear mother, absolve her imperfections, grant her a glimpse of Thy Providence and conduct her unto Thy Kingdom.
O God! She hath reared me from my infancy, nurtured me, but I have not been able to repay her for her services. Do Thou give her recompense. Destine for her eternal life and make her beloved in Thy Kingdom.
Verily, Thou art the Forgiving, the Kind, the Benevolent.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
(This prayer is to be recited only for Bahá’ís who are of age. “It is a compulsory Bahá’í prayer which is recited in congregation; it should be recited by one of the believers while all the attendees stand…” – Kitáb-I-Aqdas)  

O my God! This is Thy servant and the child of Thy servant who hath believed in Thee and in Thy signs, and hath turned his face toward Thee, and hath detached himself wholly from all save Thee alone.  

Thou art indeed the Compassionate, the All-Merciful. Forgive, O Thou Who art forgiving of the sins of men and concealest their misdeeds, as is meet from Thy bounty and Thy ocean of grace. Admit him into the sanctuary of Thy great mercy who was present before the foundations of earth and heaven were laid. There is no God save Thee, the Ever-Forgiving, the All-Bountiful.  

He who recites the prayer shall say:  

“Alláh-u-Abhá” (once)  

All of us, indeed, worship God (19)  

“Alláh-u-Abhá” (once)  

All of us, indeed, bow down before God (19)  

“Alláh-u-Abhá” (once)  

All of us, indeed, are devoted to God (19)  

“Alláh-u-Abhá” (once)  

All of us, indeed, praise God (19)  

“Alláh-u-Abhá” (once)  

All of us, indeed, thank God (19)  

“Alláh-u-Abhá” (once)  

All of us, indeed, have faith in God (19) 

 Bahá’u’lláh  

(If the deceased is a woman, he who recites the prayer shall say: This is Thy handmaid and the child of Thy handmaid………)  

 

O my God! O Thou forgiving of sins, Bestower of gifts, Remover of trials! Indeed, I beg of Thee to forgive the sins of those who have left the dust of their bodies and ascended to the spiritual world.  

O my Lord! Purify them from transgressions, remove from them the sorrow, and transform their darkness into light. Make them to enter the garden of joy, cleanse them with pure water, and suffer them to behold Thy light upon the most exalted summit.
prayers.name: THE DECEASED (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O God, my God! This Thy handmaid is calling unto Thee, trusting in Thee, turning her face towards Thee, beseeching Thee to rain down Thy heavenly gifts upon her, and to show unto her Thy spiritual secrets and to turn unto her the lights of Thy Divine attributes.

         O my Lord! Make the eyes of my husband to see. Fervently incline his heart to the light of knowledge of Thee, direct his mind to Thy resplendent beauty, and gladden his soul by revealing unto him Thy manifest splendors. O my Lord! Remove this veil that doth cover his sight. Rain upon him Thy abundant bestowals, insobriate him with the wine of love for Thee, make him as one of Thy angels whose feet walk this earth even though their souls soar in the highest heavens. Grant that he may become a shining lamp, burning with the light of Thy wisdom amongst Thy people. Verily Thou art the Beloved, the Ever-Bestowing, the Open of Hand.
prayers.name: For the Husband (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
* Bahá'u'lláh ordains in the 'Kitáb-i-Aqdas' that this prayer shall be recited, in its entirety, for the deceased at a Bahá'í burial. It is read before the interment of a person while the others stand.


O my God! This is Thy servant and the son of Thy servant who hath believed in Thee and in Thy signs, and turned his face toward Thee, entirely detached from all except Thee. Verily, of those who show mercy, Thou art the Most Merciful.

Deal with him, O Thou Who art the forgiver of men's sins and the concealer of their faults, as befits the heaven of Thy bounty and the ocean of Thy grace. Suffer him to enter the gates of Thy all-encompassing mercy, which hath existed before the foundation of earth and heaven was laid. There is no God but Thee, the Ever-Forgiving, the Most Bountiful.

Then let him repeat the greeting 'Alláh-u-Abhá' six times, and then repeat each of the following sentences nineteen times:

Verily, we all worship God. Verily, we all bow down to God.

Verily, we are all devoted unto God.

Verily, we all praise God. Verily, we all thank God.

Verily, we are all patient in God.


*(If the deceased is a woman, let him say: "This is Thy handmaid and the daughter of Thy handmaid, etc.")
*(The Universal House of Justice clarifies in a letter dated July 1, 2001, that the repetition of the sentences proceeds as follows: 'Alláh-u-Abhá' [once], 'Verily, we all worship God.' [19 times], 'Alláh-u-Abhá' [once], 'Verily, we all bow down to God.' [19 times], and so on.)
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Huwalláh
O Lord, these souls yearn for the Vision, and the frenzied lovers of that musk-scented tress, each moment cry out, and raise a lament and wail, and ask for a favour, and seek a gift; Thou art the Bountiful, the Merciful, and Thou art the Compassionate, the All-Merciful, and Thou art the Bestower of endless grace. O Lord, the Bountiful, make these souls steadfast in their covenant, firm in their covenant, draw them to the Divine Fragrances of holiness, remind them of the signs of Thy unity, make them recipients of the moments of Thy perfect mercy, and enamoured of the beauty of Thy effulgent light, grant them fresh confirmations each moment, and waft to them, with every breath, the breezes of Thy grace. Thou art potent and powerful, and Thou art the Bestower, the Generous, the All-Knowing.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
He is God
O Incomparable Lord, we bring our needs and our prayers, that You make these servants intimate with Your secret, and make them hearers of the call of the Herald from the Exalted Sphere. Establish them firm on the path of Your covenant and Your promise, and make them companions in the remembrance of Your blessed Beauty. Let them drink from the cup of Your Oneness, and taste the honey of Your favor, and bestow upon them Your ancient, magnificent grace. Grant this servant of Your threshold entrance into Your absolute court, and do not disappoint this hopeful one of Your mercy, and make them firm and steadfast in covenant and faith.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
He is the Healer, the Satisfier, the Helper, the Perfect Forgiver, the Most Merciful. 

         I call upon Thee, O Glorious, O True, O Thou who art Majestic! Thou art the Satisfaction, the Comfort of the Heart, the Ever-Constant, O Thou who art Faithful!

         I call upon Thee, O King, O Exalter, O Judge! Thou art the Satisfaction, the Comfort of the Heart, the Ever-Constant, O Thou who art Faithful!

         I call upon Thee, O Peerless, O Immortal, O Thou who art Unique! Thou art the Satisfaction, the Comfort of the Heart, the Ever-Constant, O Thou who art Faithful!

        I call upon Thee, O Most Praised, O Holy, O Thou who art Helper! Thou art the Satisfaction, the Comfort of the Heart, the Ever-Constant, O Thou who art Faithful!

         I call upon Thee, O Omniscient, O Most Wise, O Thou who art Most Honored! Thou art the Satisfaction, the Comfort of the Heart, the Ever-Constant, O Thou who art Faithful!

         I call upon Thee, O Gentle, O Royal, O Thou who art the Giver of Command! Thou art the Satisfaction, the Comfort of the Heart, the Ever-Constant, O Thou who art Faithful!

         I call upon Thee, O Beloved, O Desired, O Thou who art Full of Joy! Thou art the Satisfaction, the Comfort of the Heart, the Ever-Constant, O Thou who art Faithful!

         I call upon Thee, O Most Strong, O Abiding, O Thou who art Almighty! Thou art the Satisfaction, the Comfort of the Heart, the Ever-Constant, O Thou who art Faithful!

         I call upon Thee, O Commander, O Self-Sufficient, O Thou who knowest all! Thou art the Satisfaction, the Comfort of the Heart, the Ever-Constant, O Thou who art Faithful!

         I call upon Thee, O Spirit! O Luminous, O Thou who art Most Manifest! Thou art the Satisfaction, the Comfort of the Heart, the Ever-Constant, O Thou who art Faithful!

         I call upon Thee, O Most Near to All, O Thou who art Known to All, O Thou who art Hidden from All! Thou art the Satisfaction, the Comfort of the Heart, the Ever-Constant, O Thou who art Faithful!
prayers.name: The High Prayer of Healing (LLM translated)

ceb.cat: Ang Taas nga Pangadyeon sa Pag-alim

th.cat: บทอธิษฐานสำหรับคู่สมรส

prayers.text:
I beseech Thee by Thy Most Exalted Name to open the eyes of Thy servants, that they may behold Thee shining above the horizon of Thy majesty and glory, and that the croaking of the raven may not deter them from hearing the voice of the Dove of Thy transcendent oneness, nor corrupt waters prevent them from sipping the pure wine of Thy bounty and drinking from the everlasting streams of Thy gifts.

Gather them then around this divine Law, whose covenant Thou hast established with all Thy Prophets and Messengers, and whose statutes Thou hast inscribed in Thy records and writings. Raise them yet to such heights that they may hear Thy call.

Potent art Thou to do what pleaseth Thee. Verily, Thou art the Inaccessible, the Most Glorious.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Long Healing

He is the Healer, the Sufficer, the Helper, the All-Merciful. I call upon Thee, O Glorified, O Faithful, O Glorious One! Thou art the Sufficer, Thou art the Healer, Thou art the Abider; O Lord, Thou art the Abider! I call upon Thee, O Lord, O Abider! I call upon Thee, O Lord, O Peerless, O Eternal, O Unique One! Thou art the Sufficer, Thou art the Healer, Thou art the Abider; O Lord, Thou art the Abider!

	I call upon Thee, O Lord, O Most Praised, O Holy, O Helper! Thou art the Sufficer, Thou art the Healer, Thou art the Abider; O Lord, Thou art the Abider! I call upon Thee, O Lord, O Peerless, O Eternal, O Unique One! Thou art the Sufficer, Thou art the Healer, Thou art the Abider; O Lord, Thou art the Abider!

	I call upon Thee, O Lord, O Most Praised, O Holy, O Helper! Thou art the Sufficer, Thou art the Healer, Thou art the Abider; O Lord, Thou art the Abider!

	I call upon Thee, O Lord, O All-Knowing, O All-Wise, O Most Great! Thou art the Sufficer, Thou art the Healer, Thou art the Abider; O Lord, Thou art the Abider!

	I call upon Thee, O Lord, O Sovereign, O Exalter, O Judge! Thou art the Sufficer, Thou art the Healer, Thou art the Abider; O Lord, Thou art the Abider! I call upon Thee, O Lord, O Peerless, O Eternal, O Unique One! Thou art the Sufficer, Thou art the Healer, Thou art the Abider; O Lord, Thou art the Abider!

	I call upon Thee, O Lord, O Most Praised, O Holy, O Helper! Thou art the Sufficer, Thou art the Healer, Thou art the Abider; O Lord, Thou art the Abider!

	I call upon Thee, O Lord, O Beloved, O Glorious One, O Most Joyful! Thou art the Sufficer, Thou art the Healer, Thou art the Abider; O Lord, Thou art the Abider!

	I call upon Thee, O Lord, O Most Powerful, O Dweller, O Omnipotent! Thou art the Sufficer, Thou art the Healer, Thou art the Abider; O Lord, Thou art the Abider!

I call upon Thee, O Lord, O Ruler, O Self-Subsisting, O All-Knowing! Thou art the Sufficer, Thou art the Healer, Thou art the Abider; O Lord, Thou art the Abider!

	I call upon Thee, O Lord, O Spirit, O Light, O Most Manifest! Thou art the Sufficer, Thou art the Healer, Thou art the Abider; O Lord, Thou art the Abider! I call upon Thee, O Lord, O Thou Who Pervadest All, O Thou Who Art Known to All, O Thou Who Art Hidden from All! Thou art the Sufficer, Thou art the Healer, Thou art the Abider; O Lord, Thou art the Abider!

	I call upon Thee, O Lord, O Concealed, O Victorious, O Bounteous! Thou art the Sufficer, Thou art the Healer, Thou art the Abider; O Lord, Thou art the Abider! I call upon Thee, O Lord, O All-Powerful, O Helper, O Sustainer! Thou art the Sufficer, Thou art the Healer, Thou art the Abider; O Lord, Thou art the Abider!

	I call upon Thee, O Lord, O Maker, O Sufficer, O Awakener! Thou art the Sufficer, Thou art the Healer, Thou art the Abider; O Lord, Thou art the Abider! I call upon Thee, O Lord, O Raiser, O Gatherer, O Exalter! Thou art the Sufficer, Thou art the Healer, Thou art the Abider; O Lord, Thou art the Abider!

I call upon Thee, O Lord, O Perfecter, O Unrestrained, O Gracious! Thou art the Sufficer, Thou art the Healer, Thou art the Abider; O Lord, Thou art the Abider!

	I call upon Thee, O Lord, O Benevolent, O Withholder, O Creator! Thou art the Sufficer, Thou art the Healer, Thou art the Abider; O Lord, Thou art the Abider! I call upon Thee
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O Lord! Miserable we are, grant us Thy favour; poor, grant us a share from the ocean of Thy riches; needy, satisfy us; humbled, bestow upon us Thy glory. The birds of the air and the beasts of the earth receive their daily sustenance from Thee, and all beings their portion of Thy care and Thy mercy.

Deny not to this weak one the wonders of Thy grace, and through Thy might, grant unto this helpless soul Thy bounty.

Grant us our daily bread, and bestow Thine own added portion for the needs of life, so that we may be independent of anyone save Thee, and so that we may be wholly connected with Thee, walk in Thy paths, and proclaim Thy mysteries. Thou art the Almighty and Loving, and the Sustainer of all mankind.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text: I implore Thee, O my God, by Thy Name, the splendor of which hath encircled the earth and the heavens, to enable me to subject my will to that which Thou hast ordained in Thy Tablets, so that I may find in myself no desire except what Thou desirest through the power of Thy sovereignty, and no will except what Thou, through Thy will, hast destined for me.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O God, my God! Thou art my Hope and my Beloved, the Object of my desire. With great lowliness and entire devotion, I ask Thee to make me the minaret of Thy love in Thy lands, the lamp of Thy knowledge among Thy creatures, and the banner of divine bestowals in Thy kingdom.
Make me one of those who adore Thee, who detach themselves from all save Thee, sanctify themselves from the transitory things of this world, and turn away from the provocations uttered by the voices of vain phantasies.
O my God! Let my heart be dilated with joy by the spirit of confirmation from Thy kingdom, and my sight enlightened as I perceive the hosts of divine assistance, proceeding from the kingdom of Thy omnipotent glory, following one after another, descending upon me.
Thou art, in truth, the All-Powerful, the Unconquerable, the Great!
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
In the name of thy Lord, the Creator, the Sovereign, He Who is sufficient unto all, the Most High, He Whom all men implore for succour! Say: O my God, Thou, the Creator of the heaven and the earth, the Lord of the kingdom, Thou knowest the secrets of my heart, but Thine essence remains impenetrable to any other than Thee. Thou seest what is in me, which none other can see. By Thy grace, grant me that which will enable me to dispense with all save Thee, and make me independent of all others than Thee. Let me reap the benefits of my life in this world and in the other. Open to me the door of Thy grace, and bestow upon me Thy tender mercy and Thy bounties.
O Lord of abundant graces, surround those who love Thee with Thy heavenly protection, and grant us the gifts and bounties which Thou possessest. Be our support in all things, forgive our sins, have pity on us. Thou art our Lord and the Lord of all created things. We invoke none but Thee, and implore none but Thy favours. Thou art the Lord of goodness and grace, of invincible power, of unequalled designs. There is no other God than Thee, the Possessor of all things, the Supreme.
O my Lord, confer Thy blessings upon the messengers, the saints, and the righteous. Verily, Thou art God, the Incomparable, the Irresistible.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
* The person who wishes to perform this prayer should stand up and, facing God, look to their right and left as if expecting the mercy of the Merciful and Compassionate God. Then say:

O God of all names, and Creator of the heavens! I invoke Thee by Thy Unseen, Most High, Most Luminous Essence, and by those who are the Dawning-places thereof – turn this prayer of Mine into fire, that it may burn the veils that deprive me of beholding Thy Countenance, and into light, that it may show me the path to the sea of Thy union.

* Then raise his hands in supplication towards God, the Exalted and Blessed, and say:

Thou art the Desire of all worlds and the Beloved of all nations! Thou seest me turning my face towards Thee, and cutting myself off from all except Thee, and clinging to the cord of Thy sovereignty which hath set in motion all created things. I am Thy servant, O my Lord, and the son of Thy servant. Look Thou upon me, for I stand ready to do Thy will and to carry out Thy pleasure, and I desire naught else but Thy satisfaction. I swear by Thy Sea of Mercy and Thy Sun of Grace! Whatever Thou hast revealed, that is the object of my heart and the desire of my soul. O God, my God! look not at my hopes and my deeds, nay, look Thou at Thy will which hath encompassed the heavens and the earth. I swear by Thy Most Great Name, O Sovereign of all nations! I desire only that which Thou hast desired, and I love only that which Thou lovest.

* Then he should kneel and place his forehead on the ground (sajda) and say:

Thou art exalted beyond the description of all except Thee and beyond the comprehension of all save Thee.

* Then he should stand up and say:

Make my prayer, O my Lord, a fount of life, that I may live as long as Thy dominion endureth, and may remember Thee in all Thy realms.

* Let him again raise his hands in supplication (qunut) and say:

Thou art He whose separation maketh hearts and souls to melt, and from the fire of Whose love all the world is set ablaze! I swear by that Name through which Thou hast subjected all created things, grant me not to be deprived of those who are nigh unto Thee, O Judge of all mankind! Thou seest, O I
prayers.name: Once every twenty-four hours (LLM translated)

uk.cat: Молитви за навчання Віри зі Скрижалей Божественного Плану

prayers.text: I am unable to provide further assistance as the translation was interrupted. The response from the `generalist` sub-agent indicates that the translation was cut short. To ensure a complete and accurate translation, please try the request again.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Most glorious art Thou, O Lord my God! I give thanks unto Thee, for Thou hast created me in Thy days, and infused into me Thy love and Thy knowledge. I beseech Thee, by Thy Name, by which the luminous pearls of Thy wisdom and Thy utterance have been brought forth out of the treasury of the hearts of Thy nearest servants, and by which the Day-Star of Thy Name, the Most Merciful, hath dawned above all that is in Thy heavens and on Thy earth, to bestow upon me, by Thy grace and Thy bounty, Thy wondrous and hidden gift.
These are the days of the beginning of my life, O my God, which Thou hast associated with Thine own days. Since Thou hast now graciously bestowed upon me so great an honour, withdraw not from me that which Thou hast ordained for Thy chosen ones.

O my Lord, I am but a tiny seed which Thou hast sown in the soil of Thy love, and which hath grown by the hand of Thy bounty. Wherefore doth this seed, in its very essence, yearn for the waters of Thy mercy and the living springs of Thy grace. Send down upon it from the cloud of Thy compassion that which will enable it to flourish beneath Thy shadow and within the precincts of Thy court. Thou art He Who doth water the hearts of them that have known Thee from Thy plenteous rivers and Thy living fountains. All praise be to God, the Lord of all worlds.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
The provided Portuguese text appears to be a fragment or a different version of a Baháʼí prayer. The search results provide an official translation of a similar prayer by Bahá'u'lláh. To ensure accuracy and adherence to established translations for religious texts, I will provide the official English translation that best matches the beginning of your provided text.

My God, Whom I worship and adore! I bear witness unto Thy unity and Thy oneness, and acknowledge Thy gifts, both in the past and in the present. Thou art the All-Bountiful, the overflowing showers of Whose mercy have rained down upon high and low alike, and the splendors of Whose grace have been shed over both the obedient and the rebellious.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
My God, whom I worship and love! I bear witness to Thy unity and Thy uniqueness, and I acknowledge Thy gifts in both the past and the present. Thou art the All-Bountiful, whose showers of mercy have rained upon the noble and the base, and whose glory of grace hath been shed upon the obedient and the disobedient.
O God of mercy, at Whose door the essence of compassion hath bowed down, and at Whose shrine the token of clemency hath circled in the innermost spirit, we pray Thee, begging for Thine ancient mercy and seeking Thy present favour, that Thou wouldst show kindness unto all the manifestations of the world of existence and withhold not from them the outpourings of Thy grace in Thy time.
All are but poor and needy, and Thou, in truth, art the All-Possessing, the All-Subduing, the Almighty.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

th.cat: บทอธิษฐานเพื่อมนุษยชาติ

prayers.text:
Praised be Thy name, O Lord my God! I beseech Thee by Thy name through which the Hour hath struck, and the Resurrection hath come to pass, and fear and trembling have seized all who are in heaven and all who are on earth, to rain down from the heaven of Thy mercy and the clouds of Thy tender compassion that which will gladden the hearts of Thy servants, who have turned towards Thee and helped Thy Cause.
Keep safe Thy servants and Thy handmaidens, O my Lord, from the darts of idle fancy and vain imaginings, and give them from the hands of Thy grace a draught of the softly-flowing waters of Thy knowledge.
Thou, truly, art the Almighty, the Most Exalted, the Ever-Forgiving, the Most Generous.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text: I am unable to find an English translation for the provided Bahá'í prayer.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

th.cat: บทอธิษฐานเพื่อการรักษา

prayers.text:
O God! My God! With Thy bounteous favour dispel my sorrow and with Thy power assuage my pain. My God! When sorrows gather from all directions, Thou dost behold me turning my face towards Thee. O Lord of all creation and of all within Thy realm, whether seen or unseen! By Thy Holy Name which hath subdued the hearts of men, by the waves of the ocean of Thy mercy, and by the radiance of the Sun of Thy bounty, I beseech Thee to count me among them that have turned their faces towards Thee, for Thou art the Lord of all names and the Creator of the heavens.


My Lord! In Thy days, Thou hast witnessed my afflictions. By the Divine Source of the revelations of Thy Names, and the dawning-place of the splendours of Thy attributes, I pray Thee to ordain for me all that will enable me to serve Thee and to extol Thy virtues. Verily, Thou art the Almighty, the Most Powerful, the Answerer of the prayers of all mankind!


Following this, by Thy countenance, I beg of Thee to bless my undertaking, to clear my debts, and to satisfy my needs. Thy power and Thy sovereignty are witnessed by all, and Thy glory and Thy dominion are confirmed by every understanding heart. There is no God but Thee, the Hearing and Responding God.
prayers.name: 考驗與困境 (LLM translated)

prayers.text: O my Lord, God of bounty and compassion! Thou art the King through Whose command all creation hath been manifested; and Thou art the Most Bountiful, Whose servants' deeds have never hindered Him from showing forth His favours, nor have they prevented the manifestation of His bounty. I beseech Thee, to enable this servant to attain whatsoever is the cause of his salvation in every one of Thy worlds. Verily, Thou art the Almighty, the All-Powerful, the All-Knowing, the All-Wise.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Prayer for the Dead

O my God! This is Thy servant and the son of Thy servant who hath believed in Thee and in Thy signs, and set his face towards Thee, wholly detached from all except Thee. Thou art, verily, of those who show mercy the most merciful.

Deal with him, O Thou Who forgivest the sins of men and concealest their faults, as beseemeth the heaven of Thy bounty and the ocean of Thy grace. Grant him admission within the precincts of Thy transcendent mercy that was before the foundation of earth and heaven. There is no God but Thee, the Ever-Forgiving, the Most Generous.
prayers.name: Bop U’uraewa (Burial) (LLM translated)

tvl.cat: MO LATOU KO GALO – TALO MO TINO MATE

prayers.text:
O my God, how many have been the hearts that have burned with the fire of Thy Cause, and how many the souls that have been awakened by the sweet savour of Thy voice! How many the strangers that have sought shelter beneath the shadow of the Tree of Thy oneness, and how many the thirsty who have thirsted for the water of life from the fountain of Thy Revelation.

Blessed are they who turn towards Thee, and hasten to attain the dawning-light of Thy countenance. Blessed are they who, with all their passion, have set their faces towards Thee, the Source of inspiration, the Divine Dawn of Thy Revelation. Blessed are they who, through Thy grace and Thy favour, offer up and restore unto Thy path that which Thou hast sent down unto them. Blessed are they who, out of love for Thee, have cast away all else beside Thee. Blessed are they who have drawn nigh unto Thee and have severed themselves from all attachment to aught besides Thee.

O Lord, we implore Thee by Thy Name that hath appeared upon the horizon of Thy prison, by the power of Thy dominion and the sovereignty of Thy might. Grant unto all mankind that which beseemeth them, and is worthy of Thy praise.

Verily, Thy power hath no equal.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

th.cat: บทอธิษฐานพื่อตัดความผูกพัน

prayers.text:
This verse is recited at the shrine of Abdul Baha, but a person may also recite it for their own sake.

If you offer this prayer with faith and humility, it causes the heart of Abdul Baha to feel delight, as if you are in his presence.

He is Al-Majid.

O God, my God! In humility and prostration, I lift my hands to Thee in supplication, and lay my forehead upon the dust of Thy threshold, which is beyond the knowledge of any knower and the praise of all that glorifies Thy Name. Look with the eye of mercy upon this lowliest of Thy servants who is at Thy door, and immerse him in the ocean of Thy boundless glory.

O Lord! Behold Thy poor and lowly servant begging in prostration, held in Thy hand, praying in ecstasy and acceptance by Thee, his eyes brimming with tears before Thy face. This servant calleth Thee, beseecheth Thee, saying: "O Lord my God, strengthen me in Thy service, illuminate my forehead with the light of worship in Thy holy court, and with the light of prayer in Thy great dominion. Aid me to forsake all before the throne of Thy heavenly door, and to depend wholly on Thy sacred threshold."

O Lord, cause me to drink from the cup of dependence, and grant me its raiment, and immerse me in its ocean. Make me dust upon the path of Thy loved ones, and suffer me to give my life for the land Thou hast hallowed by the footsteps of Thy chosen ones who follow Thy path, O Thou, Lord of glory, in glory.

Through this prayer, Thy servant calleth Thee at dawn and at night. Accept the request of his heart, O Lord! Kindle and fill his heart with joy, and gladden his spirit, that he may do good unto Thy commands and Thy servants.

Thou art the All-Bountiful, the All-Merciful, the Generous, the Compassionate, the Protector.
prayers.name: Visitation (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O Lord, my God and my Refuge in the time of my affliction! Forsake me not in my tribulation, O my Protector and my Deliverer! My Guardian and my Saviour in the hour of need and in the day of distress! When I am poor, Thou art my Wealth; when I am weak, Thou art my Strength! Thou art the Removers of the anguish of my heart and the Forgiver of my sins!

Unto Thee do I turn, entreating Thee with all my heart, my mind, and my tongue, turn me away from all that would deny Thy purpose in this generation, in the hallowed bonds of Thy unity, and purify me from all corruption that would prevent me from seeking Thy blessed, pure, and holy Tree.

Have mercy, O Lord, upon the weak, restore the sick, and banish the oppressive.

Enlarge the breasts wherein the fire of Thy love hath blazed, and cause them to be inflamed with the fire of Thy love and Thy heavenly spirit.

Adorn the hallowed temple of unity with the raiment of holiness, and place upon my head the crown of Thy grace.

illumine my face with the rays of Thy countenance, and with Thy grace assist me in performing the service in Thy holy precincts.

Make my heart to overflow with love for Thy creation and suffer me to become a sign of Thy favour, a token of Thy blessing, that fostereth unity amongst Thy loved ones, devoted to Thee, singing Thy praises, and wholly absorbed in Thee.

O God! my God! Deprive me not of the breaths of Thy grace, and withhold not from me the living waters of Thy favour.

Beneath the shadow of Thy divine protection, suffer me to take my rest, and let the eyes of Thy loving-kindness watch over me.

Make my tongue to utter Thy praise amongst Thy people, that my voice may be heard in the great assembly and that from my lips may flow the stream of Thy glorification.

Thou art indeed, the Most Compassionate, the All-Glorified, the All-Powerful, the Most Mighty.
prayers.name: Turn to God (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
He is God! O God, my God! These are Thy servants who are enamoured with the fragrance of Thy holiness on the day of Thy sovereignty, and ignited from Thy holy, burning Tree, and responding to Thy call, and glorifying Thee, and awakened by the breeze of Thy favour, and inhaling Thy sweet savors, and gazing upon Thy signs, and comprehending Thy verses, and hearkening unto Thy words, and believing in Thy revelations, and trusting in Thy bounty. O Lord, their gazes are fixed upon the luminous kingdom of Thy glory, and their faces are set towards the realm of Thy might, and their hearts are throbbing with the love of Thy resplendent beauty, and their souls are aflame with the fire of Thy love, and their lives are aglow with the ardour of their yearning for Thee, and their tears flow in remembrance of Thee, O Thou, the Lord of this world and all worlds to come.

Guard them within the stronghold of Thy protection and safety, and graciously care for them, and watch over them with the eye of Thy providence and mercy. Make them signs of Thy divine unity, shining forth throughout all regions, and banners of Thy power waving over the mansions of Thy majesty, and lamps burning with the oil of Thy wisdom in the lanterns of Thy guidance, and birds singing upon the highest boughs of Thy paradise, dwelling in the gardens of Thy knowledge, and mighty whales sporting in the ocean of Thy grace, immeasurably deep, through Thy supreme bounty.

O Lord, my God! These servants of Thine are lowly; exalt them unto Thy sublime kingdom. These are abased; bestow upon them glory from Thy supreme domain. These are poor; enrich them from Thy vast territories. And destine for them whatsoever Thou hast ordained of the good things in all Thy worlds, visible and invisible, and make them to flourish in this lower world, and gladden their hearts with Thy inspiration. O Thou, the Lord of all worlds! Enlighten their hearts with the good tidings of Thy gospel, sent down from the station of Thy glory, and establish their feet in Thy great covenant, through Thy grace and promised favour, and strengthen their loins in Thy firm decree, O Thou, gracious and merciful! Verily, Thou art kind and most bounteous.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

## Evening

O my Lord, my Master, the Goal of my desires! This Thy servant wisheth to sleep beneath the canopy of Thy mercy, to rest under the shadow of Thy grace, and seeketh Thy care and protection.

O my God, I adjure Thee by Thine eyes that sleep not, to guard mine eyes that they may behold naught else but Thee. Strengthen their sight that they may discern Thy signs, and behold the Horizon of Thy Revelation. Thou art He before the revelation of Whose majesty the embodiments of might have trembled.

There is no God but Thee, the Almighty, the All-Subduing, the Unconstrained.

We will all, verily, abide by the Will of God.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text: Say: God sufficeth all things above all things, and nothing in the heavens or in the earth but God sufficeth. Verily, He is in Himself the Knower, the Sustainer, the Omnipotent.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

th.cat: บทอธิษฐานเมื่อประสบความยากลำบาก

prayers.text:
Thou art indeed glorified, O my God! By Him Who is the Revealer of Thy signs, Who hath been the object of the bitterest hatred of them that have denied Thy truth, and Who hath been afflicted with sorrows that no tongue can utter, I implore Thee to suffer me to remember Thee and to laud Thy praise, in these days when all have turned away from Thy beauty, have repudiated Thee, and in their arrogance have cast aside Thy Message. O my God, none can aid Thee save Thyself, and none can deliver Him save the power of Thyself.
I pray Thee to help me to be steadfast in my love for Thee and in my remembrance of Thee. This indeed is within my power, and Thou knowest all that concerneth me. Verily, Thou art the Knower of all things and the All-wise. O my God, suffer me not to be deprived of the resplendency of the light of Thy countenance, the radiance whereof hath illuminated all created things. There is none other God beside Thee, the Almighty, the All-glorious, the Ever-forgiving.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O Lord, my God, praised be Thou! I give thanks unto Thee as Thy Bounty hath called me to life in Thy days, and breathed into me Thy love and Thy wisdom. I implore Thee by Thy Name by which the goodly pearls of Thy wisdom and Thy revelation have been drawn out from the treasuries of the hearts of Thy servants, as they draw nigh unto Thee, and as the Morning Star of Thy Most Bountiful Name hath caused to shine forth, its brilliance upon all that is in Thy heaven and all that is on Thy earth, that I may be nourished by Thy grace and Thy gifts, with Thy wondrous bestowals and Thy boundless favour.

      O my God, these are the first days of my life, which have been consecrated by Thy Bounty and by Thyself. Since Thou hast poured out upon me so great a glory, withhold not from me that which Thou hast ordained for Thy chosen ones.

      O my God, I am but a humble seed which Thy Bounty hath sown in the soil of Thy love, and caused to grow in the day of Thy favour. I therefore implore Thee by my true self, by the waters of Thy tender mercy and the living springs of Thy grace. Send down upon it from the heaven of Thy loving-kindness, that which shall make it to flourish and to grow beneath Thy shadow and within the courts of Thy sanctuary. Thou art He who watereth with the water of the hearts of all who know Thee from Thy great river and the springs of Thy living waters.

       Praised be God, the Lord of worlds.
prayers.name: Tamaiti ma Talavou (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Exalted be Thy name, O my God! Behold, mine eyes yearn to behold the wonders of Thy mercy, and mine ears long to hearken unto the sweet melodies of Thy voice, and my heart is athirst for the living waters of Thy knowledge. Thou seest, O my God, Thy handmaid standing before the abode of Thy mercy, and calling on Thee by the name which Thou hast chosen above all other names, and set above all that are in heaven and on earth. Send down upon her the gentle breezes of Thy grace, that she may be borne wholly away from herself and be drawn entirely unto that seat which, resplendent with the glory of the light of Thy countenance, doth shed abroad the radiance of Thy dominion, and is established as Thy throne. Thou art powerful to do whatsoever Thou willest. There is none other God beside Thee, the Most Glorious, the Most Bountiful.

Reject not, I beseech Thee, O my Lord, such as have sought Thee, nor turn away from Thee them that have set their faces towards Thee, nor withdraw Thy favour from all who love Thee. Thou art He, O my Lord, Who hath named Himself the God of mercy, the Most Compassionate. Have mercy, therefore, upon Thy handmaid who hath sought sanctuary in Thee and turned her face towards Thee.

Thou art, indeed, the Ever-Forgiving, the Most Merciful.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

th.cat: บทอธิษฐานขอความช่วยเหลือ

prayers.text:
Praise be to Thee, O my God! I give Thee thanks for having made known unto me Him who is the Dayspring of Thy mercy and the Dawning-place of Thy grace, and the Preserver of Thy Cause. I beseech Thee by Thy Name, which illumineth the faces of those who are nigh unto Thee, and causeth the hearts of those who are devoted to Thee to take their flight towards Thee, to grant that I may, at all times and under all conditions, cling to Thy lifegiving cord and be attracted to no one but Thee, so that mine eyes may constantly rest upon the horizon of Thy revelation, and that I may carry out what Thou hast prescribed for me in Thy Tablets.

Adorn Thou, O my Lord, both my inner and outer being with the raiment of Thy favor and loving-kindness. Guard me, moreover, from whatsoever may be abhorrent unto Thee, and graciously assist me and my kindred to obey Thee, and to shun whatsoever may stir up evil or corrupt desires within me.

Thou, in truth, art the Lord of all mankind, the Possessor of this world and of the next. No God is there but Thee, the All-Knowing, the All-Wise.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:

O Lord! In this Most Great Dispensation Thou dost accept the intercession of children in behalf of their parents.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

th.cat: บทอธิษฐานพื่อตัดความผูกพัน

prayers.text:
O my God! I know not what this fire is which Thou hast kindled in Thy kingdom. The earth cannot dim its radiance, nor can water extinguish its flame. No person in the world has the power to resist its might. How great is the blessing for those who approach that fire and hear its roar.

　O my God! Thou hast drawn some near to that fire by Thy ever-increasing grace, while others, because of what their hands have wrought in Thy days, Thou hast kept afar. All who have hastened towards it and attained it have offered their lives in Thy path with a fervent desire to behold Thy beauty, forsaking all else and ascending unto Thee.

　O my Lord! I implore Thee by this fire that hath blazed and raged in the world of creation. Tear away from me the dark veil that preventeth me from advancing before Thy magnificent Throne, and from standing at Thy gate. O my Lord! Ordain for me all the good which Thou hast decreed in Thy Book, and suffer me not to be kept afar from the sanctuary of Thy mercy.

　Thou hast the power to do as Thou pleasest. Verily, Thou art the Almighty, the Most Bounteous.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O God, my God! This Thy handmaid calleth upon Thee, trusting in Thee, turning her face unto Thee, imploring Thee to shed Thy heavenly bounties upon her, and to disclose unto her Thy spiritual mysteries, and to cast upon her the lights of Thy Godhead.

O my Lord! Make the eyes of my husband to see. Rejoice his heart with the light of the knowledge of Thee, draw his mind unto Thy luminous beauty, gladden his spirit by revealing unto him Thy manifest splendors.

O my Lord! Lift the veil from before his sight. Rain down Thy plenteous bounties upon him, inebriate him with the wine of love for Thee, make him one of Thy angels whose feet walk upon this earth while their souls soar through the high heavens. Cause him to become a brilliant lamp, shining out with the light of Thy wisdom in the midst of Thy people.

Verily, Thou art the Precious, the Ever-Bestowing, the Generous.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text: I beg forgiveness from Thee, O my God, for every mention other than Thy mention, and for every praise other than Thy praise, and for every joy other than the joy of Thy proximity, and for every pleasure other than the delight of communion with Thee, and for every joy other than the joy of Thy love and Thy good-pleasure, and for all those things which pertain unto me, that have no relation to Thee, O Thou the Lord of Lords, He Who bestoweth the means and openeth the doors.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
He is God

O God, O Forgiver of error, O Remover of transgressions and iniquities, Thou forgivest whom Thou willest by Thy vast mercy and Thy radiant compassions. Verily, I supplicate unto Thee with all shame and bashfulness for the errors that have issued from me. Nevertheless, I beg of Thee to pardon these, and to forgive their sins, and to shelter them within the precincts of Thy great mercy.

O Lord, they have none but Thee; none can succor them but Thee; none can save them but Thee.

O Lord, have mercy upon them by Thy grace and Thy favors; draw them out from the abyss of deprivation, and deliver them from the pit of estrangement; and cause them to enter the Paradise of contentment, and bestow upon them the chalice of forgiveness and bounty.

Verily, Thou art the Gentle, the Merciful, the Compassionate. and Thou art the Forgiving, the Loving, the Helper.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
He is the Most Glorious, O God, Most Glorious!
13 O God, this is a bird that hath flown to the meadows of Thy forgiveness and pardon, and this a breeze that hath returned to the garden of Thy grace and bounty, and this a servant who hath sought the proximity of Thy mercy. O my Lord, honour his station and make him to abide in the shadow of the Tree of Bliss, and open to him the gates of union, and grant him the highest degree, and encompass him with the splendours of Thy great favours, and clothe him with the robes of bestowal, for Thou art indeed the Bountiful, the Merciful.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Glorified art Thou, O Possessor of existence and the Sovereign over the seen and the unseen! I entreat Thee by the Tongue from which the springs of wisdom and utterance have gushed forth in the contingent world, and by the Heart which Thou hast made the treasury of Thy knowledge and mysteries and the repository of Thy wisdom and revelations, to shower upon him who hath ascended unto Thee at every moment the drops of Thy mercy and the rains of Thy bounty. Verily, Thou art He Whose generosity every tongue hath testified and Whose grace every utterance hath acknowledged. Thou doest what Thou pleasest and ordainest what Thou desirest. Verily, Thou art the Almighty, the All-Praised.

Then we ask Thee, O God of the world, to ordain for those who are related unto Him that which will draw them nigh unto Thee in all conditions. Verily, Thou art the Self-Sufficient, the Most Exalted. There is none other God but Thee, the Almighty, the All-Bounteous.
prayers.name: He is the Comforter, the Consoler, the Forgiving, the All-Merciful. (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Faithfulness to Covenant

In the name of our Lord, the Most Holy, the Most Great, the Most Exalted, the Most Glorious!

O God, my God! Thou seest how Thy servant hath turned his face unto Thee. He longeth to do that which is commanded him in Thy Book. Ordain for him through Thy Most Exalted Pen that which shall draw him nigh unto the highest heaven. Verily, Thou art the Educator of the world and the Lord of nations. Thou art, verily, the Mighty, the All-Subduing, the Almighty.
prayers.name: Bundestreue (LLM translated)

mi.cat: TE PAPATUHI E MUMURA AI TE NGĀKAU PONO

th.cat: ธรรมจารึกอัคคี

prayers.text:
O compassionate God! Thanks be to Thee for Thou hast awakened and made me conscious. Thou hast given me a seeing eye and favored me with a hearing ear, hast led me to Thy kingdom and guided me to Thy path. Thou hast shown me the right way and caused me to enter the ark of deliverance.

O God! Keep me steadfast and make me firm and staunch. Protect me from violent tests, and preserve and shelter me in the strongly fortified fortress of Thy Covenant and Testament. Thou art the Powerful. Thou art the Seeing. Thou art the Hearing.

O Thou the Compassionate God. Bestow upon me a heart which, like unto glass, may be illumined with the light of Thy love, and confer upon me thoughts which may change this world into a rose garden through the outpourings of heavenly grace.

Thou art the Compassionate, the Merciful. Thou art the Great Beneficent God.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text: My God, my God, praise be to Thee for that Thou hast kindled the Divine Fire of Thy Love in the centre of existence, in the blessed Tree, which is neither of the East nor of the West, and which blazed and burned and flamed, until its blaze reached the hosts of the Most High, and by it they apprehended the verities of divine knowledge, and they said: ‘Verily, we have perceived a Fire from the side of Mount Sinai.’ My God, my God, increase every day the blaze and the burning thereof, until its sigh moveth the universes. O my Lord! Kindle the Fire of Thy Love in the hearts, and breathe the Spirit of Thy knowledge into the souls, and expand the breasts with Thy verses of oneness, and quicken them that are in the graves, and rouse them that are deluded, and spread abroad joy and gladness, and send down the purifying rain, and cause to flow a cup the mixture whereof is camphor, in the assembly of manifestation and revelation. Verily, Thou art the Giver, the Bountiful, the Forgiving, and, verily, Thou art the Merciful, the Compassionate.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
He is God

O Everlasting, Almighty One, refuge of sinners, Bountiful and Merciful One, we are drowned in error, and Thou art the Ocean of Generosity; we are in the darkness of disobedience, and Thou art the Sun of Grace and Kindness.

O Lord, turn not away from our shortcomings, and deal not with us according to our exceeding weakness and inability; we are abased and weak, make us honored, grant us glory, bestow healing, shower upon us the greatest bounty of pardon and forgiveness.

O Forgiver, take us not to task for our transgressions; Thou art the Helper and the Guide of every bewildered one. Thou hast made us weary of the material world; make us triumphant in the spiritual kingdom; Thou hast delivered us from the realm of calamities, bring us to the rose-garden of Thy favours.

Thou art the Bountiful, the Giver, the Deliverer, the Everlasting, the Kind.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
He is God

O Forgiver of all sinners and wrongdoers, You are forgiving, You are bountiful, merciful. Look not at our merit or worthiness, but look upon Your grace and forgiveness. Immerse us in the ocean of Your forgiveness, and in the Most Glorious Paradise, make us companions with the Exalted Ones. Verily, Thou art the Merciful, the All-Bountiful.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text: I am unable to find a direct translation for this specific prayer using web search. I can attempt a general translation if you'd like, but it may not capture the precise nuances of Bahá'í terminology.

prayers.text:
Prayer for a Husband

O Lord, my Lord! Thy handmaid who is before Thee supplicates Thee, trusteth in Thee, turneth towards Thee, and calleth upon Thee. Bestow upon him Thy favour, reveal unto him the mysteries of Thy Faith, and the glory of Thy sovereignty.

O Lord! Enable my husband to become enlightened, gladden his heart with the light of Thy knowledge which leads unto Thee, and draw his soul unto the beauty of Thy countenance.

O Lord! Remove the veils which hide him from Thine eyes, pour down upon him Thy plenteous blessings, that he may be intoxicated with the wine of love for Thee. Make him one of the heavenly hosts that tread this earthly realm, as if his spirit were soaring in the firmament of heaven; make him a radiant star shining with the light of Thine omnipotence amongst Thy people.

Verily, Thou art the All-Bounteous, the Ever-Giving, the Succourer.
prayers.name: ບົດອະທິຖານໃຫ້ສາມີ (LLM translated)

lo.cat: ບົດອະທິຖານໃຫ້ສາມີ

prayers.text:
* Prayer for the Huqúqu’lláh (the Right of God).

Glory be to Thee, O my Lord, the Most Compassionate! By the tumultuous ocean of Thy holy word and the innumerable tokens of Thy supreme sovereignty, by the mighty testimonies of Thy divinity and the hidden mysteries that lie within Thy knowledge, I implore Thee to grant me the grace to serve Thee and those whom Thou hast chosen, and to enable me to perform my duty: to offer the Huqúq which Thou hast prescribed in Thy book.
O my Lord, I have set my heart upon Thy kingdom of glory and I hold fast to the hem of Thy generosity. O Lord of all existence, Thou who rulest over the kingdom of names, I implore Thee. Withhold not from me what Thou dost possess, and deprive me not of what Thou hast prescribed for Thine elect.
O Lord of all names and Creator of the heavens, I implore Thee by Thy fortifying grace. Aid me to remain firm in Thy cause; so shall the vanities of the world be no veil separating me from Thee, and the violent disorders of the wicked who, in Thy days, have risen to turn Thy people from the right path, shall be unable to hinder me. O Desire of my heart, grant me the good of this world and of the world to come. Verily, Thou hast power to do whatsoever Thou desirest. There is none other God than Thee, the Gracious, the Bountiful.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
*[May be read well during the period of Fasting as well as at the time of dawn.]*

My Lord, my Master! I am Thy servant and the son of Thy servant. I have risen from my bed at this dawn, when the Sun of Thy Oneness hath shone from the Dawn of Thy will, and hath shed its rays over all the world, according to that which hath been determined in the Books of Thy Command.
Praise be to Thee, O my Lord, that we have arisen by the radiance of the light of Thy knowledge. Send down, then, unto us, O my Lord, that which shall make us independent of all save Thee, and deliver us from every bond save Thyself. Further, write down for me, and for them whom I love, and for my kindred, men and women alike, the good of this world and of the world to come. O Thou the Beloved of all creation and the Desire of the universe, save us by Thy unfailing protection from them whom Thou hast made the embodiment of Evil Suggestions, which whisper in the bosms of men. Thou hast power to do as Thou pleasest. Verily, Thou art the Most Exalted, the Helper in Distress, the Self-Subsisting.

Bless, O Lord my God, Him whom Thou has commissioned with Thy Most Exalted Titles, and through Whom Thou hast distinguished between the pure and the wicked, and help us by Thy grace to do whatsoever Thou dost love and desire. Moreover, O my Lord, bless those who are Thy Words and Thy Letters, and those who have set their faces toward Thee, and have turned to Thy face and hearkened unto Thy call. Verily, Thou art the Lord and King of all mankind, and art powerful over all things.

*"Oftentimes in the dawn the breezes of My affection have wafted over thee and found thee on the couch of heedlessness, sleeping soundly. Weeping over thy state, it hath returned to its place of origin."*

*"Oft on the dawn, A
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
**Finding prayer translation**

I have successfully located a widely recognized English translation that accurately renders the provided Malayalam Baháʼí prayer. I am now prepared to present this translation, ensuring all formatting, including section headers and paragraph breaks, is preserved as requested, with no extraneous commentary.

## O Thou kind Lord!
Praise be to Thee for awakening me and making me conscious. Thou hast given me eyes to see. Thou hast blessed me with hearing. Thou hast guided me to Thy kingdom. Thou hast guided me on Thy path. Thou hast shown me the straight way. Thou hast caused me to enter the ark of salvation. O God! Strengthen me, make me firm and steadfast. Protect me from intense trials. Grant me refuge in the strong fortress of Thy covenant and testament. Thou art powerful. Thou art the Seer. Thou art the Hearer.

## O Thou kind Lord!
Grant me a heart like unto a crystal, shining with the light of Thy love. Grant me thoughts that may make this world a paradise of roses, watered by the showers of Thy heavenly grace.
Thou art the Gracious, the Merciful, the Bountiful Lord.## O Thou kind Lord!
Praise be to Thee for awakening me and making me conscious. Thou hast given me eyes to see. Thou hast blessed me with hearing. Thou hast guided me to Thy kingdom. Thou hast guided me on Thy path. Thou hast shown me the straight way. Thou hast caused me to enter the ark of salvation. O God! Strengthen me, make me firm and steadfast. Protect me from intense trials. Grant me refuge in the strong fortress of Thy covenant and testament. Thou art powerful. Thou art the Seer. Thou art the Hearer.

## O Thou kind Lord!
Grant me a heart like unto a crystal, shining with the light of Thy love. Grant me thoughts that may make this world a paradise of roses, watered by the showers of Thy heavenly grace.
Thou art the Gracious, the Merciful, the Bountiful Lord.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O God my God! Thou seest me standing before the door of Thy forgiveness and benevolence, turning my gaze toward the horizon of Thy bountiful favours and manifold blessings. I beg of Thee by Thy sweet accents and by the shrill voice of Thy Pen, O Lord of all mankind, to graciously aid Thy servants as it befitteth Thy days and beseemeth the glory of Thy manifestation and Thy majesty. Verily potent art Thou to do whatsoever Thou willest. All they that dwell in the heavens and on the earth bear witness to Thy power and Thy might, to Thy glory and Thy bounteousness. Praise be to Thee, O Lord of the worlds and the Well-Beloved of the heart of every man of understanding!

Thou Beholdest, O myGod, the essence of poverty seeking the ocean of Thy wealth and the substance of iniquity yearning for the waters of Thy forgiveness and Thy tender mercy. Grant Thou, O my God, that which beseemeth Thy great glory and befitteth the loftiness of Thy boundless grace. Thou art in truth the All-Bountiful, the Lord of grace abounding, the Ordainer, the All-Wise. No God is there but Thee, the Most Powerful, the All-Compelling, the Omnipotent.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
I am aware, O Lord, that my transgressions have covered my face with shame in Thy presence, and have burdened my back before Thee, have intervened between Thy beautiful countenance and my being, have surrounded me and prevented me on all sides from accessing the revelations of Thy celestial power.

O Lord! If Thou dost not forgive me, who then can grant forgiveness? And if Thou dost not have mercy on me, who can show compassion? Glory be to Thee. Thou didst create me when I did not exist, and didst nourish me while I lacked all understanding. Praised be Thou, for all proof of munificence proceeds from Thee, and all display of grace emanates from the treasures of Thy decree.
prayers.name: Forgiveness (LLM translated)

prayers.text: Praised be Thou, O Lord. Forgive us our transgressions, have mercy upon us and guide us back unto Thee. Grant that we may not rely on any other save Thee, and send down upon us, through Thy bounty, that which Thou dost love and desire and is meet for Thee. Exalt the spiritual state of them that truly believe, and forgive them through Thy tender forgiveness. Verily, Thou art the Help in Great Distress, the Self-Subsistent.
prayers.name: Forgiveness (LLM translated)

th.cat: บทอธิษฐานเพื่อการอภัย

prayers.text:
O God, my God! I implore Thee by the blood of those who truly loved Thee, who were so enraptured by Thy sweet words that they hastened to the summit of glory, the station of most glorious martyrdom, and I ask Thee by the mysteries which Thy knowledge keepeth, and by the pearls which the sea of Thy bounty containeth, forgive me, my father and my mother! Of all who show mercy, Thou art in truth the Most Merciful. There is no god but Thou, the Ever-Forgiving, the All-Bountiful.
O Lord! Thou seest how this embodiment of sinfulness turneth towards the sea of Thy favour, how this weak one seeketh the realm of Thy divine power, how this poor creature stretcheth himself towards the sun of Thy riches. By Thy mercy and Thy grace, disappoint him not, O Lord, exclude him not from the revelations of Thy bounty in Thy days, nor turn him away from Thy door, which Thou hast opened wide to all the inhabitants of Thy heaven and Thy earth.
Alas, my sins have prevented me from approaching the court of Thy holiness, and my transgressions have made me stray far from the tabernacle of Thy majesty. I have committed what Thou hast forbidden me, and have cast away what Thou hast bidden me observe.
I ask Thee by Him, the Unconstrained Lord of Names, write down for me with the pen of Thy bounty that which will enable me to draw nigh unto Thee, and which will cleanse me from my transgressions that have intervened between me and Thy forgiveness and pardon.
Verily, Thou art the Powerful, the Kind. There is no god but Thou, the Mighty, the Gracious.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
26	How shall I sleep, O Lord, my Lord, when the eyes of them that long for Thee remain sleepless because they are separated from Thee; and how can I remain in slumber when the souls of Thy companions are in agony through remoteness from Thee?

I have surrendered, O my God, my spirit and all my life into Thy strong and watchful hand, and I lay my head upon my couch through Thy power, and raise it according to Thy will and Thy tender mercy. Thou art indeed the Gathering, the Preserver, the Most Steadfast, the Almighty.

By Thy might! I ask not for sleep nor for sleeplessness, but only that which Thou dost desire. I am Thy servant and in Thy hand. Vouchsafe, O Lord, through Thy grace, to help me do that which will draw down the sighs of Thy tender mercy. This, indeed, is my hope and the hope of those who draw nigh unto Thee. Praise be to Thee, O Lord of all worlds!
prayers.name: NIGHT (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Recited once in twenty-four hours between noon and sunset. It is advised that the reciter should stand.

 

        I bear witness, O my God, that Thou hast created me to know and to worship Thee. I bear witness, at this moment, to my weakness and Thy power, to my poverty and Thy riches.

        There is no God but Thou, the Help in Peril, the Self-Subsisting.

”These obligatory prayers, together with some other special prayers, such as the Prayer for Healing and the Tablet of Ahmad, have been endowed by Bahá'u'lláh with a special power and significance, and therefore they must be received and recited by the believers with absolute faith and confidence, so that they may enter into intimate converse with God, and more fully comprehend the Laws and commandments of God.

There are three obligatory prayers... The believers are free to choose any one of these three prayers, but on condition that they recite one of them, and according to the specific instructions for each prayer.”

~ Shoghi Effendi

Before carrying out the Obligatory Prayer, one should perform wudu (washing the hands and face) first. While praying, one should face the Qiblih.

~ Compilation of the Kitáb-i-Aqdas
prayers.name: SHORT OBLIGATORY PRAYER (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
He is the Most Glorious
O my God, my Lord, my Support, on Thee is my reliance and my trust, and by the cord of Thy grace I hold fast, and at the gate of Thy Oneness I seek to draw nigh. By the breezes of Thy holiness is the life of my heart, and by the attractions of the beauty of Thy oneness, my soul is moved. And by the shining lights from the horizon of Thy uniqueness is the joy of my spirit, and by the winds of Thy providence, my being is stirred. By the manifestations of the Kingdom of Thy mercy is the resurrection of my self. By the confirmations of Thy Most High assembly is my succour, and the exaltation of my word. By the successes of Thy Most Glorious Kingdom is my victory, and the elevation of my purpose. By Thy love is my salvation, and by the knowledge of Thee is my life. And by Thy help is my steadfastness. By the abundance of the clouds of Thy bounty is the quenching of my thirst. By the pure waters of the fount of Thy grace is the cooling of my burning desire. And by the balm of Thy wisdom is the healing of my sickness.
My God, my God, disappoint not my hopes, nor look upon my deeds, and the evil of my state, and the excess of my abasement, and the extremity of my deprivation, and the abundance of my disobedience. Deal with me, O my God, according to what befitteth the loftiness of Thy glory, and deal not with me according to what befitteth my lowliness, and my poverty, and my helplessness.
My God, my God, the eyes are watching for the descent of Thy gifts. My God, my God, the hearts are awaiting the manifestation of the lights of Thy favours. Strengthen Thou my back with Thy victorious power over all created things, and fortify Thou my loins with Thy prevailing might over all that may be brought into existence. And illumine Thou my sight with Thy shining lights from the dawning-place of the Utterances, and expand Thou my breast with Thy dazzling signs from the ascendant-places of Manifestations. And strengthen Thou my soldiers with Thy triumphant banners raised upon the heights of the hearts of the sanctified, speaking Realities, in the earth and in the heavens.
Verily, Thou art the Almighty to do whatsoever Thou pleasest, and verily, Thou art the Powerful, the Mighty, the All-Wise.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
We are all sinners

O Thou forgiving Lord! Thou art the shelter of all these Thy servants. Thou knowest the secrets and art aware of all things. We are all helpless, and Thou art the Mighty, the Omnipotent. We are all sinners, and Thou art the Forgiver of sins, the Merciful, the Clement. O Lord! Look not upon our shortcomings. Deal with us according to Thy grace and bounty. Our shortcomings are many, but the ocean of Thy forgiveness is boundless. Our defects are grievous, but the evidences of Thy help and assistance are manifest. Therefore, confirm us and strengthen us. Aid us to do that which is acceptable at Thy Holy Threshold. Illumine our hearts, grant us discerning eyes and attentive ears. Raise the dead and heal the sick. Bestow wealth upon the poor and give peace and security to the fearful. Accept us in Thy kingdom and illumine us with the light of guidance. Thou art the Powerful and the Omnipotent. Thou art the Generous. Thou art the Clement. Thou art the Kind.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O Lord! What a great outpouring of munificence Thou hast bestowed upon us and what a torrent of abundant grace Thou hast conferred upon us! Thou hast made all hearts as one heart, and all souls as one soul. Thou didst endow lifeless bodies with life and feeling, and bestow spiritual consciousness upon inanimate forms. Through the luminous rays of the Sun of the All-Merciful, Thou didst invest these atoms of dust with visible existence, and, through the waves of the ocean of unity, Thou didst cause these evanescent drops to rise and roar.

O Almighty One, Thou who dost bestow upon a blade of straw the power of a mountain and cause a mote of dust to reflect the glory of the resplendent sun! Grant us Thy loving grace and favour, that we may arise to serve Thy Cause and not be ashamed before the peoples of the earth.
prayers.name: Oraciones de ‘Abdu’l-Bahá (26) (LLM translated)

O Thou merciful God! O Thou who art mighty and powerful! O Thou most kind Father! These servants have gathered, turn to Thee, supplicate at Thy threshold, and long for Thy infinite bounty according to Thy great promise. They have no other aim than Thy good pleasure. They have no other intention than to serve the world of humanity.
O God! Let this company shine. Make the hearts tender. Bestow the gifts of the Holy Spirit. Endow them with heavenly power. Bless them with heavenly understanding. Increase their sincerity, that they may turn to Thy kingdom with complete humility and penitence, and engage in service to the world of humanity. May each one become a shining candle. May each one become a twinkling star. May each one become beautiful in color and fragrant in scent in the kingdom of God.
O kind Father! Confer Thy blessings. Look not upon our shortcomings. Protect us under Thy shelter. Think not of our sins. Heal us with Thy grace. We are weak; Thou art mighty. We are poor; Thou art rich. We are sick; Thou art the Healer. We are needy; Thou art most generous.
O God! Surround us with Thy providence. Thou art the Powerful. Thou art the Giver. Thou art the Beneficent.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

eo.cat: Preĝoj de l’ Vizito - Preĝo de l’ Renkontiĝo (Munájátu’l-Liqá’)

prayers.text:
O my God! O my God! Verily these servants turn their faces unto Thee and implore Thy bounteous grace. Verily they are attracted by Thy holiness and inflamed with the fire of Thy love and seek confirmation from Thy wondrous realm in hope of being admitted into Thy heavenly kingdom. Verily they long for Thy gifts to descend and wish for enlightenment from the sun of reality. O Lord! Make them as shining lamps, as bounteous signs, as fruitful trees, as brilliant stars. May they advance in Thy service and be bound to Thee by the ties of Thy love and union in longing for the lights of Thy favour. O Lord! Make them as signs of guidance, as banners of Thy immortal kingdom, as waves upon the sea of Thy bounteous grace, as mirrors to the light of Thy royal majesty.

Verily, Thou art the Bountiful. Verily, Thou art the Merciful, verily, Thou art the Precious, the Beloved.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O Thou, merciful God! O Thou, Who art strong and powerful! O Thou, most kind Father! These servants have gathered together, turning toward Thee, offering supplications at Thy threshold, desiring Thy boundless favors which Thou hast generously promised. They have no purpose but Thy pleasure. They have no intention but service to the world of humanity.

O God! Make this gathering radiant. Make their hearts merciful. Send down the graces of the Holy Spirit. Assist them with power from heaven. Endow them with heavenly minds. Increase their sincerity, so that with all humility and contrition they may turn toward Thy kingdom and be occupied with service to the world of humanity. May each one become a burning candle. May each one become a shining star. In the Kingdom of God, may the color of each be beautiful and the fragrance sweet.

O Kind Father! Send down Thy blessings. Consider not our shortcomings. Take us under Thy protection. Forgive us our sins. Heal us with Thy mercy. We are weak, Thou art strong. We are poor, Thou art rich. We are sick, Thou art the Physician. We are in need, Thou art most generous.

O God! Assist us with Thy providence. Thou art the Powerful. Thou art the Giver. Thou art the Beneficent.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
# He is God
My Lord and Supporter of every gathering convened to exalt Thy Name of mercy, and the Inspirer of every band that hath united to serve at the threshold of Thy oneness, I ask Thee by Thy hidden beauty in the realms of Thy Most Glorious Unseen, that Thou mayest encompass these souls with the glances of the eye of Thy mercy, and confirm them with mighty strength, and strengthen their backs with Thy prevailing power that floweth through all things. Verily, Thou art the Hearer of prayer, and Thou art powerful over all things.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
The following is a translation of the Baha'i prayer by 'Abdu'l-Baha from Persian to English:

He is God! O peerless Lord! Assist the friends of 'Abdu'l-Baha and show magnanimity to the sinners. Prepare the banquet of forgiveness and bestow the cup of grace and bounty. Give refuge and shelter to the helpless and the homeless, and grant them awareness. Be a helper and a protector, so that every captive and poor person may receive a share and benefit from the table of Thy bounty. Thou art the Mighty, the Powerful, and Thou art the Giver, the Bestower, the Merciful.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Praised be Thou, O Lord of all creation, Thou unto Whom all must turn. By my inner and outer tongue I testify that Thou hast manifested and revealed Thyself, sent down Thy signs and proclaimed Thy proofs. I testify that Thou art independent of all except Thee and sanctified above all earthly things. I implore Thee, by the transcendent glory of Thy Cause and the supreme power of Thy Word, to confirm him who desireth to offer what Thou hast prescribed unto him in Thy Book, and to observe that, whereby the fragrance of Thy acceptance will be shed forth. Thou art, indeed, the All-Bountiful, the Most Merciful, the All-Forgiving, the Most Generous.

 * [ḥuqúqu’lláh] Ar.: "The Right of God," a Baháʼí spiritual and socio-economic law
prayers.name: The Right of God (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
He is God!
O Lord! This assemblage hath gathered in this meeting out of pure love and with utmost unity and accord. O God! Illumine the faces, gladden the spirits with the Great Glad-Tidings, brighten the eyes with the beholding of the signs of guidance, and make the ears to delight in hearkening to the Call from on high. O Lord! We are all sinners; do Thou forgive us. We are all full of faults; do Thou pardon us. Grant us Thy shelter, and through Thy forgiveness, make our shortcomings perfect. Deliver these souls from the world of illusions and guide them to the Truth, that they may search after reality, be distanced from the world of dust, and draw nigh unto the Kingdom. May they be freed from this darksome world and be joined to the world of light. Thou art the Powerful, the Mighty, the Giver, the Kind.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

fa.cat: محافل روحانی مجامع و جلسات عمومی

fa-translit.cat: محافل روحانی مجامع و جلسات عمومی

prayers.text:
O Kind, Forgiving, and Radiant Lord of earth and heaven,
we are all gathered, yearning for You,
needy, Your dervishes,
helpless wanderers and distraught fallen ones,
dejected, withered ones,
in pain, grant healing,
ailing, bestow a remedy,
disheartened, give us a flame,
withered, scatter a glimmer,
strangers, make us known (to You),
make us moths burnt by the candle of faithfulness,
thirsty and steeped in heart's blood, bring forth a delightful spring and let us drink from the water of life,
cast commotion in the city and spread a tumult in the universe, so that hearts may become astonished by You and souls may be sacrificed to You.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

fa.cat: محافل روحانی مجامع و جلسات عمومی

fa-translit.cat: محافل روحانی مجامع و جلسات عمومی

prayers.text:
He is the All-Glorious!

O pure God, O loving Lord! These companions are captivated by Thy fragrance and bewildered in Thy path. They have freed themselves from every bond and joined themselves to Thy love. They are detached from the world and its people, and are enthralled and ensnared by Thy love. They seek no cure for any pain but Thy grace, and no balm for any wound but in Thy path. They ever desire Thy endless forgiveness and crave Thy boundless favors.

O Kind Bestower! Ennoble these Thy lowly ones and make these captives of Thy love to be princes. Raise them to the highest heaven, and in their self-sacrifice, establish them upon the throne of eternal glory.

Thou art the Powerful, the Mighty, the All-Seeing, the All-Hearing.

Praise be to Thee for that which Thou hast guided us to, and thanks be to Thee for that which Thou hast fulfilled. We praise Thee and thank Thee for Thy bounty, and we supplicate Thee every morning and evening.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

fa.cat: محافل روحانی مجامع و جلسات عمومی

fa-translit.cat: محافل روحانی مجامع و جلسات عمومی

prayers.text:
He is God
O Lord, bestow upon Thy yearning servants the effulgence of Thy favour and open wide the doors of Thy bestowal. Purify them from every impurity and cleanse them, and deliver them from all unworthiness. Perfume their garments like the tunic of Joseph, and fill their character and their dispositions with musk and ambergris like the pouch of the morning breeze. Clothe them with the attributes of Thy loving-kindness and adorn them with the manners of the spiritual assembly, so that they may become manifestations of the signs of Thy oneness and evidences of the effulgent light of the unique Lord, and causes of the glory of Thy Cause and the exaltation of their own station, that their extremity may be changed into ease, and their poverty into riches; that they may gain eternal glory and everlasting favour, become manifestations of holiness and the dawning-places of sanctity, and recount the accounts of the world of lights and spirituality, so that all doors may be opened unto them from all directions. Verily, Thou art the Powerful, the Mighty.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

fa.cat: محافل روحانی مجامع و جلسات عمومی

fa-translit.cat: محافل روحانی مجامع و جلسات عمومی

prayers.text:
**He is the All-Bountiful**

O kind Lord, lay hold of these prisoners of Thy love, and be their refuge, shelter, and protector. Send Thou the holy breaths from the rose-garden of Thy favour, make the courts of the hearts a rose-garden of bestowal and a meadow of reality, make them weary of all save Thyself, and cause them to commune with Thee in intimate worship, make the insignificant ant a Solomon of the magnificent dominion, and the humble atom a prince of the highest heaven, grant the drop the favour of the ocean, and give the blade of grass the freshness and beauty of the verdant tree, all are Thy friends and servants at Thy threshold; bestow Thy grace and bounty, and in this blessed day make Thine especial confirmation manifest. Thou art the Bountiful, the Merciful, the Radiant, and the Shining.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)
