prayers.text:
He is God

Pure Creator, we are all sinners and You are the Forgiver, we are all full of faults and You are the Remover of distress.
Do not record the sins of these sinners in the preserved tablet, and erase them from the inscribed scroll.
Do not look at (our) worthiness and capacity, but deal with us according to what is worthy of the threshold of the All-Merciful.
Forgiveness is a glorious attribute, and pardon and clemency are among the greatest perfections of that Majestic Lord.
So forgive the sins of these wretched ones and overlook the shortcomings of these sinners.
The sin of this servant is the greatest of sins, and the sin of this helpless one is greater than a majestic mountain.
First, forgive the shortcomings of this one without provision, then the sins of every ignorant sinner.
You are the Bountiful and the Merciful, and You are the Forgiver and the Helper.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

fa.cat: محافل روحانی مجامع و جلسات عمومی

fa-translit.cat: محافل روحانی مجامع و جلسات عمومی

prayers.text:
He is the All-Glorious

O Lord, O Merciful, these friends abide at Thy threshold, and these companions are flames burning from the heat of love. In remembrance of Thee, they are like the surging sea, and in the assembly of Divine Unity, like a brilliant lamp. Each is a wave in the ocean of Thy grace, and each a casket of the pearls of Thy guidance. At dawn, with a knowing heart, they weep and lament, and at night, in remembrance of Thy beauty, they sleep and find rest. Thou art the Generous and the Merciful, and Thou art the Everlasting and the Most Compassionate. O Thou Self-Sufficient, forgive the dead, and favour those who remain. Be the cure for the helpless, and grant refuge and safety to the free. Overlook our shortcomings, and forgive our sins. Thou art the Veiler (of faults), Thou art the Forgiver.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

fa.cat: محافل روحانی مجامع و جلسات عمومی

fa-translit.cat: محافل روحانی مجامع و جلسات عمومی

prayers.text:
He is the All-Glorious,
O Lord, make this gathering a shining lamp and confirm these souls, make the faces radiant and the dispositions a garden and paradise, quicken the spirits with the breaths of holiness and adorn the beings with the great guidance, bestow heavenly forgiveness and grant abundant merciful gifts, and preserve and protect them in the sanctuary of Thy care and protection, include endless favours and perfect special bestowals.
Thou art the Giver, the Bestower, and the All-Knowing.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

fa.cat: محافل روحانی مجامع و جلسات عمومی

fa-translit.cat: محافل روحانی مجامع و جلسات عمومی

prayers.text:
God is Glory.
O Lord, enlighten this assembly and make this gathering a candle of the world, for their intention is the common good and their aim is service to humanity.
O Merciful Lord, such an assembly is deserving of Thy favours, and such a gathering is worthy of boundless grace and bestowal.
Thou art Almighty, Glorious, and All-Powerful, and Thou art the One, incomparable Lord.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

fa.cat: محافل روحانی مجامع و جلسات عمومی

prayers.text:
He is God
O Unique Lord, make these souls wholly luminous, their hearts merciful, and their spirits divine, that they may in all stations arise to that which is befitting this Great Day, and be enabled to attain Thy pleasure.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

fa.cat: محافل روحانی مجامع و جلسات عمومی

fa-translit.cat: محافل روحانی مجامع و جلسات عمومی

prayers.text:
Sing, O My servant, the verses of God,
which you have received,
as they are intoned by those
who stand close to Him,
so that the sweetness of your melody
may enrapture your own soul,
and seize the heart of every man!

He who recites in the privacy of his room
the verses revealed by God,
the fragments of his words shall be scattered
by the omnipresent angels of the Almighty,
and they shall make the heart
of every righteous man throb.

Though he may at first suspect nothing of its effect,
the virtue of the grace in which he has shared
must, sooner or later,
change his soul.

Thus have the mysteries of God's Revelation
been ordained by the Will of Him,
Who is the source of power and wisdom.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O Lord my God! What mystery is there in the Fire which Thou didst kindle in my heart! My very limbs bear witness to the intensity of its heat, and show forth the destructive power of its flame. Should the tongue of my body ever venture to describe Thee as One Whose potency hath ever been superior to the potency of the mightiest of men, the tongue of the heart would tell me: 'Thou art naught but words adequate only for things that are of the same kind and nature. But He, verily, is infinitely exalted above the mention of all His creatures.'
## Strength in Love and Adversity
The power of Thy might beareth witness unto me, O my Well-Beloved! Every member of my body would seem to be endued with a tongue to glorify Thee and magnify Thy name. Armed with the power of Thy love, the hatred which moveth them that are set against Thee can never alarm me, and with Thy praise upon my lips, the judgments of Thy decree can never fill me with sorrow. Strengthen, then, the love of Thee in my breast, and suffer me to face the assaults of all the peoples of the earth. I swear by Thee! Every hair of my head crieth out: 'Were it not for the adversities which have befallen me in Thy way, how could I ever have tasted the divine sweetness of Thy tenderness and love?'
## Steadfastness and Service
Send down, therefore, O my Lord, upon me and upon them that love me, that which will make us steadfast in Thy Faith. Suffer them, then, to become the Hands of Thine Cause amongst Thy servants, that they may spread Thy signs and manifest Thy sovereignty. There is no God other than Thee, Who hast the power to do whatsoever Thou wishest. Verily, Thou art the Most Glorious, the Most Praised.
prayers.name: Invocation and Divine Fire (LLM translated)

th.cat: บทอธิษฐานเพื่อการอภัย

prayers.text:
It is Thou, O my God, Who through Thy names healest the sick and restoreth the ailing, Who quencheth the thirst of the thirsty and allayest the suffering of the pained, Who guidest the straying and lifteth up the abased, Who enrich the poor and enlighten the ignorant, Who gladden the toil-worn and delight the burdened, Who warmeth the shivering and raiseth up the oppressed. By Thy name was all creation stirred, the heavens spread forth, the earth founded, and the clouds formed to rain down upon the earth. This is indeed a token of Thy grace unto all Thy creatures.

I implore Thee, therefore, by Thy name through which Thou didst reveal Thy divine essence and exalt Thy Cause above all creation, by every one of Thy most exalted titles and Thy most glorious attributes, by all Thy perfections, for the sake of which Thy transcendent, Most Holy Essence is praised—send forth this night from the clouds of Thy mercy the showers of Thy healing upon this infant, whom Thou hast united in the kingdom of Thy creation unto Thyself, the Most Glorious. Clothe him, then, O my God, through Thy grace, with the raiment of well-being and health, and preserve him, O my Beloved, from every affliction and sickness, and from all that is abhorrent unto Thee. Thy power is indeed equal unto all things. Thou art the All-Powerful, the Self-Subsisting. Send unto him, O my God, also the good of this and of the future world, and the good of past and future generations. Verily, Thy power and Thy wisdom are able to do this.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O My Lord, my God! Aid Thy lovers to remain steadfast in Thy Cause, to walk in Thy paths, to remain firm in Thy Teachings. Grant them Thy bounty that they may overcome the assaults of self and passion, and follow the light of Thy divine guidance. Thou art the All-Powerful, the Most Compassionate, the Self-Subsisting, the Bestower, the Loving, the Almighty, the Most Generous.

* "Strive ye with what ye have been commanded, and let not the wicked among men and their words grieve you." - Báb
* "It is easy to approach the Kingdom of Heaven, but difficult to stand firm and steadfast in it, for its trials are severe and hard to bear." - ‘Abdu’l-Bahá
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text: It is incumbent upon you to cherish them with the utmost affection and loving-kindness, and to encourage them to spiritual pursuits, that their faces may be turned towards God, that their lives may be molded after the pattern of His laws and their character be unsurpassed; that they may possess all praiseworthy and noble attributes; that they may acquire the knowledge of all sciences and arts; that they may be consecrated to the spiritual and eternal life, be enkindled with the fire of the love of God, and be numbered among the spiritual inhabitants of His Kingdom, receiving from the Heavenly Host the education that is of God.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O Thou, Provider! Thou didst breathe over the friends in the West the sweet fragrance of the Holy Spirit and, with the light of divine guidance, Thou didst illuminate the Western sky. Thou hast caused the once remote to draw nigh unto Thyself; transformed strangers into affectionate friends; awakened those who slept; made heedless ones attentive.
O Thou, Provider! Aid these noble friends to win Thy good pleasure. Make them equally kind to strangers and to friends. Cause them to enter the world that perisheth not; grant them a share of heavenly grace; make them true Bahá’ís, sincere unto God; protect them from outward shows and establish them firmly in the truth. Make them signs and symbols of the Kingdom, luminous stars above the horizons of this nether life. May they be a comfort and solace to mankind, and promoters of world peace. Intoxicate them with the wine of Thy purpose, and suffer them all to tread the path of Thy commandments.
O Thou, Provider! The dearest hope of this servant of Thy Threshold is to see the friends of the East and West in intimate embrace; to see all members of human society gathered together in love in one great congregation, as drops of water in one mighty sea; to behold all as birds in one rose-garden - as pearls of one ocean, leaves of one tree, rays of one sun.
Thou art the Great, the Almighty; Thou art the God of strength, the All-Powerful, the All-Seeing.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O God, O Thou Who hast shed Thy radiance upon the luminous realities of human beings, pouring upon them the brilliant lights of knowledge and guidance, and hast chosen them from among all created things for this supreme grace, and hast made them to encompass all things, to understand their innermost essence, to discover their mysteries, and to bring them forth from darkness into the visible world! «He, verily, showeth His special mercy to whom He willeth».

O Lord, help Thy loved ones to acquire knowledge and to discover the sciences and arts, and to unravel the secrets treasured in the innermost reality of all created beings. Make them to hear the hidden truths that are written and engraved in the very depths of all that exists. Make them ensigns of guidance among all creatures and penetrating rays of the mind that shed their light upon this «first life». Make them leaders who guide towards Thee, beacons who lead to Thy path, heralds who urge souls to turn towards Thy Kingdom.

Thou art, indeed, the Mighty, the Protector, the Potent, the Defender, the Strong, the Most Generous.
prayers.name: Humanidad (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Prayer for Parents

O Lord! In this Greatest Divine Teachings, Thou dost accept the prayer of children for their parents. This is one of the special and unending gifts of this Teaching.

Therefore, O Thou good Lord, accept the request of this Thy servant at the threshold of Thy oneness and immerse his father in the ocean of Thy grace, since this son has arisen to serve Thee and strives to walk the path of Thy love. Verily, Thou art the Bestower, the Forgiver, the Good.
prayers.name: Family (LLM translated)

th.cat: บทอธิษฐานเพื่อบิดามารดา

prayers.text:
Family
Prayer for Husband

O God, my God! Thy handmaid calls to Thee, trusting in Thee, turning her face toward Thee, and supplicating Thy heavenly favours, to reveal unto her the spiritual mysteries, and to shed upon her the divine light of Thy holiness.

O my Lord! Open Thou the eyes of my husband. With the light of Thy knowledge, gladden his heart, with Thy brilliant spiritual beauty, attract his mind, manifest the effulgent signs of Thy divine unity, to inspire his soul.

O my Lord! Remove Thou the veil from his eyes. Shower upon him Thy boundless favours, inebriate him with the divine wine of Thy love, that he may be counted among those who tread the earth, whose souls soar among the angels of the highest heaven. Make him a shining lamp, and among Thy people, let him radiate the light of wisdom.

Verily, Thou art the Beloved, the Everlasting Bestower, the Bountiful.
prayers.name: 家庭 (LLM translated)

th.cat: บทอธิษฐานให้สามี

prayers.text:
O Thou kind Lord! O Thou Who art bountiful and merciful! We are servants at Thy threshold, gathered beneath the sheltering shadow of Thy divine unity.
The sun of Thy mercy shineth upon all, and the clouds of Thy grace rain down upon all.
Thy gifts encompass all, Thy loving providence sustaineth all, Thy protection shieldeth all, and the glance of Thine affection resteth upon all.
O Lord! Grant us Thy boundless gifts and let the light of Thy guidance shine.
Enlighten the eyes and gladden the hearts with everlasting joy.
Endow all people with a new spirit and bestow upon them eternal life.
Open the gates of true understanding and let the light of faith shine brightly.
Gather all people beneath the shadow of Thy grace and let them unite in harmony, so that they may become as the rays of one sun, as the waves of one sea, and the fruits of one tree.
May they drink from the same source.
May they be refreshed by the same breeze.
May they be illumined by the same light.
Thou art the Giver, the Gracious, the Almighty.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
The prayer provided in the prompt is the "Prayer for Unity" by Bahá'u'lláh, not the Prayer for Children. Here is the English translation:

O my God! O my God! Unite the hearts of Thy servants, and reveal to them Thy great purpose. May they follow Thy commandments and abide in Thy law. Help them, O God, in their endeavor, and grant them strength to serve Thee. O God, leave them not to themselves, but guide their steps by the light of Thy knowledge, and cheer their hearts by Thy love. Verily, Thou art their Helper and their Lord.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Praised be Thou, O God, for Thy revelation of love to mankind! O Thou, who art our life and light, lead Thy servants on Thy path and make us rich in Thee and free from all except Thee.

O God, teach us of Thy unity and grant us understanding of Thy incomparable station, so that we may not see anyone other than Thee. Thou art the Merciful and the bestower of grace!

O God, enkindle in the hearts of Thy loved ones the fire of Thy love, so that it may consume the thought of all that is not of Thee.

O God, reveal to us Thy exalted eternity - that Thou hast always been and shall always remain, and that no God exists except Thee. Verily, in Thee we find safety and strength.
prayers.name: Family (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O God, O Thou who hast bestowed Thy greatness upon the radiant realities of humankind, diffusing upon them the brilliant lights of knowledge and guidance, and hast chosen them from all creations for this heavenly grace, and made them to encompass all things, to understand their inner essence, and to reveal their secrets, bringing them out from darkness into the visible world! 'He truly shows His unique mercy to whomever He wills.'1 O Lord, help Thy beloved ones to acquire knowledge, sciences, and arts, and to reveal the secrets hidden in the innermost reality of all creations. Make them to hear the hidden truths that are written and established in the heart of all that exists. Make them to become symbols of guidance among all creations and penetrating rays of the mind that scatter their light in this 'first life'.2 Make them to be guides towards Thee, companions on Thy path, runners who urge people to Thy Kingdom.

Thou art truly the Mighty, the Protector, the Capable, the Defender, the Powerful, the Most Generous.

1 Quran 51:56

2 Quran, 55:19-22
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O my Lord! O my Lord! Verily, Thy servant,
humbled himself before the throne of Thy holiness, and alighted at the gate of Thy unity, hath believed in Thee and in Thy verses, hath borne witness to Thy word, and been consumed with the fire of Thy love, and been drowned in the ocean of Thy knowledge, and been attracted by the breezes of Thy grace, hath cast himself before Thee, turned his face towards Thee, offered his supplications unto Thee, and been established in Thy praise and forgiveness. He hath departed from this world and flown towards Thy eternal dominion, yearning for the grace of meeting Thee.

O my God, glorify his station, shelter him beneath the shadow of Thy loftiest tenderness, and cause him to enter Thy paradise of light, and graciously grant him eternal life in the rose garden of Thy highest glory, that he may plunge into the ocean of divine mysteries.

Verily, Thou art the Self-Sufficing, the Almighty, the Forgiver, and the Provider.
prayers.name: PRAYER FOR THE DEPARTED (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O Lord my God! We are weak, but Thou art the Lord of Power. We are pitiable, but Thou art the Most Mighty, the Most Glorious. We are poor, but Thou art the Lord of all Wealth, the Most Generous. Aid us with Thy boundless grace to serve at Thy Holy Threshold, and with the power of Thy favour and grace support and uphold us as we worship Thee in the places where Thy praise shall flow forth. Help us to spread Thy holy fragrance among Thy creatures, and strengthen our bodies to serve Thee among Thy devoted servants. That we may guide all nations towards Thy Most Great Glory, and call all peoples to the shores of the wondrous ocean of Thy unity.

O Lord of Heaven! Deliver us from the fetters of the earth and its peoples, from the sins and iniquities of past ages, and from the tribulations of the future, so that we may with exceeding joy and radiant gladness proclaim and spread abroad only Thy Word, and rejoice in praising Thee day and night, call all peoples to the path of guidance, charge them to adhere to uprightness and justice, and become the singers of Thy praises among all Thy creatures. Thou art able to do whatsoever Thou pleasest. Verily, Thou art the Almighty, the All-Powerful.
prayers.name: Абдул-Бахагийн 26 мөргөл (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
He is God! O Thou Almighty Lord! Strengthen all mankind that they may do according to the instructions and teachings recorded in these writings, so that wars and strifes may be eliminated from the world of man; that the roots of enmity may be destroyed and the foundations of love and affection be established; that the hearts may be filled with love and the souls be attracted; that wisdom may advance and the faces become brightened and illumined; that there be no more wars and strifes and that reconciliation and peace appear; that the Unity of the world of man may pitch its tent on the “apex of the horizons,” so that peoples and parties become as one nation, that different continents become as one continent and the whole earth as one land; that the sects of antagonizing and dogmatic religions be unified; that the world of creation be adorned and all the people of the earth abide in unity and peace.

Verily, Thou art the Giver, the Bestower, the Beholder!
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O Allah, Thou art that Allah Who hath scattered the brilliant rays of knowledge and guidance upon mankind, spreading His effulgence upon their luminous beings, and hath chosen them from among all creation for this high favor, and hath made them capable of encompassing all things by bringing them from the unseen world to the seen world, to comprehend their inner essence, to unveil their secrets! 'He bestoweth His grace on whomsoever He willeth.' 1 (Quran, 3:73)

O Lord, assist Thy chosen ones to obtain knowledge, learning, and art, and to unveil the hidden secrets within the essence of all created things. Let them be aware of the hidden truths written and engraved in the core of all that exists. Make them banners of guidance and penetrating rays of intellect among all creatures, which shine their light upon the 'first creation' 2 (Quran, 56:62) in this world. Make them guides who lead to Thee, conductors on Thy path, and heralds who inspire humanity to strive towards Thy dominion.

Verily, Thou art the Mighty, the Preserver, the Powerful, the Protector, the Glorious, and the Most Generous.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
In the Name of our Lord, the Most Holy, the Greatest, the Most Sublime, the Most Glorious!

O God, my God! Thou seest how Thy servant hath set his face towards Thee and desired to be honored by fulfilling what hath been ordained unto him in Thy Book. Ordain for him, through Thy Most Sublime Pen, that which will draw him nigh unto the Most Sublime Height. Thou art, verily, the Educator of the world and the Lord of nations, and Thou art, verily, the Mighty, the All-Dominating, the Omnipotent.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text: Magnified art Thou, O Lord of all creation, unto Whom all things must turn. With my inner and outer tongue, I bear witness that Thou hast manifested and revealed Thyself, sent down Thy signs, and proclaimed Thy proofs. I attest to Thy self-sufficiency from all save Thee and Thy sanctity above all earthly things. I beseech Thee, by the transcendent glory of Thy Cause and by the supreme potency of Thy Word, to grant confirmation unto him who desireth to offer that which Thou hast prescribed for him in Thy Book and to observe that which will spread the fragrance of Thy acceptance. Verily, Thou art the Almighty, the All-Merciful, the All-Clement, the All-Bountiful.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Revealed from the Kingdom of Glory for the Days of Há.

In My Name, the Exile, the Wronged One.

O my God! These days have been ennobled by the name of ‘Há’ which Thou didst confer upon them. Methinks Thou hast made every one of those days a herald and a messenger, bearing unto men the glad tidings of the days in which Thou hast enjoined the Fast upon Thy servants, that every soul may prepare itself for meeting Thee, and may fix in its heart a place for Thee, and purify it by Thy Name for Thy descent upon it. O God of all existence and the Mighty One over all things seen and unseen! I entreat Thee by Thy Most Great Names, and Thy most exalted attributes, and the manifestations of Thy Cause in the Kingdom of Creation, and the dawning-places of Thy Revelation in the Realm of Everlastingness, to shower upon him who hath fasted in Thy love the rains of the clouds of Thy mercy and the effusions of the heaven of Thy providence. O Lord! Withhold not from them what is with Thee, as a reward for what they have done in Thy love and good-pleasure. I beg of Thee, O King of Names, and Maker of the heaven, and Creator of the heaven, and Sovereign of Names, and Upholder of the heaven, and Preserver thereof, to open the gates of Thy grace and mercy and providence unto the faces of those who have submitted to Thy command. O Lord! They are the poor, and with Thee are the oceans of wealth; they are the weak, and Thou art the Mighty by Thy power, and the Dominant by Thy sovereignty. O Lord! Thou seest their eyes gazing towards the horizon of Thy bounty, and their hearts turned towards the quarter of Thy gifts. Look, O my God, upon the hearts which Thou hast made as thrones for Thy sovereignty and as dawning-places for the effulgence of the lights of Thy love. Shower upon them, O my God, from the ocean of Thy generosity that which will make them the manifestations of this Name among Thy servants. O Lord! Strengthen them to that whereby Thy Cause may be exalted in Thy Kingdom, and then reveal from them that whereby their names may endure in Thy Kingdom and Thy Realm. O Lord! These are they who have been consumed by the fire of Thy love in Thy lands, and have witnessed every adversity in Thy path, and have tasted every bitterness, hoping for the honey of bounty from the heaven of Thy generosity. O Lord! These are they who have borne in His love what Thy servants and Thy creatures have not borne. O Lord! The devoted ones have been consumed.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Revealed in the Days of Al-Hā':
His utterance, Most High:
He is the Most Holy, the Most Great, the Most Beautiful.

Glory be to Him Who hath manifested Himself as He willed! Verily, He is the All-Powerful, the All-Sufficient, the Everlasting. These are the days of Al-Hā', and We have commanded all to spend thereon upon themselves and upon those who have turned towards this Exalted Station. Remember God, and then know their worth, for they speak of that Name by which God hath subdued the unseen and the visible. Verily, We have made them before the fast, a bounty from Us, and I am the All-Powerful to do what hath been and what shall be. Blessed is he who hath acted according to what he was commanded by God, and woe unto every heedless, rejected one. Verily, We have revealed the verses and sent them down unto thee on this sacred and praiseworthy Day, that thou mayest give thanks to God, thy Lord, and remember Him with a remembrance that may awaken the heedless.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
The provided text is a Bahá'í prayer. I will translate it into English for you.

## Trials and Calamities

O my Lord, my God, may Thy glory be magnified! O my Master, Thou hearest the sighs of those who desire to see Thee, but are yet separated from Thee and far from Thy court. Thou alone bearest witness to the lamentations of those who, though knowing Thee, are far from Thee and long to meet Thee. Their hearts are like treasures of Thy remembrance and glorification, and through them only Thy power is manifested. For the sake of such souls, I pray Thee, O Lord, grant these Thy servants strength to attain the station of the manifestation of the effulgence of Thy glory, and admit those who depend solely on Thee into the pavilion of Thy transcendent grace and favor.

O my Lord, I am naked, clothe me with the garment of Thy tender mercy. I am very thirsty, let me drink from the ocean of Thy boundless grace. I am a stranger; lead me to the source of Thy favors. I am sick, sprinkle upon me the healing waters of Thy grace. I am a prisoner, release me from bonds by the power of Thy might and will, so that flying on the wings of detachment, I may reach the highest peaks of Thy creation. Verily, Thou doest whatsoever Thou willeth. There is no other God beside Thee. Thou art the Companion in distress. Thou art in all respects glorious, and unimpeded.
prayers.name: કસોટીઓ અને સંકટો (LLM translated)

lo.cat: ບົດອະທິຖານວັນໂນວ໌ຣຸສ

prayers.text:
It is God! O incomparable Lord, in Thy supreme wisdom, Thou hast enjoined men to marry so that their generations may succeed each other in this contingent world and that forever, as long as the world lasts, they may devote themselves at the threshold of Thy unity with humility and fervor, adoration, deference, and veneration. "I have created spirits and men only that they may worship Me."* In the heaven of Thy mercy, unite these two birds of the nest of Thy love; make them instruments that attract eternal grace so that, from the union of these two oceans of love, a wave of tenderness may arise and carry to the shores of life the pearls of a pure and beautiful posterity. "He hath allowed the two seas to meet. Between them is a barrier which they shall not transgress. Which of the signs of your Lord will ye deny? From each of them, He bringeth forth pearls of various kinds."†
O benevolent Lord, cause pearls and corals to be born from this union. Verily, Thou art the Almighty, the Most Great, the Magnanimous.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O my Lord! O my God! These two bright moons have joined in wedlock for Thy love, are united for service at Thy sacred Threshold, and have become one soul for the work of Thy Cause.

O my Master, O Thou most merciful Lord! Make Thou this union a radiant strand of Thy boundless favour; O Thou bounteous, O Thou generous! Make them such a shining ray of Thy gifts, that from this great Tree may there spring forth such branches as may flourish and bear fruit in the rain of Thy bestowals.
Verily Thou art generous, verily Thou art almighty, verily Thou art merciful, O Thou most merciful!
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O my God and my Master! I am a servant of Thine and a child of one of Thy servants. I have risen from my couch at dawn, when the Sun of Thy uniqueness shone in the Dawn of Thy purpose, radiating itself over the whole world, according to what had been prescribed in the Books of Thy Decree.

Praise be to Thee, O my God, for we have awakened to the splendors of the light of Thy knowledge. Send down, then, upon us, O my Lord, that which will enable us to dispense with everyone save Thee, and detach us from all things, save from Thee. Destine for me, also, and for those who are dear to me and for all my kindred, the good of this world and of the next. Preserve us, then, O Thou Beloved of all creation and Object of the desire of all that hath been and all that is to come—through Thy unfailing protection—against those who have manifested the Evil Entity that whispereth in the hearts of men. Thou art Powerful to do as Thou pleasest. Thou art, indeed, the All-Possessing, the Help in Peril, the Self-Subsisting.

Bless, O my Lord God, Him Whom Thou hast set above Thy most excellent Titles, through Whom Thou hast separated the righteous from the wicked, and help us, graciously, to do that which Thou dost love and desire. Bless, also, O my God, Those Who are Thy Words and Thy Letters, and all who have turned their faces unto Thee, directing them towards Thy countenance and hearkening unto Thy Call.

Thou art, indeed, the Lord and King of all men, and Thou hast power over all things.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

Thanks be to Thee, O my God! Thou hast awakened me, Thou hast caused me to be born and to emerge from my slumber. My face turned towards the splendours of the Sun of Thy revelation which illumineth the heavens of Thy power and Thy majesty, I have awakened this morning, recognizing Thy signs, believing in Thy book, and clinging to Thy cord.
By the power of Thy will and the irresistible might of Thy purpose, I implore Thee. Make Thy revelations during my sleep the surest foundations of the habitations of Thy love, buried in the hearts of Thy loved ones, and the best instrument for the revelation of the proofs of Thy grace and Thy tender kindness.
O my Lord, from Thy sublime pen, grant me the good of this world and the next. I bear witness that Thou holdest in Thy hands the reins of all things.
Thou dost wield them as Thou pleasest. There is none other God but Thee, the Mighty, the Faithful.
By Thy command, humiliation is changed into glory, weakness into strength, powerlessness into might, fear into serenity, and doubt into certainty.
There is none other God but Thee, the Powerful, the Bountiful.
Thou dost not disappoint him who seeketh Thee, nor dost Thou turn away him who desireth Thee. Ordain for me that which is meet for the heavens of Thy generosity and the ocean of Thy munificence. Thou art, verily, the All-Powerful, the Omnipotent.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O my God and my Lord! I am Thy servant and the son of Thy servant. At this dawn hour I have arisen from my couch, when the sun of Thy unity hath shone forth from the horizon of Thy will, according to the laws Thou hast decreed in the Book of Thy command, and hath shed its radiance upon all the world.
Praise be to Thee, O my God, that we have awakened unto the dawning-light of Thy knowledge. Send down unto us, O my Lord, that which shall make us detached from all save Thee, and enable us to break free from all attachment to any save Thee. Ordain for me and for them that love me, for all men and all women, alike, good and welfare in this world and in the world to come. Shield us, through Thy never-failing providence, from all those whom Thou hast made the symbol of evil inclinations, O Thou the Desire of all the worlds and the Beloved of all creation! Thou art able to do whatsoever Thou pleasest. Thou art, in truth, the All-Powerful, the Deliverer, the Self-Subsistent.
Bless, O God, my God, Him Whom Thou hast made the Dayspring of Thy most excellent titles, and by Whom Thou hast distinguished the pure from the wicked. Graciously aid us to do that which is pleasing unto Thee, and to be fashioned after the manner of Thy love. O my Lord, bless them that are the Words and the Letters of Thy command, and them that have turned towards Thee, that have set their hope in Thee, and have heard Thy call. Thou art, in truth, the Lord and the Sovereign of all things, and art supreme.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

lo.cat: ບົດອະທິຖານເພື່ອມະນຸດຊາດ

prayers.text:
O God, and the God of all Names, and the Creator of the heavens! My prayer in Thy Name who is the Sun of Thy power and the Dawn of Thy manifested power, by Whom all made things are moved, all animated beings are resurrected, and all disembodied spirits are sustained – my prayer to Thee is to grant that I may cast off all attachment to any save Thee, that I may follow Thy purpose, that I may desire what Thou dost desire through the power of Thy sovereignty, and that I may do the work of Thy pleasure.

Here I am, in this moment, praying to Thee, my God, to bestow upon me the way whereby I may be led to relinquish all save Thee. Thou seest me, O my God, my face is turned towards Thee, my hands cling to the cord of Thy loving-kindness. Send down upon me Thy bounty, and write for me Thy inscriptions concerning Thy chosen ones.

Thou art able to do what Thou wishest. There is no God beside Thee, the Forgiver of sins in all ages, the Bountiful.-All.
prayers.name: MŌ TE PONONGATANGA (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
"The Naw-Rúz celebration should be held before sunset on March 21st. It has nothing to do with the Nineteen Day Feast. The latter is of administrative significance, while Naw-Rúz is our New Year celebration, a feast of hospitality and joy." —from a letter written on behalf of Shoghi Effendi

I glorify Thee, O my God, for Thou hast ordained Naw-Rúz a feast for those who have observed the fast out of love for Thee, and have abstained from all that is repugnant to Thee. Grant, O my Lord, that the flame of Thy love and the embers kindled by the fast Thou hast ordained may ardently inspire them in the service of Thy Cause, and render them capable of devoutly glorifying Thee and remembering Thee.

As Thou hast adorned them with the ornament of the fast ordained by Thee, O my Lord, so by Thy grace and generous patronage, adorn them now also by accepting this fast. For all the deeds of men depend on Thy good pleasure and are directed by Thy decree. If Thou dost regard him who hath broken the fast as one who hath observed it, then he shall be reckoned among those who have observed it from everlasting. But if Thou choosest that one who hath observed the fast be regarded as having broken it, then he shall be reckoned among those who have defiled the Vesture of Thy Revelation with dust, and have strayed far from the crystal clear waters of the living Fountain.

By Thee was the banner "Praised be Thou in Thy deeds" raised, and the standard "Thy commandments must be obeyed" unfurled. Make known to Thy servants this exalted station of Thine, O my God, that they may realize that the excellence of all things depends on Thy decree and Thy word, and that the measure of the value of every act is the good pleasure of Thy permission and Thy will, and that they may recognize that the reins of men's deeds are held by Thy acceptance and Thy command. Make this known to them, that nothing may keep them far from Thy Beauty in these days, concerning which Christ thus loudly proclaims: "All power is Thine, O Thou, the Begetter of the Spirit (Jesus)," and concerning which Thy Friend (Muhammad) thus cries out: "Glory be to Thee, O Thou, the Beloved
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O Lord, my Lord!
This is the day which Thou hast consecrated by Thy Holy tongue and pen of glory, and made it a day of heavenly blessings, and of divine clemency and favour. Thou hast honored it with Thy gifts, bestowed upon it Thy grace, enwrapped it in Thy effulgence, and made it a day of joy and gladness unto such as have turned towards Thy divine revelation, that their hearts may rejoice, and the radiance of felicity may illumine the lives of them that remember the manifestation of Thy glory on Thy holy Mount.
Consecrate it, O Lord, by Thy favour, and make it a day of joy and of blessing unto Thy loved ones, who have remained steadfast and unmoved in Thy covenant, Thy friends who have sung Thy praises, and specially unto this Thy servant, who hath turned his face towards Thy holy kingdom, sought the glory of Thy might and Thy sovereignty, circled round the throne of Thy majesty, and bowed himself down on the earth at the threshold of Thy divine mercy.
Thou art the Gracious, the Bountiful, the Most Merciful.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
# THE MOST GREAT, THE MOST GLORIOUS
This is a Book from Our presence unto her who hath believed in God, the All-Possessing, the Self-Subsisting, and desired her Lord when He came with manifest sovereignty. If thou wouldst gaze upon the greater prospect, thou wouldst behold the Lord of destiny in the hands of those who disbelieved in God, the Almighty, the Beloved. Despite all that hath befallen Him, His tongue speaketh of the mention of God, His foot walketh towards the desired aim, His heart stirreth at the remembrance of the Beloved, and with His finger the thread of existence moveth. Remember thy Lord, O My handmaiden, with this mentioned remembrance. O my God and my Beloved! I am a handmaiden from amongst Thy handmaidens; I have turned unto Thee and believed in Thee after the servants turned away from Thee. O my Lord! Inscribe me among the people of Thy pavilion of glory and the tents of Thy grandeur, then make me of those who were circling around the Throne of Thy grandeur and turned their hearts towards the precinct of Thy good pleasure. Verily, Thou art the Self-Sufficient in all conditions. Have mercy upon Thy male and female servants, then preserve them in the shelter of Thy preservation and protection. Verily, Thou art the All-Powerful, the Most Exalted, the Most High, the Great.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
The prayers revealed by 'Abdu'l-Bahá

You are the Lord of exceeding bounty,
You are the Ruler of perfect compassion.

You are the solace of my heart in the morning,
You alone are the observer of my hardships and losses.

The heart that dwells for a moment on Your tribute,
Seeking no friend other than You,
Willfully desires hardships for itself.

I chant Your praise in my heart,
With joy, it desires peril for itself.

The heart that cannot withstand sighing upon seeing You,
Let it wither and be extinguished.

The eye that does not ache to weep upon seeing You,
It is better that it becomes blind before that.
Protect this humble one in the time of intense suffering,
I beseech You, O God, as the All-Powerful.

Let the eternal and delightful Light
Shine brilliantly within me.

Discarding the consideration of worthiness or unworthiness,
You are the merciful Lord, show mercy, O God.

How can a bird with a broken wing fly?
Have mercy and provide a new one, O Lord.
prayers.name: అబ్దుల్-బహా వెలువరించిన ప్రార్థనలు (LLM translated)

tvl.cat: MO LATOU KO GALO – TALO MO TINO MATE

prayers.text:
O Lord! My God! My refuge in suffering, my shield against calamity, my sanctuary in distress, my companion in loneliness! My solace in sorrow, my loving friend in solitude! Remover of my pains, forgiver of my sins!

I turn my face towards Thee, and with all My heart, and all My soul, and all My tongue, I pray to Thee, O Lord my God! that Thou mayest not let me transgress Thy commandments in this, Thy great epoch, and that Thou mayest cleanse me from all impurity, and enable me to seek the shade of the tree of Thy grace with a pure heart. O Lord, have mercy upon the weak, and heal the sick, and quench the thirst of the yearning.

Bring joy to the heart that hath been set ablaze by the fire of Thy love, and cause it to burn with the flame of Thy heavenly love and spirit. Clothe the body with the raiment of Thy sanctity, and crown the head with the diadem of Thy grace.

Light Thou my face with the lamp of Thy divine unity, and assist me in serving at Thy holy Threshold. Let my heart overflow with Thy love for all Thy creatures, and permit me to be a sign of Thy mercy, and a token of Thy grace, and a promoter of concord amongst all the favoured of Thy name. Enable me to be devoted unto Thee, and to utter praises of Thee, and to forget myself and remember Thee always.

O God, my God! Withdraw not from me the gentle breezes of Thy forgiveness and Thy grace, nor withhold from me the flowing waters of Thy help and Thy favour. Let me repose beneath the shadow of Thy sheltering wings, and look upon me with Thine eye of mercy.

Open my lips to utter Thy praise amongst Thy peoples, and let my voice rise in Thy great assemblies, and my tongue be loosened in Thy remembrance. Thou art indeed the Gracious, the Glorious, the Almighty, the All-powerful.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
My Lord, My Adored One, My King, My Desire! What tongue can recount My gratitude to Thee? I was heedless, Thou didst awaken me. I turned away from Thee, by Thy grace Thou didst help me to turn towards Thee. I was like unto the dead, Thou didst bring me back to life with the water of life. I was withered, Thou didst revive me with the heavenly river of Thy words that flowed from the Pen of the Most Compassionate.
O Thou, the Almighty God! All this hath come to pass through Thy bounty! Suffer him not to be removed from the ocean of Thy munificence, nor to be kept back from the sea of Thy mercy. I implore Thee to help and assist me at all times, and to grant me Thy ancient favours from the heaven of Thy mercy. Verily, Thou art the All-Bountiful and the Everlasting King.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
**Searching for Translations**

I am performing a web search to find an existing English translation of the provided Spanish Bahá'í prayer. My objective is to locate a faithful rendition that preserves the original formatting, including section headers and paragraph breaks.

## Other prayers revealed by Bahá’u’lláh

O God, my God! I give thanks to Thee for having guided me to Thy straight Path and for having enabled me to recognize Thee and turn unto Thee, and for having made known to me the oneness of Thy Essence and the sanctity of Thy Being. I implore Thee, by Them Who are the Dawning-Points of Thy Cause, the Dawns of Thy favour and the Repositories of Thy knowledge and wisdom, to bless the gift Thou hast bestowed upon me through Thy bounty and favour. Ordain for me and for her mother, as well as for her, the good of this world and of the next. Thou art, in truth, the Lord of all that existeth, Who listeneth and is ready to answer.
prayers.name: Otras oraciones reveladas por Bahá’u’lláh (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
(Leaving the house)

     O my Lord, my God! I have left my house, holding fast to the cords of Thy love, and committed my affairs to Thy care and protection. I implore Thy favour to protect me from all who are seeking to bring me to ruin, and from the unjust, the wicked, and the ungodly, who have strayed far from Thee. Let Thy succour guard me, and Thy divine power preserve me, that I may return to my home in safety.

Verily, Thou art the All-Bounteous, the Helper in distress, the Self-Subsisting.
prayers.name: Prayer for Protection (LLM translated)

lo.cat: ຂໍຄວາມຄຸ້ມຄອງ

prayers.text:
# Long Obligatory Prayer
*(Revealed by Baháʼu'lláh)*

**To be recited once in twenty-four hours, at any time one feels at all spiritually inclined to do so.**

*Whoso wisheth to recite this prayer, let him stand up and face God, and, as he standeth in his place, let him look to the right and to the left, as if waiting for the mercy of his Lord, the Most Merciful, the Compassionate. Then let him say:*

**O Thou Who art the Lord of all names and the Maker of the heavens!** I beseech Thee by them Who are the Day-Springs of Thine invisible Essence, the Most Exalted, the All-Glorious, to make of my prayer a fire that will burn away the veils which have shut me out from Thy beauty, and a light that will lead me unto the ocean of Thy Presence.

*Let him then raise his hands in supplication toward God—blessed and exalted be He—and say:*

**O Thou the Desire of the world and the Beloved of the nations!** Thou seest me turning toward Thee, and rid of all attachment to any one save Thee, and clinging to Thy cord, through whose movement the whole creation hath been stirred up. I am Thy servant, O my Lord, and the son of Thy servant. Behold me standing ready to do Thy will and Thy desire, and wishing naught else except Thy good pleasure. I implore Thee by the Ocean of Thy mercy and the Daystar of Thy grace to do with Thy servant as Thou willest and pleasest. By Thy might which is far above all mention and praise! Whatsoever is revealed by Thee is the desire of my heart and the beloved of my soul. O God, my God! Look not upon my hopes and my doings, nay rather look upon Thy will that hath encompassed the heavens and the earth. By Thy Most Great Name, O Thou Lord of all nations! I have desired only what Thou didst desire, and love only what Thou dost love.

*Let him then kneel, and bowing his forehead to the ground, let him say:*

**Exalted art Thou above the description of any one save Thyself, and the comprehension of aught else but Thee.**

*Let him then stand and say:*

**Make my prayer, O my Lord, a fountain of living waters** whereby I may live as long as Thy sovereignty endureth, and may make mention of Thee in every world of Thy worlds.

*Let him again raise his hands in supplication, and say:*

**O Thou in separation from Whom hearts and souls have melted,** and by the blaze of Whose love the whole world hath been set afire! I implore Thee by Thy Name whereby Thou hast subdued the whole creation, not to withhold from me that which is with Thee, O Thou Who rulest over all men! Thou seest, O my Lord, this stranger hastening to his most exalted home beneath the canopy of Thy majesty and within the precincts of Thy mercy; and this transgressor seeking the ocean of Thy forgiveness; and this lowly one the court of Thy glory; and this poor creature the orient of Thy wealth. Thine is the authority to command whatsoever Thou wilt. I bear witness that Thou art to be praised in Thy doings, and to be obeyed in Thy behests, and to remain unconstrained in Thy bidding.

*Let him then raise his hands and repeat the Greatest Name (Alláh-u-Abhá) thrice. Let him then bend down with hands resting on the knees before God—blessed and exalted be He—and say:*

**Thou seest,
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
The translated text is:
Glory be to Thee, O my Lord, Thou who didst call into existence all created things by Thy command.
O Lord! Assist Thy loved ones and grant them Thy victory. O Lord, send down upon them the hosts of Thy divine assistance, that they may aid and succure them, and crown them with glory, and bestow upon them honour and exaltation, and enrich them, and make them triumphant through an wondrous victory, and send down from Thy heaven such as shall succour them, and such as shall cooperate with them, and such as shall be as Thou hast bidden them, from amongst the angels that are in heaven and that are on earth, and all that is between them.
Thou art their Lord, the Lord of heaven and earth, and the Lord of all worlds. O Lord, give unto Thy servants the victory through the power of their strength, and make them prevail over all the peoples of the world, for they are, in truth, Thy servants who have turned themselves away from all else save Thee, and Thou art, verily, the Protector of the sincere.
O Lord, suffer them to be so strengthened by their love of Thy inviolable Faith, that their hearts may become more powerful than all that is in heaven and on earth, and all that is between them; and O Lord, make their hands mighty with the signs of Thy wondrous power, that they may manifest Thy might before all mankind.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
He is the Compassionate, the All-Bountiful!

O God, my God! Thou seest me, Thou knowest me; Thou art my Haven and my Refuge. None have I sought nor any will I seek save Thee; no path have I trodden nor any will I tread but the path of Thy love. In the darksome night of despair, my eye turneth expectant and full of hope to the morn of Thy boundless favor and at the hour of dawn my drooping soul is refreshed and strengthened in remembrance of Thy beauty and perfection. He whom the grace of Thy mercy aideth, though he be but a drop, shall become the boundless ocean, and the merest atom which the outpouring of Thy loving-kindness assisteth, shall shine even as the radiant star.

Shelter under Thy protection, O Thou Spirit of purity, Thou Whom art the All-Bountiful Provider, this enthralled, enkindled servant of Thine. Aid him in this world of being to remain steadfast and firm in Thy love and grant that this broken-winged bird attain a refuge and shelter in Thy divine nest that abideth upon the celestial tree.
prayers.name: Protection (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O My God, Thou art the Goal of my adoration and my longing, Thou Most Bountiful, Most Compassionate! All life cometh from Thee, and all power resteth in the hand of Thy might. Whomsoever Thou exaltest is exalted above the angels and attaineth the station of: 'Verily, We raised him to a high station', and whomsoever Thou dost abase is abased lower than the dust, nay, less than naught.

O Divine Providence! Wicked, sinful, and helpless as we are, we nevertheless seek from Thee a 'seat of truth' and long to gaze upon the Face of the Almighty King. Thine is the command, Thine is all sovereignty, and the dominion of power boweth before Thy decree. All that Thou doest is pure justice, nay, the sum of grace. A ray from the radiance of Thy Name, the All-Merciful, sufficeth to banish and efface every trace of sinfulness from the world, and a breath of the gentle winds of the Day of Thy Revelation sufficeth to adorn all mankind with a new garment.

O Almighty, grant Thy weak creatures Thy strength, and revive those who are as the dead, that they may find Thee, reach the sea of Thy guidance, and remain steadfast in Thy Cause. Should the fragrance of Thy praise be diffused in any of the languages of the East or the West, that language will surely be cherished and esteemed. But if languages are deprived of this sweet perfume, they are unworthy of mention, whether in words or thoughts.

We beseech Thee, O Providence, to show all men Thy way and to guide them aright. Verily, Thou art the Almighty, the All-Powerful, the All-Knowing, the All-Seeing.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Glorified be Thy Name, O Lord! In whom shall I seek refuge, since Thou art, in truth, my God and my Beloved; to whom shall I turn for shelter, since Thou art my Lord and my Possessor; and to whom shall I flee, since Thou art, in truth, my Master and my Sanctuary; and to whom shall I implore, since Thou art, in truth, my Treasure and the Object of my desire; and through whom shall I plead before Thee, since Thou art, in truth, my highest aspiration and my supreme desire? All hope is frustrated, save the yearning for Thy celestial grace, and every door is shut, save that which leadeth to the Fount of Thy blessings.

O my Lord, I beseech Thee by the effulgence of Thy most resplendent glory, before Whose radiance all souls humbly bow down and prostrate in adoration for love of Thee – a glory that doth so radiate that fire is converted into light, the dead are quickened, and all difficulty is transformed into ease. I implore Thee by that great, that wondrous glory and by the glory of Thy exalted sovereignty, O Thou Who art the Lord of indomitable power, that through Thy bounty Thou mayest transform us into that which is possessed by Thee, and mayest help us to become the sources of Thy light, and mayest graciously bestow upon us that which is worthy of the majesty of Thy transcendent dominion. For unto Thee have I lifted up my hands, O Lord, and in Thee have I found the support that sustaineth me, O Lord, and unto Thee have I resigned myself, O Lord, and in Thee have I placed all my trust, O Lord, and by Thee am I strengthened, O Lord.
Verily, there is no power, nor strength, except in Thee.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Glorified art Thou, O Lord my God! I give thanks unto Thee for having enabled me to recognise the Manifestation of Thyself, for having separated me from Thine enemies and exposed before my sight their wickedness and foul deeds in Thy days, and for having delivered me from all attachment to them, and for turning me wholly towards Thy grace and Thy bounteous favours. I give thanks unto Thee, likewise, for having sent down unto me, from the clouds of Thy will, that which hath so purified me from the insinuations of the faithless and the allusions of the unbelievers that I have set my heart firmly on Thee and have fled from them that have denied the light of Thy countenance. I give thanks unto Thee, also, for having enabled me to be steadfast in Thy love, to proclaim Thy praise, and to extol Thy virtues. And I give thanks unto Thee for that Thou hast given me to drink of the cup of Thy mercy which hath surpassed all that is visible and all that is invisible.

Thou art the Almighty, the Most Exalted, the All-Glorious, the Loving.
prayers.name: Thanksgiving and Praise (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
aised be Thou, O my God, that Thou hast graciously remembered me through Thy Most Exalted Pen, in a time when Thou wert captive in the Greatest Prison by reason of what had been perpetrated by the hands of them that have strayed from Thee and from Thy most radiant signs. O my Lord! I have turned towards Thee and set my face towards Thy horizon. I beseech Thee, by the grievances suffered by Him Who is the Dawning Light of Thy signs and the Day-Spring of Thy clear tokens, to ordain for me that which will profit me in every world of Thy worlds. Thou, in truth, knowest me better than I know myself. Thou art the All-Knowing, the All-Informed.

I further ask of Thee, O Lord of all that existeth and Possessor of all that is visible and invisible, to grant unto me a virtuous child who shall call Thee to mind in Thy land, and sing Thy praise throughout Thy kingdoms; albeit, Thou hast, with this Tablet, made me sufficiently rich to dispense with every fruit, or trace, or mention. I conclude my supplication, at this moment, with that which one of Thy chosen ones hath previously expressed: 'O my Lord, leave me not without issue, though Thou Thyself be the best of inheritors.'3
prayers.name: Other prayers revealed by Baháʼu'lláh (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
I beseech Thee, O my God, by Thy mighty signs, and by the revelation of Thy grace amongst men, not to drive me away from the gate of the city of Thy presence, nor to disappoint me of the hopes I have set on the manifestations of Thy grace amidst Thy creatures. Thou seest me, O my God, clinging to Thy name, the Most Holy, the Most Luminous, the Most Mighty, the Most Great, the Most Exalted, the Most Glorious, and holding fast to the hem of the robe to which have clung all in this world and in the world to come.

I beseech Thee, O my God, by Thy most sweet Voice and by Thy most exalted Word, to draw me ever nearer unto the threshold of Thy door, and to suffer me not to be kept remote from the shadow of Thy mercy and the canopy of Thy bounty. Thou seest me, O my God, clinging to Thy name, the Most Holy, the Most Luminous, the Most Mighty, the Most Great, the Most Exalted, the Most Glorious, and holding fast to the hem of the robe to which have clung all in this world and in the world to come.

I beseech Thee, O my God, by the splendor of Thy luminous brow and the brightness of the light of Thy countenance, which shineth from the supreme horizon, to attract me by the fragrance of Thy raiment, and make me drink of the choice wine of Thine utterance. Thou seest me, O my God, clinging to Thy name, the Most Holy, the Most Luminous, the Most Mighty, the Most Great, the Most Exalted, the Most Glorious, and holding fast to the hem of the robe to which have clung all in this world and in the world to come.

I beseech Thee, O my God, by Thy hair which moveth across Thy face, even as Thy most exalted Pen hath moved across the pages of Thy Tablets, shedding the fragrance of hidden meanings over the kingdom of Thy creation, so to raise me up to serve Thy Cause that I shall not fall back, nor be hindered by the suggestions of them who have cavilled at Thy signs and turned away from Thy face. Thou seest me, O my God, clinging to Thy name, the Most Holy, the Most Luminous, the Most Mighty, the Most Great, the Most Exalted, the Most Glorious, and holding fast to the hem of the robe to which have clung all in this world and in the world to come.

I beseech Thee, O my God, by Thy name which Thou hast made the King of Names, by which all who are in heaven and all who are on earth have been enraptured, to enable me to gaze on the Day-Star of Thy Beauty, and to supply me with the wine of Thine utterance. Thou seest me, O my God, clinging to Thy name, the Most Holy, the Most Luminous, the Most Mighty, the Most Great, the Most Exalted, the Most Glorious, and holding fast to the hem of the robe to which have clung all in this world and in the world to come.

I beseech Thee, O my God, by the Tabernacle of Thy majesty upon the loftiest summits, and the Canopy of Thy Revelation on the highest hills, to graciously aid me to do what Thy will hath desired and Thy purpose hath manifested. Thou seest me, O my God, clinging to Thy name, the Most Holy, the Most Luminous, the Most Mighty, the Most Great, the Most Exalted, the Most Glorious, and holding fast to the hem of the robe to which have clung all in this world and in the world to come.

I beseech Thee, O my God, by Thy Beauty which shineth forth above the horizon of eternity, a Beauty before which, as soon as it revealeth itself, the kingdom of beauty boweth down in worship, magnifying it in ringing tones, to cause me to die to all that I possess and to live to everything that belongeth unto Thee. Thou seest me, O my God, clinging to Thy name, the Most Holy, the Most Luminous, the Most Mighty, the Most Great, the Most Exalted, the Most Glorious, and holding fast to the hem of the robe to which have clung all in this world and in the world to come.

I beseech Thee, O my God, by the Manifestation of Thy Name, the Well-Beloved, through Whom the hearts of Thy lovers have been consumed, and the souls of all that dwell on earth have soared aloft, to aid me to remember Thee amongst Thy creatures, and to extol Thee amidst Thy people. Thou seest me, O my God, clinging to Thy name, the Most Holy, the Most Luminous, the Most Mighty, the Most Great, the Most Exalted, the Most Glorious, and holding fast to the hem of the robe to which have clung all in this world and in the world to come.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Glory be to God, my God. Now is the period wherein Thou hast commanded all to observe the fast, through which their souls may be purified, and all attachment to aught besides Thee be relinquished. And that which is worthy of Thy heavenly courts may ascend from their hearts, and become worthy of the throne of the revelation of Thy unity. O my Lord, I beseech Thee that this fast may become a river of life-giving water, and bring the virtues Thou hast ordained for the fast. Cleanse, by this means, the hearts of Thy servants, who are such that neither the corruptions of this world, nor the clamour and uproar of them that have denied Thy signs, the most glorious tokens of Thy manifestation, Who art the bestower of dominion, power, majesty and glory, have been able to detain them from turning towards Thy Name, glorified be Thou. These servants have hastened towards Thy mercy, as soon as they heard Thy call, and have not been hindered by the changes and vicissitudes of this world, nor by human limitations.

O my God. I bear witness to Thy unity and acknowledge Thy oneness, and bow my head in humility before the revelation of Thy majesty, and gaze upon the effulgence of Thy transcendent glory. I have believed in Thee, and from the time that Thou didst make Thyself known unto me through Thy dominion and power, and didst reveal Thyself unto human eyes, I have believed in Thee. I have detached myself from all things, and clinging to the cords of Thy gifts and Thy favours, have turned my face towards Him, and believed in His truth, and in the truth of all the wondrous law and teachings that have been revealed unto Him. I have fasted for love of Thee, and in obedience to Thy command. And I have broken my fast with praise unto Thee, and in a manner that is pleasing unto Thee. O my Lord, I beseech Thee not to number me amongst them that have denied Thy truth, and doubted Thy signs, and disbelieved Thy testimony, and distorted Thy sayings, whilst I have fasted by day and prostrated before Thee by night.

O my Lord, open mine eyes and the eyes of all who seek Thee, that they may recognise Thee, as Thou hast commanded us in Thy holy Book, wherein Thou hast said: "And say, O ye my servants! Verily, I am God, besides Whom there is none other God. I am the Preserver, the All-Bountiful, the Merciful." This is the Book which hath been sent down by Thee, unto Him Whom Thou hast chosen from amongst all creation, and hast singled out for Thy favour, and made the bearer of Thy message unto Thy people. Thou hast, in Thy grace, enabled us to recognise Him, and to accept all that hath been sent down by Him, and to attain unto the presence of Him Whom Thou hast promised in Thy Book and Thy Epistles. Therefore, praise be unto Thee, O my God.

O my God. As I have gazed upon Thee, and have held fast to the cords of Thy providence and Thy forbearance, and clung to the hem of Thy love and Thy bounteous favour, I beseech Thee not to disappoint this my hope, and to cast me out from the precincts of Thy court and the sanctuary of Thy presence, to obtain that which Thou hast ordained for them that have fasted for love of Thee. O my God. I confess that whatsoever proceedeth from me is unworthy of Thy dominion, and falleth short of Thy majesty. Nevertheless, I beg of Thee, by Thy Name that hath revealed Thee unto all creation, and by the glory of Thy most excellent title, and by the effulgence of Thy beauty that hath dawned through Thy Name, to cause me to drink of the sweet wine of Thy mercy, and the pure beverage of Thy favours, that I may turn my gaze towards Thee, and detach myself from all else, and regard this world and all that hath been created therein as naught, and unworthy of a passing day.
O my God. I further ask Thee to send down upon us the rain of Thy forgiveness from the cloud of Thy mercy, and the dew of Thy favours from the heaven of Thy good-pleasure. Verily, Thou art the All-Powerful, the Most Glorious, the Most Merciful.
O my Lord, turn not away from them that turn towards Thee, nor drive from Thy courts those that have drawn nigh unto Thee. Dash not the hopes of them that, with uplifted hands, have sought Thy favours, nor be heedless of Thy faithful servants.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Praise be to Thee, O Lord my God! I beseech Thee by this Revelation whereby darkness hath been turned into light, through which the Frequented Fane hath been built, and the Written Tablet revealed, and the Outspread Roll uncovered, to send down upon me and upon them who are in my company that which will enable us to soar into the heavens of Thy transcendent glory, and will wash us from the stain of such doubts as have hindered the suspicious from entering into the tabernacle of Thy unity.

I am the one, O my Lord, who hath held fast the cord of Thy loving-kindness, and clung to the hem of Thy mercy and favors. Do Thou ordain for me and for my loved ones the good of this world and of the world to come. Supply them, then, with the Hidden Gift Thou didst ordain for the choicest among Thy creatures.

These are, O my Lord, the days in which Thou hast bidden Thy servants to observe the fast. Blessed is he that observeth the fast wholly for Thy sake and with absolute detachment from all things except Thee. Assist me and assist them, O my Lord, to obey Thee and to keep Thy precepts. Thou, verily, hast power to do what Thou choosest.

There is no God but Thee, the All-Knowing, the All-Wise. All praise be to God, the Lord of all worlds.
prayers.name: FASTING (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O my God, my God! Praise be unto Thee for Thou hast set aflame the fire of divine love in the Sacred Tree upon the summit of the Most Lofty Mount: that Tree which is “neither of the East nor of the West” , that fire which blazed until its flames ascended unto the Celestial Host, and from it these beings perceived the guiding light and cried: “Verily, we have beheld a fire upon the Mount of Sinai.”
O my God, my God! Magnify from day to day this

fire until its voice stirreth the whole earth. O Thou, my Lord! Kindle the light of Thy love in every heart, breathe into the souls of men the spirit of Thy knowledge, gladden their breasts with the verses of Thy unity. Quicken those who dwell within their tombs, admonish the proud, spread abroad the tidings of Thy gladness throughout the world, send down Thy crystal waters, and in the presence of the Manifest Splendour, give unto all the cup that is “fined from the camphor fountain”.
Verily, Thou art the Bestower, the Forgiver, the All-Giving. Verily, Thou art the Gracious, the Compassionate.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Praise be to Thee, O Lord, my God! Disregard not him whom Thou hast raised up through the power of Thy changeless sovereignty, nor cast away him whom Thou hast caused to enter the seat of Thy eternal worship, nor distance him whom Thou hast caused to approach Thee. O my God, wilt Thou abandon those who have taken refuge in Thee from Thy dominion? O my desire, wilt Thou keep afar from Thee him whom Thou hast made a shelter? Wilt Thou abase him whom Thou hast exalted, or forget those whom Thou hast enabled to remember Thee?

Praise be to Thee, O God, and exalted be Thy praise! Thou art, from everlasting, the King of all creation and its Prime Mover, and Thou wilt remain, for all eternity, the immortal Lord and Ordainer of all things Thou hast created. Praised be Thou, O my God! If Thou ceasest to show compassion to Thy servants, who then will show them mercy, and if Thou withholdest help from Thy beloved ones, who then will aid them?

Praised art Thou, and exalted beyond measure! In Thy truth, Thou art the Adored, and verily we all worship Thee; in Thy justice, Thou art made manifest, and verily we all bear witness to Thee. Truly, in Thy grace, Thou art the Beloved. There is no God but Thee, the Helper in peril, the Self-Subsisting.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
**Searching for translation**

I am performing a Google web search to find an English translation of the Bahá'í prayer "Protección" from Spanish. My aim is to find a translation that accurately reflects the original text, including the section header and paragraph breaks.

**Performing Translation**

I am using a web search to find an existing English translation of the Bahá'í prayer "Protección" from Spanish. I will ensure the section header is translated and paragraph breaks are preserved exactly as in the original.

**Translating the prayer**

I am now executing a search to find an English translation of the Bahá'í prayer "Protección." My priority is to accurately translate the text, including the section header and preserve all original paragraph breaks.

I found a prayer titled "The Prayer for Protection (The 'Bearer of this Tablet')" which has a Spanish and English version. The provided Spanish text in the prompt matches the beginning of the Spanish text found in the search results for this specific prayer. Therefore, I will provide the English translation for this prayer.

## Protection

O Thou Kind and Loving Providence! The East is astir and the West is agitated like the eternal waves of the sea. The gentle breezes of holiness are blowing, and from the Invisible Realm, the resplendent rays of the Sun of Truth are shining. The hymns of divine unity are chanted, and the banners of celestial power are unfurled. The angelic Voice is heard, which, like the roaring of the Leviathan, sends forth the call to selflessness and annihilation. The triumphant call of Yá Bahá’u’l-Abhá resounds everywhere, and in all regions the summons of Yá Alíyyu’l-A‘lá is heard. There is no stir in the world save that of the Glory of the Unique Conqueror of Hearts, nor agitation save that of the surging waves of the love of Him Who is the Incomparable, the Beloved.

The Loved Ones of the Lord, with musky breaths, burn like luminous candles in all countries, and the Friends of the All-Merciful are found in all regions, like blooming flowers. They rest not for a moment; they breathe not without uttering Thy remembrance, and they yearn for naught but to serve Thy Cause. In the meadows of truth they are like nightingales of sweet song, and in the garden of guidance, like flowers of vibrant hue. With mystical blossoms they adorn the pathways of the Garden of Reality; like undulating cypresses, they flank the shores of the Divine Will. On the horizon of existence they shine like brilliant stars; in the firmament of the world they gleam like radiant constellations. They are manifestations of celestial grace and dawns of the light of divine aid.

O Loving Lord! Grant that all may remain firm and steadfast, and shine with everlasting radiance, so that, with every breath, gentle breezes may diffuse from the pergolas of Thy loving-kindness, that from the ocean of Thy grace a mist may arise, the bounteous rains of Thy love may bestow refreshment, and the zephyr may shed its perfume from the rose-garden of divine unity.

O Beloved of the World! Send forth a ray of Thy Effulgence. O Beloved of
prayers.name: Protección (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Thou art glorified, O Lord, my God! By Thy name, the Opener of the Way, I beg Thee to keep us safe from the malevolence of Thine adversaries who deny Thy covenant and dispute Thy beauty.
By Thy name, the Conqueror, subdue those who have wronged Thy former Manifestation who now appeareth invested with Thy title, the Most Glorious.
By Thy name, the Chastiser, seize those who have treated Thy Cause with contempt, derided Thy mighty words, and are unable to attain unto this most exalted station.
By Thy name, the Victorious, enable Thy loved ones to triumph over Thine enemies and over the infidels among Thy creatures.
By Thy name, the Divider of the Heart, tear away the veil that concealeth the deeds of those who have dishonoured Thee and undermined Thy faith among Thy people.
By Thy name, the Saviour, heal the broken hearts of those who love Thee and vouchsafe to bless them in their undertakings.
By Thy name, the All-Knowing, teach them the wonders of Thy wisdom, that they may cleave steadfastly unto Thy faith and walk in the paths of Thy good-pleasure.
By Thy name, the Protector, protect them from the tyranny of the oppressor, the perfidy of the wicked, and the malice of the troublemakers.
By Thy name, the Protector, shelter them within the fortress of Thy might and power, that they may be safe from the arrows of doubt shot by those who rebel against Thee.
By Thy name, blessed above all names, the Object of Thy favour and the Revealer of Thy beauty, hallow for Thy servants these days that the pen of Thy decree hath clearly mentioned and that are predetermined in Thy irrevocable Tablet, according to Thy will and Thy wisdom.
And by Thy name, the Conqueror, subject the inhabitants of Thy kingdom to Thy law, that all may turn their faces toward Thee and relinquish all in their love for Thee, and for Thy good-pleasure.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O my God! Thou seest the Sun of Thy word shining upon the horizon of Thy prison-city, for within its walls the Manifestation of Thy being, the Dawn of the light of Thy unity, is raising its voice to praise Thee.
And the sweet fragances of Thy love are spreading over Thy cities and enveloping all the inhabitants of Thy kingdom.
O my God! Since Thou dost reveal Thy grace, suffer not Thy servants to turn away their gaze from it. O my God! Look not upon their state, nor their preoccupations, nor their deeds, but look upon the greatness of Thy glory and the abundance of Thy gifts, the power of Thy might and the excellence of Thy favours. I swear by Thy glory! If Thou shouldst regard them with the eye of justice, all would deserve Thy wrath and the rod of Thy anger. O my God! Hold Thy creatures in the hand of Thy grace and make known unto them that which is best for them in all that Thou hast created in the kingdom of Thy creation.
We testify, O my God, that Thou art God and that there is none other God but Thee. Thou hast existed from all eternity, without equal, without rival, and Thou remainest for ever the same. By the eyes which behold Thee, seated upon the throne of unity, the seat of singleness, I beseech Thee. Aid by Thy Most Great Name all those who love Thee and exalt them to such heights that they may testify, with all their being and with their utterance, that Thou art God alone, the Incomparable, the Unique, the Eternal. Thou art, at all times, without peer or equal. Thou art, in truth, the Most Glorious, the Almighty, Whose succour all men implore.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
In His Name, the Most Glorious, the Most High, the Most Lofty!

Praised be Thou! O Thou, who art my God, my Master and my Lord, my Sustainer and my Hope, my Refuge and my Light. I implore Thee by Thy hidden, precious Name, known unto none but Thee: Protect the bearer of this Tablet from every calamity and plague, from every wicked person, whether man or woman, from the evil of the evildoers and the stratagems of the ungodly. Preserve him also, O my God, from all pain and suffering, O Thou in Whose hand is the dominion of all things. Verily, Thy power is all-embracing. Thou doest whatsoever Thou wilst, and ordainest whatsoever Thou pleasest.

O Thou King of Kings! O Thou Gracious Lord! O Thou Source of eternal bounty, grace, generosity and bestowal! O Thou Healer! O Thou Sufficient! O Thou Essence of light! O Thou Light above all light! O Thou Revealer of every manifestation! O Thou Compassionate! O Thou Merciful! Have mercy upon the bearer of this Tablet through Thy greatest mercy and Thy superabundant grace, O Thou Bountiful, O Thou Generous. Protect him also through Thy safeguarding from all that his heart and mind abhor. Thou art, verily, the Most Powerful of the Powerful.

May the glory of God rest upon thee, O thou dawning sun! Testify unto that which God hath testified of Himself: There is none other God beside Him, the Almighty, the Most Beloved.
prayers.name: Schutz (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Magnified art thou, O Lord, my God! By thy name which thou hast set above all names, which hath rent the heavens and caused the Sun of thy beauty to shine from the horizon with the radiance of thy name, the Supreme, the Most High, I ask thee to grant me the succour of thy wondrous help, to shelter me beneath the wing of thy solicitude and protection.
I am one of thy handmaidens, O my Lord! Unto thee I have turned, in thee I have placed my trust. Confirm my love for thee and strengthen me to do thy good pleasure, to such a degree that neither the defection of the faithless among thy people nor the clamour of the hypocrites among thy creatures may keep me back from thee.
O my Lord, purify my ear that I may hear the verses sent down unto thee, illumine my heart with the light of thy knowledge, and loosen my tongue that it may mention thee and chant thy praise. O my God, by thy power, my soul hath cleaved unto none other than thee, my heart seeketh none but thee alone.
There is none other God than thee, the Most Glorious, the Most Bountiful, the Ever-Forgiving, the Most Merciful.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
I Guds navn, den overveldende storhets Herre, den altbetvingende.

Helliget være Herren som har kilden til herredømme i sin hånd. Han skaper hva han enn vil ved sitt befalingsord "bli" -og det blir. Hos ham har all øvrighetsmakt ligget til nå, og hos ham skal den forbli heretter. Han gjør seierrik hvem han enn vil, gjennom sin befalings kraft. Han er i sannhet den sterke, den allmektige. Ham tilhører all herlighet og velde i åpenbaringens og skapningens riker og i hva som enn ligger mellom dem. Sannelig, han er den mektige, den overmåte herlige. Fra evighet har han vært kilden til ubetvingelig styrke, og det skal han til evighet forbli. Han er sannelig maktens og kraftens Herre. Alle himmelens og jordens riker og hva som enn er mellom dem tilhører Gud, og hans kraft er alle ting overlegen. Alle skatter på jorden og i himmelen og i alt mellom dem tilhører ham, og hans beskyttelse omfatter alle ting. Han er skaperen av himmelen og jorden og hva som enn ligger mellom dem, og han står i sannhet som vitne til alle ting. Han er dommens Herre for alle som bor i himmelen og på jorden og i hva som enn ligger mellom dem, og Gud er sannelig snar i sin dom. Han fastsetter det mål som er tildelt alle som er i himmelen og på jorden og i hva som enn ligger mellom dem. Sannelig, han er den høyeste beskytter. Han holder i sitt grep nøklene til himmelen og jorden og til alt mellom dem. Etter eget behag skjenker han gaver, gjennom sin befalings kraft. I sannhet omfatter hans nåde alt, og han er den allvitende.

Si: Gud er meg nok; han er den som i sitt grep holder altets rike. Gjennom kraften hos sine hærskarer i himmelen og på jorden og i hva som enn ligger mellom dem beskytter han hvem han enn vil blant sine tjenere.

Umåtelig opphøyet er du, o Herre! Beskytt oss mot det som ligger foran oss og bak oss, over våre hoder, til høyre, til venstre, under våre føtter og på enhver annen side av oss. Sannelig, din beskyttelse av alle ting svikter aldri. [1]
prayers.name: (LLM translated)
