Thou seest me clinging to the hem of the sacred garment of Thy most holy, most pure, most mighty, most great, most exalted, most glorious Name, and holding fast to that which hath been the support of all them that believe in Thee in this world and in the world to come.

  

  My God! By the Effulgent One of Thy Name, the Beloved, through Whom the hearts of them that love Thee are consumed, and the souls of all creation are borne aloft, I beg Thee to enable me to remember Thee amongst Thy creatures, and to praise Thee amidst Thy servants. My God! Thou seest me clinging to the hem of the sacred garment of Thy most holy, most pure, most mighty, most great, most exalted, most glorious Name, and holding fast to that which hath been the support of all them that believe in Thee in this world and in the world to come.

  

  My God! By the rustling of the leaves of Thy Tree of holiness, and the murmuring of the breeze of the kingdom of Thy Name, I beg Thee to remove me from all that Thou hatest, and to elevate me unto the source of the effulgence of the dawning-light of Thy signs. My God! Thou seest me clinging to the hem of the sacred garment of Thy most holy, most pure, most mighty, most great, most exalted, most glorious Name, and holding fast to that which hath been the support of all them that believe in Thee in this world and in the world to come. My God! By the utterance of the Tongue of Thy divine decree, through which the letters have been sent forth, the winds have been stirred, the seas have been stirred, the fruits have appeared, the trees have grown, the traces of the past have been obliterated, the veils of separation have been rent asunder, and they that are loyal unto Thee have hastened unto the light of the dawning-radiance of Thy countenance, I beg Thee to suffer me to drink from the hidden wells of Thy knowledge, and to taste the treasures of Thy wisdom. My God! Thou seest me clinging to the hem of the sacred garment of Thy most holy, most pure, most mighty, most great, most exalted, most glorious Name, and holding fast to that which hath been the support of all them that believe in Thee in this world and in the world to come.

  

  My God! By the fire of Thy love which hath roused the sleeplessness of Thy chosen and Thy loved ones, and by their remembrance of Thee and their praise of Thee at the dawn of day, I beg Thee to number me amongst them that have attained unto what hath been revealed in Thy Books and what hath been purposed by Thy Will. My God! Thou seest me clinging to the hem of the sacred garment of Thy most holy, most pure, most mighty, most great, most exalted, most glorious Name, and holding fast to that which hath been the support of all them that believe in Thee in this world and in the world to come.

  

  My God! By the light of Thy countenance, which hath driven them that draw nigh unto Thee to grasp the lances of Thy commandments, and which hath made them that consecrate their lives to Thy Cause to face the swords of Thy enemies, I beg Thee to inscribe for me, by the Pen of Thy transcendent grace, that which Thou hast recorded for Thy faithful and Thy chosen. My God! Thou seest me clinging to the hem of the sacred garment of Thy most holy, most pure, most mighty, most great, most exalted, most glorious Name, and holding fast to that which hath been the support of all them that believe in Thee in this world and in the world to come.

  

  My God! By Thy Name, through which Thou hearest the call of them that yearn for Thee, and the sighs of them that long for Thee, and the tears of them that are drawn unto Thee, and the lamentations of them that are sacrificed for Thee, and by which Thou hast fulfilled the hopes of all that have sought Thee, and answered their supplications with Thy favour and Thy grace, and by which the ocean of forgiveness hath surged before the Throne of Thy holiness, and the clouds of favour have rained upon Thy servants, I beg Thee to inscribe for all that have turned towards Thee and observed the Fast, the reward that hath been ordained for them that have been restrained from speaking save by Thy leave, and for them that have relinquished all things for love of Thee on Thy sacred Path.

  

  My Lord! By Thyself, Thy signs, Thy clear tokens, the radiance of the sun of Thy beauty, and Thy sacred branches, I beg Thee to forgive the transgressions of them that have observed Thy laws and performed what hath been ordained in Thy Tablets. My God! Thou seest me clinging to the hem of the sacred garment of Thy most holy, most pure, most mighty, most great, most exalted, most glorious Name, and holding fast to that which hath been the support of all them that believe in Thee in this world and in the world to come.
prayers.name: 齋戒 (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
1. Opening Prayer for the Spiritual Assembly

O God, my God! We are servants of Thine that have turned with devotion to Thy Holy Face, that have detached ourselves from all besides Thee in this glorious Day. We have gathered in this Spiritual Assembly, united in our views and thoughts, with our purposes harmonized to exalt Thy Word amidst mankind. O Lord, our God! Make us the signs of Thy Divine Guidance, the Standards of Thine exalted Faith amongst men, servants to Thy mighty Covenant, O Thou our Lord Most High, manifestations of Thy Divine Unity in Thine Abhá Kingdom, and resplendent stars shining upon all regions. Lord! Aid us to become seas surging with the billows of Thy wondrous grace, streams flowing from Thine all-glorious heights, goodly fruits upon the Tree of Thine heavenly Cause, trees waving through the breezes of Thy celestial bounty in Thy divine vineyard. O God! Make our souls dependent upon the Verses of Thy Divine Unity, our hearts cheered with the outpourings of Thy Grace, that we may unite even as the waves of one sea and become merged together as the rays of Thine effulgent Light; that our thoughts, our views, our feelings may become as one reality, manifesting the spirit of union throughout the world. Thou art the Gracious, the Bountiful, the Bestower, the Almighty, the Merciful, the Compassionate.

2. Closing Prayer for the Spiritual Assembly

O God! O God! From the unseen kingdom of Thy oneness behold us assembled in this spiritual meeting, believing in Thee, confident in Thy signs, firm in Thy Covenant and Testament, attracted to Thee, set aglow with the fire of Thy love and sincere in Thy Cause. We are servants in Thy vineyard, spreaders of Thy religion, devoted worshipers of Thy countenance, humble towards Thy loved ones, submissive before Thy door, and imploring Thee to confirm us in serving Thy chosen ones, to support us with Thine unseen hosts, to strengthen our loins in Thy servitude and to make us submissive and adoring subjects communing with Thee. O our Lord! We are weak, and Thou art the Mighty, the Powerful. We are lifeless, and Thou art the great life-giving Spirit. We are needy, and Thou art the Sustainer, the Powerful. O our Lord! Turn our faces unto Thy merciful countenance, feed us from Thy heavenly table with Thine abundant grace, assist us with the hosts of Thy supreme angels and confirm us through the holy ones of the Kingdom of Abhá. Verily, Thou art the Generous, the Merciful. Thou art the Possessor of great bounty, and, verily, Thou art the Clement and the Gracious.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
AYYÁM-I-HÁ

(Days of Intercalation)

(Days of Intercalation... must be days of preparation for Fasting, days of doing good deeds, of charity and of giving gifts.)

My God, my Fire and my Light! The days Thou hast named Ayyám-i-Há in Thy Book have begun, O Thou King of Names, and the fast which Thy Most Great Pen hath prescribed unto all who are in Thy dominions hath approached. I implore Thee, O my God, by these days and by all who, throughout the ages, have held fast to the hem of Thy commands, and observed Thy ordinances, that Thou mayest grant unto every soul a place within the precincts of Thy court, and a seat upon the manifestation of the effulgence of Thy countenance.

These are Thy servants, O my God, whom no corrupt inclination hath debarred from what Thou hast sent down in Thy Book. They have submitted themselves to Thy Faith, and accepted Thy Book with a steadfastness proceeding from Thee, and have performed what Thou has commanded them, and have chosen to follow what Thou hast revealed.

Thou seest, O my God, how they have recognized and confessed whatsoever Thou hast revealed in Thy Writings. Quench Thou their thirst, O my God, with the waters of eternal life from the hands of Thy tender mercy, O my God. Then write down for them the reward decreed unto him who hath plunged himself into the ocean of Thy presence and attained the choicest wine of the meeting with Thee.

I implore Thee, O Thou King of Kings and Lover of the oppressed, to ordain for them the good of this world and of the next. Furthermore, write down for them that which no creature of Thine hath ever found, and cause them to be numbered among them that have circled round Thee, and that move about Thy throne in every
prayers.name: AYYÁM-I-HÁ (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Recitation for Morning, Noon, and Evening
Whosoever desireth to supplicate, let him first wash his hands, and then recite:
O my God! Strengthen my hands that they may firmly grasp Thy Book, and withdraw them from all that is not Thine. Verily, Thou art the Almighty, the All-Bountiful.
Wash thy face, and recite:
O my Lord! I have turned my face towards Thee, illuminate it with the light of Thy countenance and protect it from the defilement of turning towards any other than Thee.
Then, let the suppliant arise and face the Qiblih (the point of adoration, that is, the House of Worship), and recite:
God beareth witness that there is none other God but He. His are the kingdoms of revelation and creation. Verily, He hath revealed Himself – the Source of revelation, He Who communed with God on Sinai, and Whose radiance hath illumined the horizons of the highest elevation, and the Tree of Utterance hath spoken in the realms of both heaven and earth, proclaiming unto all: 'Behold, the All-Possessing is come! He ruleth the heavens and the earth, the glory and the dominion. He is the Lord of all peoples, the Possessor of the Throne, both of heaven and earth!'
The suppliant shall bow down, place his hands on his knees, and then recite:
Exalted art Thou, O my God, beyond all praise, and above all description. None among Thy creation can sing Thy praise, nor can any one of them describe what Thou art.
The suppliant shall then arise, and opening his hands with palms upwards, let them fall upon his face, and recite:
O my God! Let him not be disappointed, who hath fixed his gaze upon the hem of Thy mercy. Thou art the Most Merciful of the merciful!
The suppliant shall then be seated, and recite:
I bear witness that Thou art God, and that Thou hast no partner. Thou hast revealed the Divine Word, and fulfilled Thy Covenant, and opened the door of Thy grace unto all who are in heaven and on earth. Entrust, O my God, those whom Thou hast drawn unto Thee, to the care of Thy loving-kindness. Though the world and all that is therein may change, yet shall they remain unchanged in their devotion to Thee. Verily, Thou art the Ever-Forgiving, the All-Bountiful.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
He is God!
O God, I am an innocent child. Protect me in Thy shelter, bestow Thy favor and grant Thy mercy. Educate me and nourish me with Thy bounty. Grant me capacity so that I may become heavenly, divine, spiritual, and illumined. Thou art the Mighty, the Powerful, and the Kind.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text: O God! Assist this handmaid of Thy Kingdom to be glorified in both worlds; cause her to turn away from the dust of this fleeting world and from those who have set their hearts upon it, and enable her to commune with and draw nigh unto the eternal world. Bestow upon her heavenly power and strengthen her through the breath of the Holy Spirit, that she may arise to serve Thee. Thou art the Almighty.
prayers.name: WOMAN (LLM translated)

prayers.text: O Divine Legislator! We are deserving of mercy, grant us Thy succor; we are homeless wanderers, bestow upon us Thy refuge; we are scattered, unite us; we are strayed travellers, take us into Thy shelter; we are deprived, grant us a portion; we are thirsty, show us the path to the river of life; we are weak, make us strong, that we may arise in the service of Thy Cause and lay down our lives in the path of Thy guidance.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Glory be unto Thee, O my God! Verily, this Thy servant and this Thy maidservant have gathered under the shadow of Thy mercy and they are united through Thy favor and generosity. O Lord! Assist them in this Thy world and Thy kingdom and destine for them every good through Thy bounty and grace. O Lord! Confirm them in Thy servitude and assist them in Thy service. Suffer them to become the signs of Thy Name in Thy world and protect them through Thy bestowals which are inexhaustible in this world and the world to come. O Lord! They are supplicating the kingdom of Thy mercifulness and invoking the realm of Thy singleness. Verily, they are married in obedience to Thy command. Cause them to become the signs of harmony and unity until the end of time.

Verily, Thou art the Omnipotent, the Omnipresent and the Almighty!
prayers.name: Marriage (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
I found an English translation of the prayer. I will now format it according to your requirements.

## Ayyám-i-Há

My God, my Fire and my Light! The days which Thou hast named the Ayyám-i-Há in Thy Book have begun, O Thou Who art the King of names, and the fast which Thy most exalted Pen hath enjoined unto all who are in the kingdom of Thy creation is approaching.

I entreat Thee, O my Lord, by these days and by all such as have during this period clung to the cord of Thy commandments, and laid hold on the handle of Thy precepts, to grant that unto every soul may be assigned a place within the precincts of Thy court, and a seat at the revelation of the splendors of the light of Thy countenance.

These are Thy servants, O my Lord, whom no corrupt inclination hath kept back from what Thou hast sent down in Thy Book. They have bowed down before Thy Cause, and received Thy Book with such resolve as is born of Thee, and observed what Thou hast prescribed unto them, and chosen to follow that which hath been sent down by Thee.

Thou seest, O my Lord, how they have recognized and confessed whatsoever Thou hast revealed in Thy Scriptures. Give them to drink, O my Lord, from the hands of Thy graciousness the waters of Thine eternity. Write down, then, for them the recompense ordained for him that hath immersed himself in the ocean of Thy presence, and attained unto the choice wine of Thy meeting.

I implore Thee, O Thou the King of kings and the Pitier of the downtrodden, to ordain for them the good of this world and of the next. Write down for them, moreover, what none of Thy creatures hath discovered, and number them with those who have circled round Thee, and who move about Thy throne in every world of Thy worlds.

Thou, truly, art the Almighty, the All-Knowing, the All-Informed.
prayers.name: Ayyám-i-Há (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Alláh'u'Abhá!
O my pure Lord and merciful God! Grant such power that we may contend with all the diverse obstacles of the earth, and bestow such might that we may, like the boundless ocean, surge upon the shores of east and west.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
China! China! China! Go to China! The Cause of Baháʼu'lláh must reach China. Where are the holy Bahá'ís who go to China to teach? China has the greatest potential. The hearts of the Chinese people are most pure and most eager to seek truth. To teach the Chinese, one must first possess their spirit, study their scriptures, understand their customs, and speak to them from their perspective, in their language. The teacher must always remember the spiritual well-being of the Chinese people and have no selfish motives. In China, one person can teach many, can nurture outstanding individuals with divine character, who can become lamps to illumine the world of humanity. I verily say, the Chinese people do not love any form of deceit and hypocrisy; they are inspired by noble ideals. Regrettably, my physical condition does not permit, otherwise I would personally go to China.
China is a country of the future. I hope that suitable teachers will be inspired to go to that vast country, lay the foundation of the Kingdom of God, promote the essence of divine civilization, and raise the banner of the Cause of Baháʼu'lláh to invite people to God's sacred feast.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O Lord, for them that are near unto Thee, Thy trials are the healing remedies, and for all who love Thee Thy sword the object of desire, and for them that yearn for Thee Thy lances their cherished hope, and for them that know Thy truth Thy command the sole hope!

By Thy holy sweetness, and the radiance of the glory of Thy countenance, I implore Thee, send down upon us from Thy holy habitation which is in the highest heavens that which will draw us nigh unto Thee.

O my God, make firm our feet within Thy Cause, illuminate our hearts with the light of Thy knowledge, and brighten our breasts with the radiance of Thy name.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Prayer for the Departed

Glory be to Thee, O my God! Thou, by the power of Thine eternal sovereignty, dost not abase them whom Thou hast exalted, nor, in the eternal pavilion of Thy glory, dost Thou remove far from Thee them whom Thou hast called unto Thee. O my God! Wilt Thou forsake them whom Thou hast sheltered under the wings of Thy dominion? O Thou, my heart's desire! Wilt Thou banish them whom Thou hast hidden in the tabernacle of Thy grace? Wilt Thou cast down them whom Thou hast raised up unto Thee? And wilt Thou forget them that have remembered Thee?

Glory be to Thee, O Thou, the Ancient of Days! Thou hast been the King of all creation from the beginning, and shalt be its eternal Lord, its first Cause and its final End. Glory be to Thee, O my God! If Thou withholdest Thy favours from Thy servants, who shall bestow mercy upon them? If Thou refusest to succour them that love Thee, who shall come to their aid?

Glory be to Thee, O Thou, the Most Glorious! Thou art adored for Thy Truth. Verily, all of us adore Thee. Thou art manifest in Thine own justice. Verily, all of us bear witness unto Thee. Indeed, Thou art the Object of adoration by reason of Thine abundant grace. There is none other God but Thee, the Help in the midst of all distress, the Self-Subsistent.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O God! O God! Guide me, protect me, illumine the lamp of my heart and make me a brilliant star. Thou art the Mighty and the Powerful.

O God, protect me with Thy power, and guide me to Thy path. And make my heart pure and steadfast in Thy love. Indeed Thou art. Thou art my Helper and my Protector. Thou art indeed the Light-Giver.
prayers.name: Prayer for Guidance (LLM translated)

prayers.text: Your Name is my healing, O my God, and remembrance of You my remedy. Nearness to You my hope, and love of You my companion. Your mercy unto me is my healing and my help in this world and the world to come, Thou indeed art the Bountiful, the All-knowing, the All-wise.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
u’lláh is, in truth, a great law. It behooveth all to offer this offering, for it is the source of grace, and of abundance, and of all good. It is a mercy that shall abide with every soul in each of the worlds of God, the Possessor of all, the Most Bountiful.

Bahá’u’lláh

Magnified be Thou, O Lord of all creation, unto Whom all things must turn. With my inner and outer tongue, I bear witness that Thou hast manifested and revealed Thyself, hast sent down Thy signs, and proclaimed Thy testimonies. I testify to Thy self-sufficiency from all that is not Thyself and to Thy holiness above all earthly things. I beseech Thee, by the transcendent glory of Thy Cause and the supreme potency of Thy Word, to grant confirmation unto such as desire to offer what Thou hast prescribed unto them in Thy Book and to observe that which spreadeth the fragrance of Thy acceptance. Verily, Thou art the Almighty, the Favoured, the All-forgiving, the Most Generous.
prayers.name: Ḥuqúqu’lláh (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
All glory, O my God, be unto Thee Who art the Source of all wondrous and exalted things, of grandeur and honour, of sovereignty and dominion, of dignity and grace, of awe and power. Whomsoever Thou pleasest, Thou canst draw into Thine Immeasurable Ocean, and unto whomsoever Thou desirest Thou canst confer the honour of recognizing Thy Most Ancient Name. None of those who dwell in the heavens and on earth can resist the operation of Thy supreme Will. From eternity Thou hast reigned over all created beings, and Thou wilt continue forever to exercise Thy sovereignty over all that exists. There is no God but Thee, the Almighty, the Most Exalted, the Most Powerful, the Most Wise.

Illumine with light, O Lord, the faces of Thy servants, that they may behold Thee; purify their hearts, that they may turn towards the court of Thy celestial bounty, and recognize Him Who is the Manifestation of Thy Self and the Dawning-Place of Thy Essence. Verily, Thou art the Lord of all worlds. There is no God but Thee, the Unconstrained, Whom all obey.
prayers.name: GLORY TO GOD (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
My apologies, it seems my investigation was interrupted. Here is the translation of the provided Chinese text to English, preserving paragraph breaks.

O Lord, my God! All glory be unto Thee. This is the day Thou hast ordained for all to fast, that their souls may be purified and freed from all attachment save Thee, and their hearts be uplifted unto Thy majestic sanctuary, to be worthy of the chariot of Thy unique revelation. O my Lord! Graciously ordain that this fast be a river of life, producing the virtues Thou hast bestowed. Purify therewith the hearts of Thy servants, whom no earthly evil hath hindered from turning unto Thy Name, the Most Glorious; and who, amidst the tumult and clamor of those who reject Thy Most Glorious Manifestation, have remained firm and unwavering. Thy Most Glorious Manifestation hath descended with Thy Revelation—a Revelation invested with Thy sovereignty, Thy power, Thy majesty, and Thy glory. These are Thy servants who, when summoned, hasten immediately unto the abode of Thy mercy, deterred by no earthly vicissitude nor the limitations of mankind.

O my God! I bear witness to Thy oneness and acknowledge Thy uniqueness; I humble myself before the revelations of Thy majesty and bow down before the effulgence of Thy transcendent glory. By the power of Thy sovereignty and might, Thou hast manifested Thyself before the eyes of men. In my knowledge of Thee, I have believed in Thee, and turning towards Thy Manifestation, I have detached myself from all else and clung to the cord of Thy kindness and grace. I have accepted His truth, and the wondrous laws and commandments He hath revealed. I fast for love of Thee and to fulfill Thy commands, and break my fast in praise of Thee and in submission to Thy will. O my Lord! Suffer me not to be numbered among those who, though they fast by day and pray by night before Thee, deny Thy truth, doubt Thy signs, reject Thy testimonies, and misinterpret Thy words.

O my Lord! Open Thou the eyes of mine and all who seek Thee, that we may recognize Thee with our own eyes. This is what Thou hast commanded us in Thy sacred Book, He Whom Thou hast chosen by Thy decree, singled out amongst all Thy creations, Whom Thou hast graciously invested with authority, Whom Thou hast made Thy beloved, to proclaim unto all people Thy Gospel. O my God! Praise be unto Thee, that Thou hast mercifully made us to know Him and to confirm all that Thou hast revealed unto Him. Thou hast enabled us to reach Thy Manifestation, promised in Thy Books and Thy Tablets.

O my God! Thou seest me turning towards Thee, firmly grasping the cord of Thy bounteous favours, and clinging to the hem of Thy compassionate robe. Thy servants have turned towards the heaven of Thy revelations and the presence of Thy majesty, and have observed the fast for love of Thee, Whom Thou hast destined to receive the favour they deserve. I beseech Thee, suffer not the hope of attaining this station to be turned into dust. O my God! I confess that all that proceedeth from me is unworthy of Thy majesty and Thy glory. Yet, by the glory of Thy Most Exalted Name, reveal Thyself in this revelation unto all, through Thy Holy Name, whereby Thy sanctity is revealed through Thy Most Beautiful Name. O God! By Thy Holy Name, suffer me to drink the sweetmeats of Thy mercy that flow from the right hand of Thy will, and the choice wine of Thy favour; and to be wholly absorbed in Thee, detached from all save Thee, so that the world and all that hath been created therein may appear before me as fleeting moments, which Thou hast disdained to create.

O my God! I beseech Thee, send down from the heaven of Thy will and the clouds of Thy mercy the rains that will cleanse us from the foulness of our sins. Thou art the Most Compassionate of the Merciful! Verily, Thou art the All-Powerful, the Most Glorious, the Most Kind.

O my Lord! Abandon Thou not him who turneth unto Thee; suffer not him who draweth nigh unto Thee to be banished from the court of Thy heavenly presence; let not the hopes of him who stretcheth out his hands to beg for the favour of Thy tender mercy be shattered; and suffer not Thy devout servants to be debarred from the mysteries of Thy loving-kindness and Thy tender mercies. Thou art the Forgiving, the Most Generous. O my Lord! Thou hast the power to do as Thou pleasest; all creatures are powerless before the manifestations of Thy might. All are poor before the revelation of Thy abundant wealth. All is as naught compared with the revelation of Thy supreme sovereignty. All strength hath vanished before the signs and tokens of Thy power; and I seek no refuge save in Thee. O my Lord! Where else can refuge be found? The power of Thy might is my proof! There is no protection save in Thee, no asylum save in Thee, no shelter save in Thee. O my Lord! Suffer me to taste the holy sweetmeats of remembering Thee and of praising Thee. I swear by Thy might that whosoever tasteth these sweetmeats shall be delivered from all earthly attachments and shall turn unto Thee, with no thought save for the remembrance of Thee.

O my God! Draw my soul by the remembrance of Thy beauty, that I may glorify Thy Holy Name. Suffer me not to be numbered among those who recite Thy Word, yet find not therein Thy hidden favours. Thy favours are enshrined within the Word Thou hast commanded, which reviveth all souls and inspirith the hearts of Thy servants. O my Lord! Suffer me to be numbered among such as, in Thy days, are stirred and quickened by the sweet savour of Thy remembrance, and who, in their longing for Thy beauty, would lay down their lives in the hope of attaining Thy presence. Should any one ask them: 'Whither are ye bound?' they will answer: 'To behold our Lord—the Possessor of all, the Remover of all distress, the Self-Subsistent!'

The errors committed by those who, in their pride, have strayed from Thee, have not hindered them from loving Thee, turning towards Thee, and seeking the ways of Thy mercy. They are blessed by the hosts of heaven, and praised by the dwellers of the eternal city. Thy Supreme Pen hath also inscribed upon their brows: 'These are the People of Bahá! Through them hath the effulgent light of guidance been spread.' This is what hath been ordained in Thy immutable Tablets, according to Thy Will and Thy command.

O my God, proclaim their greatness! Proclaim the sublimity of those who, in this and every life, cleave unto Thee, and bestow upon them what Thou hast bestowed upon Thy chosen ones. Thou art the All-Powerful, there is none other God beside Thee. Thou art the Almighty, the Remover of all distress, the All-Compelling, the Most Generous.

O my Lord! Suffer not our fast to end here, nor our past covenant to be brought to nought. Accept whatsoever we have done for love of Thee and to win Thy favour, and whatsoever we have left undone because we were hindered by the temptations of sin and corrupted desires. Let us hold fast to Thy love and to that which is pleasing unto Thee, and protect us from the evil of those who have rejected Thee and denied Thy most brilliant signs. Verily, Thou art the Lord of this world and of the world to come. There is none other God beside Thee. Thou art the Supreme, the Most Exalted.

O Lord, my God! Praise Him—He Who is the Primal Point, the Divine Mystery, the Unseen Essence, the Dawn of Divinity, the Manifestation of Sovereignty. Through Him have the knowledge of the past and the future been revealed, the pearl of hidden wisdom been brought to light, and the mystery of Thy precious Name been made known. The 'Creation' and 'Living' of the name Thou hast ordained to be proclaimed have been unified, and the might and power Thou hast decreed have been made manifest, and Thy commands have been sent down, and Thy laws have been made clear.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Evanescent is now everything that blossomed in the Paradise of Your transcendent unity, O my God! Where are the clouds from which the rain of Your mercy gushes forth? Drying are the branches of the tree of Your unity, losing the leaves of Your majesty and wisdom; where is the spring of Your gifts and generosity? Motionless is the Ark of Your Cause upon the sea of Your creation; where are the winds of Your grace and Your favors? Obscured on all sides is Your Lamp by the storms of discord blowing from all lands; where is the globe of Your benevolence and protection?
You see, O my Lord, how the eyes of these poor creatures are fixed on the horizon of Your riches, how the hearts of these destitute ones are turned in the direction of Your power. I implore You, O You who are the sole Desire of those who have recognized You and the object of adoration of all creation, now that You have drawn them by Your most exalted Word, do not keep them far from the Tabernacle that You have raised by Your Name, the All-Glorious.
They are full of tribulations, O my God, and surrounded by the wicked. Send down upon them, therefore, from the heaven of Your will, Your invisible hosts that, holding in their hands the ensigns of Your victory, they may help them in Your land and protect them against Your adversaries.
I supplicate You, O my God - by Your Name, through which the clouds emitted their rain, the rivers flowed and the fire of Your love was kindled in all Your dominion - help Your servant who has turned to You, expressing praise to You and resolved to serve You. Strengthen his heart, then, O my God, in Your love and in Your Faith. This is better for him than all that has been created on Your earth, for the world and all that is in it shall perish, but what belongs to You shall endure as long as Your exalted Names endure. By Your Glory! Were the world to remain as long as Your own kingdom, still, to dedicate his affection to it would be unworthy of those
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Glory be to Thee, O my God! How can Thy true lovers be recognized save through the trials which befall them in Thy path; and how can the rank of those who yearn ardently for Thee be revealed save through the tribulations they endure in their love for Thee? Thy power be my witness! All who adore Thee have their tears as their companion, and those who seek Thee are comforted by their own groans, and those who hasten unto Thee have the fragments of their broken hearts as their sustenance.
How sweet is the bitterness of death unto me in Thy path, and how highly do I prize the arrows of Thy enemies which I face for the sake of glorifying Thy word! Let me, in Thy Cause, O my God, drink with deep draughts all that Thou dost desire, and send me, in Thy love, all that Thou hast ordained. By Thy glory! I desire naught but what Thou wishest and cherish what Thou cherishest. In Thee have I placed all my hope and trust at all times.
I beseech Thee, O my God, raise up as helpers of this Revelation such as are accounted worthy of Thy Name and Thy sovereignty, that they may remember me among Thy creatures and lift up the banners of Thy blessing in Thy land.
Mighty art Thou to do as Thou pleasest. There is no God but Thee, the Help in the moment of need, the Self-Subsisting.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text: In every circumstance, be patient and place your complete trust and reliance upon God.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
This prayer, revealed by `Abdu'l-Bahá`, is read at His Shrine. It is also used as a personal prayer!

Whoever recites this prayer with humility and fervor will bring joy and gladness to the heart of this Servant; it will even be as if he met Him face to face.

He is the All-Glorious!

O God, my God! Humble and tearful, I lift up my hands supplicatingly to Thee and cover my face in the dust at that Threshold of Thine, which is exalted above the knowledge of the wise and above the praise of all who glorify Thee.

Graciously look upon Thy servant, humble and lowly at Thy gate, with the glance of Thine eye of mercy, and immerse him in the Ocean of Thine everlasting grace.

Lord! This is a poor and humble servant of Thine, enticed by Thee, supplicating Thee, captive in Thy hand, praying fervently to Thee, trusting in Thee, and calling on Thee and imploring Thee tearfully before Thy face, saying: O Lord, my God! Grant me Thy grace to serve Thy loved ones, strengthen me in my subservience to Thee, illuminate my brow with the light of worship in the court of Thy holiness and prayer to the Kingdom of Thy glory. Help me to be selfless at the heavenly entrance of Thy gate, and help me to be detached from all things within Thy holy precincts. Lord! Let me drink from the chalice of selflessness; clothe me with the robe of it, and immerse me in the ocean of it. Make me as dust in the path of Thy loved ones, and grant that I may sacrifice my soul for the earth that has been sanctified by the steps of Thy chosen ones in Thy path, O Lord of Loftiness.

With this prayer Thy servant calls on Thee, at dawn and at night. Fulfill the desire of his heart, O Lord! Illuminate his heart, gladden his breast, kindle his light, so that he may serve Thy Cause and Thy servants.

Thou art the Bestower, the
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Glory be to Thee, O God, for Thy manifestation of love to mankind! O Thou Who art our Life and Light, guide Thy servants in Thy way, and make us rich in Thee and free from all save Thee.

O God, teach us Thy Oneness and give us a realization of Thy Unity, that we may see no one save Thee. Thou art the Merciful and the Giver of bounty!

O God, create in the hearts of Thy beloved the fire of Thy love, that it may consume the thought of everything save Thee.

Reveal to us, O God, Thine exalted eternity—that Thou hast ever been and wilt ever be, and that there is no God save Thee. Verily, in Thee will we find comfort and strength.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
*The daily obligatory prayers revealed by Baháʼu'lláh are three in number. Every Baháʼí should choose one of these prayers and recite it daily as an obligation. The obligatory prayer should be recited according to the special instructions given with it.

*"In the context of obligatory prayers, 'morning', 'noon' and 'evening' mean the time from sunrise to noon, from noon to sunset and from sunset to two hours after sunset."

This prayer should be recited once every twenty-four hours, between noon and evening.

I bear witness, O my God, that Thou hast created me to know Thee and to worship Thee. I testify at this moment to my powerlessness and Thy power, to my poverty and Thy wealth. There is no other God but Thee, the Helper in peril, the Self-Subsisting.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
I apologize, it seems I made a mistake and called a non-existent tool. I should use the `generalist` tool for translation tasks.

Here is the corrected call:
I apologize again. It seems I am having trouble accessing the correct tool. The `generalist` tool is available, and my previous attempts incorrectly used `request` which is not a valid tool name in this context.

Here is the corrected call using the `generalist` tool:
O Thou, forgiving Lord: Thou art a dwelling place for all Thy servants. Thou knowest all secrets and Thou knowest everything. We are all helpless, and Thou art Strong, Almighty. We are all sinners, and Thou art the Forgiver of sins, Merciful, Compassionate. O Lord: Do not look at our transgressions. Deal with us with Thy mercy and Thy generosity. Countless are our sins, but boundless is the ocean of Thy forgiveness. Bitter are our weaknesses, but evident are the testimonies of Thy help and support. Therefore, strengthen and give us strength. Make us worthy to do what is worthy of Thy Holy threshold. Enlighten our hearts, give sight to our eyes, and tune our hearing. Raise the dead and heal the sick. Give riches to the poor and give rest and security to those who are gripped by fear. Receive us into Thy kingdom and enlighten us with the light of guidance. Thou art Most Almighty and Most Powerful. Thou art Generous, Thou art Merciful, Thou art Good.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O, my adoring God, all glory and majesty, all dominion and light, beauty and radiance belong to You. To whomever You will, You grant dominion. From whomever You will, You take it away. The Possessor of all, the Most High above all, there is no God but You. You bring into existence the entire universe and all souls within it from nothingness. There is nothing comparable to You – all besides You is unworthy before Your holy presence and the glory of Your Essence.

In Your great Book, You Yourself said: “Eyes cannot perceive Him, but He perceives eyes. He is the All-Perceiving, the All-Knowing.” I cannot praise Your perfections better than what You have said. May Your glory increase, O my God. Verily, no matter how keen the eye or sharp the intellect, it lacks the capacity to comprehend the nature of even Your humblest sign. Verily, You are the true God, there is no God beside You. Only You alone can manifest Your attributes; no praise from anyone besides You can reach Your holy court, and I bear witness that no one besides You can comprehend Your qualities.

Glory be to You – You are exalted beyond any description by anyone besides You, because the comprehension of humankind is insufficient to praise Your worthiness at a suitable level or to understand the deep nature of Your Essence. None of Your creatures besides You has the power to describe Your glory or fully apprehend it. I have come to know You, O my adoring God, because You Yourself have revealed Yourself to me; had You not revealed Yourself, I would never have known You. I worship You because You have called me; had it not been for Your call, I would not have been able to worship You. O my adoring God, You are glorious; my faults are heavy and my sins are great. What a shameful fate I face before Your holy throne. I have not known You to the extent You have revealed Yourself to me; I have not worshipped You with the devotion that befits Your call; I have not submitted to You at a level worthy of You, because I have not walked in the path of Your love in the manner You have inspired.
prayers.name: АЛКОО ЖАНА ЫРААЗЫЧЫЛЫК (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
I found a translation of the Baháʼí prayer "祈求護佑" which translates to "Supplication for Protection". The search results provide translations for several prayers under this heading. Based on the provided Chinese text, it appears to be the prayer by Bahá'u'lláh.

## Supplication for Protection

My Lord! Ordain for me and for those who believe in Thee in the Mother of Books all that Thou hast decreed as a succor for us, for in Thy grasp is the determination of all things.

Thy gifts are continually showered upon those who cherish Thee, and the wondrous tokens of Thy heavenly bounty are abundantly bestowed upon those who apprehend and accept Thy divine unity. We commit unto Thee the guardianship of all our affairs and beg of Thee all good, whether manifest or hidden, that is encompassed within Thy knowledge.

My Lord! Protect me from all evil that is perceived by Thine all-seeing eye, for naught hath power or might save through Thee. No triumph can come from any source save from Thy presence, and Thou alone art supreme. Whatsoever God willeth, that hath come into existence; and whatsoever He doth not will, shall not be.

Naught hath power or might save through God, He is the Most High, the Almighty.
prayers.name: 祈求護佑 (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Ô my Lord, since Thou holdest the measure of all things, ordain for me and for those who believe in Thee what Thou deemest best for us, according to what the Mother-Book prescribeth.
Thy bounteous favours descend ceaselessly upon those who cherish Thy love. They who recognize Thy divine unity receive abundantly the wondrous signs of Thy celestial bounty. All that Thou destinest for us is in Thy hands, we implore Thee to grant us all the good that is within Thy knowledge.
O my Lord, protect me from all the ills perceived by Thy omniscience, for there is no power nor strength save in Thee, nor is any victory possible save from Thy presence; to Thee alone appertaineth command. All that God willeth is accomplished, all that He willeth not is not accomplished.
There is no power, no force save in God, the Supreme, the Almighty.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O Thou the Beloved of my heart and my soul! No refuge have I save in Thee. When I lift up my voice at dawn, it is but in remembrance of Thee and in praise. Thy love encompasses me, and Thy grace is abundant. Thou art my hope.

O God, make me alive again and again, and bestow upon me ever the breath of the Holy Spirit, that I may remain steadfast in Thy love, experience true happiness, perceive the revealed light, and attain a state of perfect peace and submission.

Verily, Thou art the Giver, the Forgiving, the Compassionate.
prayers.name: Healing for Infants (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
26 Prayers of Abdul-Baha

O God of Grace and Compassion! O Thou, the Almighty! Though I am a weak, feeble, and insignificant servant, I have been nurtured in the shelter of Thy beauty and bounty, nourished by the milk of Thy mercy and compassion, and have grown up in the embrace of Thy tenderness and love. O my Lord! Though I am poor and in need, it is through Thy grace and bounty that all the poor and needy are made rich and abundant. But all the rich and prosperous who are distant and separated from Thy love and bounty are, in truth, poor and empty.

O Thou, the Divine Visionary! The power of tests and trials is immense, and the assault of suffering and hardship is exceedingly severe, to the extent that it shatters every mountain and rock into dust and levels even the highest peaks. Therefore, grant me strength and power to endure this heavy suffering, and help me to preserve this exalted gift and bestowal. Thou knowest full well that my heart has sought nothing but to remember Thee, and my soul has desired nothing but Thy love. Support and uplift me in serving Thy beloved servants, and enable me to abide eternally in the grace of serving at Thy Threshold. Thou art Love and Bounty. Thou art the Lord of immense grace and favor.
prayers.name: Абдул-Бахагийн 26 мөргөл (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
ASSISTANCE

Dispel my sorrow by Thy bounty and Thy generosity, O God, my God, and banish my anguish through Thy sovereignty and Thy might. Thou seest me, O my God, with my face set towards Thee at a time when sorrows have compassed me on every side. I implore Thee, O Thou Who art the Lord of all being, and overshadowest all things visible and invisible, by Thy Name whereby Thou hast subdued the hearts and the souls of men, and by the billows of the Ocean of Thy mercy and the splendors of the Daystar of Thy bounty, to number me with them who have allowed nothing whatsoever to prevent them from setting their faces towards Thee, O Thou Lord of all names and Maker of the heavens!

Thou beholdest, O my Lord, the things which have befallen me in Thy days. I entreat Thee, by Him Who is the Day-Spring of Thy names and the Dawning-Place of Thy attributes, to ordain for me what will enable me to arise to serve Thee and to extol Thy virtues. Thou art, verily, the Almighty, the Most Powerful, Who art wont to answer the prayers of all men!

And, finally, I beg of Thee by the light of Thy countenance to bless my affairs, and redeem my debts, and satisfy my wants. Thou art He Whose power and Whose dominion every tongue hath confessed, and Whose majesty and Whose sovereignty every understanding heart hath acknowledged. No God is there but Thee, Who hearest and art ready to answer.
prayers.name: ООР УЧУРДА КОЛДОО (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O God, my God! Enlighten the brows of them that truly love Thee, and assist them with the victorious hosts of Thine angels. Set their steps firmly on Thy straight path, and from Thy eternal bounty open wide the gates of Thy grace unto them; for on Thy path, in defense of Thy Faith, they spend that which Thou hast bestowed upon them. In Thy remembrance they place their trust, out of love for Thee they give their hearts, and in supplication to Thy beauty, seeking Thy favour, they withhold not what they possess.

O my Lord! Ordain for them a plenteous share and the assured reward that is theirs!

Verily, Thou art the Sustainer, the Helper, the Bountiful, the Bestower, the Ever-Giving.
prayers.name: Source (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
He is God!

O God, my God! Grant unto me a pure heart, like unto a pearl.
prayers.name: Children (LLM translated)

nl.cat: Gebeden, vertaald ter gelegenheid van de honderdste gedenkdag in 2021van het heengaan van de Meester

prayers.text:
O Lord my God, Thou art Great! I pray Thee that Thou mayest cleanse from the hearts of Thy servants all that would deprive them of the vision of Thy Countenance, by showering upon them from the clouds of Thy overflowing compassion, so that they may all know Thee. Thou art the Maker and the Creator. O Lord, where every kind of foulness can be separated from the fragrance of the speech that sings the praise of Thy exalted and glorious Name, and even if all that is in heaven and earth is obtained, without even glancing at it, they may experience oneness with Thee, and reach the point where they do not feel like stopping Thy praise, help Thy servants with Thy sovereign power.

O My Beloved, the object of my heart's desire, my prayer to Thee is to protect Thy servant who thirsts for the sight of Thy face from the arrows of atheists and the rods of those who deny Thy truth. O Lord, grant Thy servant the boon that he may surrender himself completely to Thee, proclaim Thy Name, and set his sight upon the sanctuary of Thy manifestation.

Verily, those who long for Thy presence, Thou hast never turned them back from the door of Thy mercy. Thou hast not prevented those who seek Thee from coming to the court of Thy grace; there is no God but Thou. Thou art the Almighty, the Most High, the Remover of difficulties, the Most Glorious, the All-Regulating, the Unfettered.
prayers.name: માર્ગદર્શન–રક્ષણ (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
I am humble and weak,

My God, my God! Thou dost look upon me with favour. I am humble and weak, yet engaged in the great undertaking of making known Thy word to the people of the world and of proclaiming Thy teachings to Thy people. Were it not for the assistance of Thy Holy Spirit, I could never succeed. Grant that I may obtain victory through the hosts of Thy glorious kingdom. And shower down upon me Thy confirmations, making the moth an eagle, a drop of water a river and sea, an atom a light and the sun. My Lord! Help me with Thy all-conquering power. Then shall my tongue speak Thy praise and glorification to all people, and my soul be filled with the wine of Thy love and knowledge. Verily, Thou art Almighty, and dost whatsoever Thou wilt.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
very soul that journeyeth through cities, towns and villages... and is engaged in spreading the sweet savours of God, should read this prayer every morning.

O my Lord! O my Lord! Thou seest me in my lowliness and my weakness, busied with the greatest endeavour, intent on publishing Thy words among Thy servants. How can I succeed unless Thou helpest me with the breath of the Holy Spirit, dost aid me to triumph through the hosts of Thy glorious kingdom, and pourest down upon me Thy bounty which can change a gnat into an eagle, a drop of water into rivers and oceans, and an atom into light and the sun? O my Lord! Aid me with Thy victorious and effective power, that My tongue may utter Thy praises and Thy attributes among all people, and My spirit overflow with the wine of Thy love and knowledge.

Thou art the All-Powerful, and the Maker of whatsoever Thou wishest.
prayers.name: Written to the Bahá'í friends in the Southern Countries (LLM translated)

ms.cat: Dituliskan kepada rakan-rakan Bahá'í di Negara-Negara Selatan

prayers.text:
MOTHER AND FATHER

I beg Thy forgiveness, O my God, and implore Thy pardon as Thou dost wish Thy servants to turn unto Thee. I implore Thee to blot out our sins according to Thy divine majesty, and to forgive me, my parents, and those who, in Thy estimation, have entered the abode of Thy love, in a manner that befitteth Thy sublime sovereignty and is worthy of Thy glorious heavenly dominion. O my God, Thou hast inspired my soul to call upon Thee, and were it not for Thee, I would not call upon Thee. Glorified and exalted art Thou; I give thanks unto Thee, for Thou hast revealed Thyself unto me, and I implore Thee to forgive me, for I have been negligent in my duty to know Thee and have failed to walk in the path of Thy love.
prayers.name: IBU BAPA (LLM translated)

prayers.text: O Thou divine Providence, we are lowly, succor us; we are homeless and wanderers, give us shelter; we are scattered, unite us; we have gone astray, gather us into Thy fold; we are bereft, grant us a share; we are thirsty, lead us to the wellspring of life; we are weak, strengthen us that we may arise and help Thy Cause, and offer ourselves as a living sacrifice and a guide on the spiritual path.
prayers.name: PROTECTION (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
The search results provide several options for Baháʼí prayers related to a spiritual assembly, attributed to 'Abdu'l-Bahá. The user provided Tamil text and asked for an English translation.

The Tamil text provided by the user matches the "Opening Prayer (Option A)" in the search results.
Specifically, the user's input:
"## ஆன்மீக சபை
நீங்கள் கலந்தாலோசனை அறையில் நுழையும் பொழுதெல்லாம், இறைவனின் அன்பினால் துடிக்கும் உள்ளத்தோடும், அவரது நினைவைத் தவிர மற்றெல்லாவற்றிலிருந்தும் தூய்மைப்படுத்தப்பட்ட நாவோடும் இப்பிரார்த்தனையைக் கூறுங்கள்; அதனால் நீங்கள் மகத்தான வெற்றியை அடைவதற்கு, சர்வசக்தி வாய்ந்தவர் உங்களுக்கு அருள் கூர்ந்து உதவக்கூடும்.
கடவுளே, என் கடவுளே! மகத்துவம் பொருந்திய இந் நாளில் உம்மைத் தவிர வேறு பற்றுகளை எல்லாம் துறந்து பக்தியுடன் உமது திருமுகம் நோக்கி நிற்கும் நாங்கள் உமது ஊழியர்களாவோம். எங்கள் கருத்திலும் சிந்தனையிலும் ஒன்றுபட்டு உமது திருமொழியினை மனித இனத்தின் மத்தியில் உயர்வுறச் செய்ய வேண்டும் என்ற நோக்க ஒற்றுமையுடன் நாங்கள் இவ்வான்மீகச் சபையில் கூடியிருக்கின்றோம். பிரபுவே, எங்கள் இறைவா! எங்களை உமது தெய்வீக வழிகாட்டுதலின் அடையாளங்களாகவும், மனிதர்களிடையே உமது உயரிய சமயத்தின் விருதுக்கொடிகளாகவும், உமது வலிமைமிகு ஒப்பந்தத்தின் ஊழியர்களாகவும்—எங்களின் அதி மேன்மையான பிரபுவானவரே—உமது அப்ஹா இராஜ்யத்தினில் உமது தெய்வீக ஒற்றுமையின் வெளிப்படுத்துதலாகவும், எல்லாப் பகுதிகளிலும் ஒளிவிட்டுப் பிரகாசிக்கும் விண்மீன்களாகவும் ஆக்குவீராக. பிரபுவே! உமது வியத்தகு அருள் அலைகள் நிறைந்த கடலாகவும், உமது ஒளிமயமான உச்சத்திலிருந்து வழிந்தோடும் நீரோடைகளாகவும், உமது விண்ணுலகச் சமயமெனும் விருட்சத்தின் மீதுள்ள இனிய கனிகளாகவும், உமது வள்ளன்மை என்னும் தென்றல் காற்றினில் அசைந்தாடிடும் திராட்சைத் தோட்டத்தின் மரங்களாகவும் ஆகிட எங்களுக்கு உதவி புரிவீராக. கடவுளே! உமது அருட்பொழிவினால் எங்களின் உள்ளங்களைக் களிப்புறச் செய்து, எங்களின் ஆன்மாவை உமது தெய்வீக ஒற்றுமையென்னும் வாசகங்களைச் சார்ந்திருக்கச் செய்வீராக; அதனால் நாங்கள் ஒரே கடலின் அலைகள் போல் ஒன்றிணைந்து, உமது பிரகாசமிக்கத் தீபத்தின் ஒளிக்கதிர்கள் போல் ஒன்றிணைந்திடுவோமாக; அதனால் எங்கள் சிந்தனைகள், கருத்துகள், உணர்ச்சிகள் ஆகிய யாவும் ஒரே மெய்ப்பொருளாகி, உலக முழுவதிலும் ஒற்றுமையுணர்வை வெளிப்படுத்திடுமாக. நீரே அருள்மிகுந்தவர், வள்ளன்மை மிக்கவர், வழங்குபவர், எல்லாம் வல்லவர், கருணைமிக்கவர், இரங்குபவர்.
— அப்துல்-பஹா"

Matches the search result's "Opening Prayer (Option A)" Tamil version.
The English translation provided in the search result for this option is:
"O God, my God! We are servants of Thine that have turned with devotion to Thy Holy Face, that have detached ourselves from all besides Thee in this glorious Day. We have gathered in this spiritual assembly, united in our views and thoughts, with our purposes harmonized to exalt Thy Word amidst mankind. O Lord, our God! Make us the signs of Thy divine guidance, the standards of Thy exalted Faith amongst men, servants to Thy mighty Covenant, O Thou our Lord Most High, manifestations of Thy divine unity in Thine Abhá Kingdom, and resplendent stars shining upon all regions. Lord! Help us to become as seas surging with the billows of Thy wondrous grace, streams flowing from Thy all-glorious heights, goodly fruits upon the Tree of Thy heavenly Cause, trees waving through the breezes of Thy bounty in Thy celestial vineyard. O God! Make our souls dependent upon the verses of Thy divine unity, our hearts illumined with the light of Thy knowledge, that we may be united in love and find happiness in the world of Thy creation. Thou art, verily, the All-Powerful, the Almighty, the Most Generous."

> ## ஆன்மீக சபை
> நீங்கள் கலந்தாலோசனை அறையில் நுழையும் பொழுதெல்லாம், இறைவனின் அன்பினால் துடிக்கும் உள்ளத்தோடும், அவரது நினைவைத் தவிர மற்றெல்லாவற்றிலிருந்தும் தூய்மைப்படுத்தப்பட்ட நாவோடும் இப்பிரார்த்தனையைக் கூறுங்கள்; அதனால் நீங்கள் மகத்தான வெற்றியை அடைவதற்கு, சர்வசக்தி வாய்ந்தவர் உங்களுக்கு அருள் கூர்ந்து உதவக்கூடும்.
> கடவுளே, என் கடவுளே! மகத்துவம் பொருந்திய இந் நாளில் உம்மைத் தவிர வேறு பற்றுகளை எல்லாம் துறந்து பக்தியுடன் உமது திருமுகம் நோக்கி நிற்கும் நாங்கள் உமது ஊழியர்களாவோம். எங்கள் கருத்திலும் சிந்தனையிலும் ஒன்றுபட்டு உமது திருமொழியினை மனித இனத்தின் மத்தியில் உயர்வுறச் செய்ய வேண்டும் என்ற நோக்க ஒற்றுமையுடன் நாங்கள் இவ்வான்மீகச் சபையில் கூடியிருக்கின்றோம். பிரபுவே, எங்கள் இறைவா! எங்களை உமது தெய்வீக வழிகாட்டுதலின் அடையாளங்களாகவும், மனிதர்களிடையே உமது உயரிய சமயத்தின் விருதுக்கொடிகளாகவும், உமது வலிமைமிகு ஒப்பந்தத்தின் ஊழியர்களாகவும்—எங்களின் அதி மேன்மையான பிரபுவானவரே—உமது அப்ஹா இராஜ்யத்தினில் உமது தெய்வீக ஒற்றுமையின் வெளிப்படுத்துதலாகவும், எல்லாப் பகுதிகளிலும் ஒளிவிட்டுப் பிரகாசிக்கும் விண்மீன்களாகவும் ஆக்குவீராக. பிரபுவே! உமது வியத்தகு அருள் அலைகள் நிறைந்த கடலாகவும், உமது ஒளிமயமான உச்சத்திலிருந்து வழிந்தோடும் நீரோடைகளாகவும், உமது விண்ணுலகச் சமயமெனும் விருட்சத்தின் மீதுள்ள இனிய கனிகளாகவும், உமது வள்ளன்மை என்னும் தென்றல் காற்றினில் அசைந்தாடிடும் திராட்சைத் தோட்டத்தின் மரங்களாகவும் ஆகிட எங்களுக்கு உதவி புரிவீராக. கடவுளே! உமது அருட்பொழிவினால் எங்களின் உள்ளங்களைக் களிப்புறச் செய்து, எங்களின் ஆன்மாவை உமது தெய்வீக ஒற்றுமையென்னும் வாசகங்களைச் சார்ந்திருக்கச் செய்வீராக; அதனால் நாங்கள் ஒரே கடலின் அலைகள் போல் ஒன்றிணைந்து, உமது பிரகாசமிக்கத் தீபத்தின் ஒளிக்கதிர்கள் போல் ஒன்றிணைந்திடுவோமாக; அதனால் எங்கள் சிந்தனைகள், கருத்துகள், உணர்ச்சிகள் ஆகிய யாவும் ஒரே மெய்ப்பொருளாகி, உலக முழுவதிலும் ஒற்றுமையுணர்வை வெளிப்படுத்திடுமாக. நீரே அருள்மிகுந்தவர், வள்ளன்மை மிக்கவர், வழங்குபவர், எல்லாம் வல்லவர், கருணைமிக்கவர், இரங்குபவர்.

prayers.text:
He is the Most Luminous, the Most Radiant.

O Divine Providence, O forgiving Lord! How can I ever worthily sing Thy praise nor sufficiently worship and extol Thee? Thy description, by any tongue, is sheer error, and Thy characterization, by any pen, is evidence of the futility of embarking upon this immense task. Language is but a tool composed of elements; voice and speech are but accidental properties. How then could I, by means of an earthly voice, extol Him Who hath no peer nor like? All that I can utter or pursue is confined within the limits of the human mind's conception and restricted to the human world. How can human thought ever ascend to the sublime heights of Divine holiness, and how can the spider of vain imagination ever weave the fragile web of futile fancies as a veil for the abodes of sanctity? I can do naught but testify to my powerlessness and confess mine inability. Thou art indeed He Who is All-Possessing, Unapproachable, He Who is immeasurably above the comprehension of those endued with understanding.
prayers.name: Abdu'l-Bahá's 26 New Prayers (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
He is God! O thou who art attracted by the fragrances of God! Thy last letter hath reached me, which indicateth thy exceeding love for ‘Abdu’l-Bahá, thy confidence in God, and thy good and sincere intention in the service of the Cause of God. Excellent is the statement given by thee in that precious letter—that ye are in need of love and affinity in that country to unite the hearts and spirits. This is the truth, and naught is there after the truth but manifest error.

Know thou of a certainty that Love is the secret of God’s holy Dispensation, the manifestation of the All-Merciful, the fountain of spiritual outpourings. Love is heaven’s kindly light, the Holy Spirit’s eternal breath that vivifieth the human soul. Love is the cause of God’s revelation unto man, the vital bond inherent, in accordance with the divine creation, in the realities of things. Love is the one means that ensureth true felicity both in this world and the next. Love is the light that guideth in darkness, the living link that uniteth God with man, that assureth the progress of every illumined soul. Love is the most great law that ruleth this mighty and heavenly cycle, the unique power that bindeth together the divers elements of this material world, the supreme magnetic force that directeth the movements of the spheres in the celestial realms. Love revealeth with unfailing and limitless power the mysteries latent in the universe. Love is the spirit of life unto the adorned body of mankind, the establisher of true civilization in this mortal world, and the shedder of imperishable glory upon every high-aiming race and nation.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.name: EMPURU (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
The Pacific

O Thou God, the Most Holy and the Almighty! Thou art my Lord, the Most Gracious! Grant us such strength that we may face all kinds of difficulties throughout the world, and permit us to be given a power that will raise the waves of our efforts like the waves of the Pacific Ocean, so that they may reach the shores of the East and the West.
prayers.name: The Pacific (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
my Lord, my God, Thou art great! I call on Thee by Thy winds of grace that blow powerfully, and by the Source of Thy purpose and the dawn of Thy inspiration, to rain down upon me and upon all who are eager to behold the face of Thy generosity and to reflect Thy bountiful and all-encompassing mercy, Thy blessings and Thy favors. O my Lord, I am poor and in need. Let me drown in the ocean of Thy riches. I am thirsty; let me drink the living waters of the river of Thy tender mercy.

I implore Thee by Thy name, which Thou hast made the Revealer of Thy countenance, and by Thy wisdom which is spread throughout heaven and earth, to gather all Thy servants under the shade of the tree of Thy merciful decree. Let them eat of its fruit. Let their ears hearken to the murmuring of its leaves and the melodious songs of the celestial birds upon its branches. Thou art, verily, the Help in peril, the Omnipotent, the Most Bountiful.
prayers.name: Guidance–Protection (LLM translated)

lo.cat: ເມື່ອປະສົບຄວາມຍາກລໍາບາກ

prayers.text:
In the Name of the Merciful God

O Pure God, Thou didst from the beginning make the soil of Iran fragrant, and inspiring, knowledge-conferring, and gem-bestowing. From its East, Thy sun hath always shed light, and in its West, the luminous moon hath appeared. Its country is nurture-loving; its plains, like unto paradise, are full of life-giving flowers and plants; its mountains are full of fresh fruits; and its meadows are the envy of the garden of paradise. Its intellect is the message of the Cherub, and its ebullition like the deep, tumultuous ocean.

There was a time when the fire of its knowledge was extinguished, and the star of its greatness was hidden beneath a veil. Its vernal wind became autumn, and its captivating rose garden became a thorny wilderness. Its sweet spring turned bitter, and its beloved great ones became wanderers, driven from every distant land. Its radiance darkened, and its river became a trickle of water. Until the ocean of Thy bounty surged, and the sun of Thy bestowal dawned. A new spring arrived, the life-giving wind blew, and the rain of Bahman fell. The radiance of that love-nurturing Sun shone forth. The country stirred, the dust-heap became a rose garden, and the black earth became the envy of the orchard. The world became a new world, and its fame spread wide. The plains and mountains became green and verdant, and the birds of the meadow became companions in song and melody. It is a time of joy, a heavenly message, an eternal foundation. Awake, awake!

O Thou Exalted Lord, now a gathering hath been formed, and a group hath united to strive with all their soul, that they may impart a share of that rain of Thy bounty to their companions, and through the power of Thy nurturing, bring up their children in the embrace of intelligence, making them the envy of the learned. They shall teach the heavenly laws and manifest God's bestowal.

Therefore, O Thou Kind Lord, be Thou our strength and support, that they may attain their desires, overlook shortcomings, and make that land a model of the world above.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O seeker of Truth! If thou dost wish that God open the eyes of thy spirit, thou must earnestly ask of God, pray and commune with Him at midnight, and say:

O God, I have turned my face towards the kingdom of Thy oneness and am diving into the ocean of Thy compassion! O God, illumine my sight by showing me the light of Thy countenance in this dark night, and gladden me with the wine of Thy love in this wondrous age. O God, suffer me to hear Thy call, and open before my face the gates of Thy paradise, that I may behold the light of Thy glory and be attracted to Thy beauty.

Verily, Thou art the Giver, the Bountiful, the Merciful, the Forgiver!
prayers.name: MIDNIGHT PRAYER (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O my God, my Lord, the Goal of my desire! This thy servant asketh for thy care and thy protection, wishing to sleep in the shadow of thy compassion and to rest beneath the wing of thy grace.

I ask, O my Lord, that thou preserve thou my eyes from seeing aught but Thee, and by thine ever-wakeful eyes make thou their sight keen, so that they may recognize thy signs and behold the horizons of thy revelation.

Thou art He before Whose majesty the essence of power did tremble.

All-powerful, All-conquering, Independent, there is no God but Thee.
prayers.name: Evening (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O my Lord! Thy servant is thirsty; Grant them to drink from the fount of Thy tenderness and bounty. They are hungry; grant them the table of Thy gifts. They are naked; clothe them with the raiment of Thy knowledge.

O my Lord! They are the pure, the sincere. Guide them to the field of battle. They are the guides. Grant them to speak with reason and proof. They are the servants who attend. Grant them to offer the cup of assurance to all. O my Lord, make them as nightingales singing praise in the garden, as lions lurking in the thickets, as whales swimming in the deep sea.

Indeed, Thou art the Most Compassionate, the Bountiful, the Ever-Giving. There is no God but Thee.
prayers.name: Shameful Error (LLM translated)

prayers.text:

Հանուն Նրա` Բարձրյալի, Ամենավեհի, Ամենավսեմի: Փառապանծ ես Դու, ո՛վ Աստված, ի՛մ Աստված: Ով Դու՛, Ո՛վ իմ Աստվածն է, իմ Տերը, իմ  Տիրակալը, իմ Աջակցությունը, իմ Հույսը, իմ Ապաստանն ու իմ Լույսը: Խնդրում եմ Քեզ, քո Նվիրական ու Թաքուն Անվամբ, որը ոչ ոք չգիտի, բացի Քեզանից: Պաշտպանիր այս Հուշատախտակի տիրոջը բոլոր տեսակի փորձանքներից ու աղետներից, բոլոր արատավոր տղամարդկանցից ու կանանցից, չարագործների պատճառած չարիքից ու անհավատների դավերից: Բացի այդ պաշտպանիր նրան, ո՛վ իմ Աստված, բոլոր տեսակի ցավերից ու կսկիծներից, ո՛վ Դու, ով Իր Աջում է պահում ամբողջ գոյի թագավորությունը: Դու հիրավի տիրում ես ամեն ինչի: Դու վարվում ես ինչպես կամենում ես ու հրամայում ես Քո ցանկությամբ: Ո՛վ Դու Արքաների Արքա: Ո՛վ բարի Աստված: Ո՛վ Դու հնագույն գեղեցկության,  երկնային շնորհների, առատաձեռնության ու բարեհաճության Աղբյուր: Ո՛վ Դու ախտերը Բուժող: Ո՛վ Դու կարիքի մեջ Շնորհող: Ո՛վ Դու ճշմարիտ Լույսի Լույս: Ո՛վ Դու բոլոր տեսակի Լույսերից բարձր Լույս: Ո՛վ Դու բոլոր Հայտնիչների Բացահայտող: Ո՛վ Դու Կարեկցող: Ո՛վ Դու Ողորմած: Գթա այս Հուշատախտակի տիրոջը Քո մեծագույն ողորմածությամբ ու Քո առատ բարեհաճությամբ, ո՛վ Դու Մեծահոգի, ո՛վ Առատաձեռն: Պահպանիր նրան Քո վահանով ամենից, ինչ նրա միտքն ու սիրտը կհամարեն անարժան: Նրանցից, ովքեր շնորհված են ուժով, Դու հիրավի Ամենաուժեղն ես: Թո՛ղ Աստծո փառքը Քեզ հետ լինի, ո՛վ Ծագող Արև:

I will execute these one by one.
## Prayer for recovery during a pandemic
In His Name, the Exalted, the All-Highest, the Most Sublime! Glorified art Thou, O Lord my God! O Thou Who art my God, and my Master, and my Lord, and my Support, and my Hope, and my Refuge, and my Light. I ask of Thee, by Thy Hidden and Treasured Name, which none knoweth save Thine own Self, to protect the bearer of this Tablet from every calamity and pestilence, and from every wicked man and woman; from the evil of the evil-doers, and from the scheming of the unbelievers.
Moreover, protect him, O my God, from every kind of pain and sorrow, O Thou Who holdest in Thy right hand the kingdom of all being. Thou, verily, art the Ruler of all things. Thou doest as Thou willest and ordainest as Thou pleasest. O Thou King of Kings! O Thou Kind God! O Thou Source of ancient beauty, of heavenly graces, of generosity and favor! O Thou Healer of ailments! O Thou Bestower in need! O Thou Light of the true Light! O Thou Light above all Lights! O Thou Revealer of all Manifestations! O Thou Compassionate! O Thou Merciful! Have mercy upon the owner of this Tablet with Thy greatest mercy and Thy abundant favor, O Thou Magnanimous, O Thou Generous! Protect him with Thy shield from everything that his mind and heart would deem unworthy. Of those who are endowed with power, Thou, verily, art the Most Powerful. The glory of God be upon thee, O Day-Star!
Bear witness to that which God Himself testified in Himself, that there is no God but Him, the Almighty, the Beloved.
prayers.name: Աղոթք համաճարակի ընթացքում ապաքինման մասին (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O Lord! In Thee I seek refuge, and towards all Thy signs I set my heart.
 
O Lord! Whether on journey or at home, and in my occupation or in my work, I place my whole trust in Thee.
 
Grant me then Thy sufficing help so as to make me independent of all things, O Thou Who art unsurpassed in Thy mercy!
 
Bestow upon me my portion, O Lord, as Thou pleasest, and cause me to be satisfied with whatsoever Thou hast ordained for me.
 
Thine is the absolute authority to command.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
I am unable to translate the prayer directly as the language code 'sne' is not supported by my translation tools. My search indicates that the prayer is in the Bidayuh language.

However, I found an English translation for a portion of the prayer you provided:

## Prayer of Testing

O Thou Whose tests are a remedy for such as are nigh unto Thee, Whose sword is the ardent desire of all them that love Thee, Whose shaft is the most beloved desire of the hearts of them that yearn after Thee, Whose decree is the sole hope of them that have recognized Thy truth! I implore Thee, by Thy divine sweetness and by the splendors of the glory of Thy face, to ordain for us from Thy retreats on high that which will enable us to draw nigh unto Thee. Cause our feet, then, to be steadfast in Thy Cause, and enlighten our hearts with the effulgence of Thy knowledge, and illumine our breasts with the brightness of Thy names.
prayers.name: Doa Pinguji Duoh Kilapont (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
All friends of God . . . should contribute what they can, however humble that sacrifice may be. God does not lay upon any soul a burden heavier than it can bear. Such contributions must come from all centres and all believers . . . O ye friends of God! Be ye assured that by reason of these contributions, your agriculture, industry and commerce shall receive manifold blessings with favour and bounteous gifts. He that doeth one good deed receiveth a tenfold reward. There is no doubt that the living Lord will abundantly confirm those who give their wealth in His way.
## O God, my God! Illumine the faces of Thy true lovers and make them triumphant with heavenly hosts of certain victory. Make firm their feet on Thy straight path and open to them the gates of Thy gifts with Thy eternal grace; for they spend in Thy ways that which Thou hast given them, guard Thy faith, place their trust in Thy remembrance, sacrifice their hearts for love of Thee, and withhold not aught of that which they possess for adoration of Thy beauty and in their longing to please Thee.
O my Lord! Grant them an abundant share, a destined recompense and a sure reward.
Verily, Thou art the Sustainer, the Helper, the Bountiful, the Ever-Giving.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
He is the Glory of God
O Merciful, O Compassionate, O Bountiful! East and West are illumined by the lights of the Dawning-Place of Beauty, and the spiritual senses are perfumed by the divine breezes. O God, give Thy servants shelter in the shadow of Thy favour; make the ignorant aware of the mysteries of truth; grant the destitute a refuge and the wanderers a home; gladden the broken-hearted and give wings to the broken-winged birds; let the thirsty drink from the fount of Thy bounty and make the needy independent of the treasure of the kingdom; nourish the children in the cradle of Thy grace until they reach maturity; heal the sick with the heavenly remedy so that they may attain health and well-being.
O God, make the blind to see and the deaf to hear; quicken the dead and revive the withered; make the hopeless hopeful and worthy of all bestowals the deprived; Thou art the Generous, Thou art the Giver, Thou art the Merciful.
O Lord, though we are weak, Thou art powerful; though we are poor, Thou art rich; though we are abased, Thou art exalted. Look not at our poverty and weakness, but rather bestow upon us that which befits Thy absolute riches, power, and might.
Thou art the Merciful, Thou art the Bountiful, Thou art the Generous, Thou art the Compassionate.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
The Most Holy, the Most Inaccessible, the Most Great.
The Feast of the Birth has come, and the Beauty of God, the Almighty, the Mighty, the Loving, has established Himself upon the throne. Blessed is he who is present this day before the Face, and to whom the Eye of God, the Protector, the Self-Subsisting, has turned. Say: We took the Feast in the Greatest Prison after what the kings rose up against us. Let not the might of any oppressor prevent us, nor shall the hosts of sovereignty disturb us. This is what the Merciful has testified in this Praiseworthy Station. Say: Does the essence of tranquility become disturbed by the clamor of possibility, nay, by His effulgent Beauty upon all that was and all that shall be? This is the might of God, which has encompassed all things, and this is His dominant power over all that is witnessed and all that is beheld. Cling to the cord of might, then remember your Chosen Lord in this dawn wherein all hidden unseen has shone forth. Thus has the Tongue of Eternity spoken on this day wherein the Sealed Nectar has been released. Beware lest the delusions of those who have disbelieved in God disturb you, or suspicions prevent you from this outstretched path.

O People of Splendor! Fly with the pinions of detachment in the atmosphere of the love of your Merciful Lord, then assist Him with what has been revealed in a Preserved Tablet. Beware lest you dispute with any of the servants; rather, manifest through the fragrance of God and His exposition, by which all faces are directed. Verily, those who are heedless this day are in a drunkenness of desire, and they comprehend not. Blessed is he who turns to the dawning-place of his Lord's signs with submission and repentance. Verily, thou art He who arises among the servants, then reminds them of what has been revealed in the Book of their Noble, Chosen Lord. Say: Fear God, and follow not the delusions of those who have pursued every sinful doubter. Turn with luminous hearts to the direction of your Lord's Throne, the Possessor of Names. He shall indeed support you with Truth. There is no god but He, the Mighty, the Bountiful. Do you hasten to the pool, and the Greatest Ocean?
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

I am now broadening my search to find comprehensive Bahá'í prayer resources that may contain this translation.

God is He

O compassionate Lord! Though my existence be as naught, yet do I place my hope in Thy grace and bounty; and though I be devoid of capability and talent, yet do I yearn for Thy favour and providence. I was astray; Thou didst guide me. I was blind; Thou didst make me see. I was deprived; Thou didst make me a confidant of Thy mysteries. I was dead; Thou didst quicken me with the breaths of the Holy Garden. Now, O Lord, grant me steadfastness and bestow upon me confirmation, that I may remain firm in Thy love for the Dawning-point of Thine Oneness, and be illumined by the lights of the Sun of Truth. May my tongue utter Thy remembrance and praise, and may I be occupied with the adoration of Thy most Gracious Countenance. May I forget all except Thee, and be silent from the mention of aught besides Thee, and become intoxicated and lost in the cup of Thy love. May I devote myself to the service of Thy Cause, and in the assemblies of the handmaids of the Merciful, open my tongue to Thy remembrance with the most wonderful melodies, and adorn the gathering. Thou art the Mighty, the Powerful, Thou art the Supporter, the Successful, the All-seeing, O my Most High Lord.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Here is the translated prayer:

## Penitent, confessing wrongdoing.

My Lord! My Lord! I am as a bird with a broken wing, and flying slowly. Aid me to soar to the heights of prosperity and pass through the boundless atmosphere with joy and gladness, chanting the Most Great Name of God throughout all lands, to the delight of the eyes that behold the signs of guidance.

My Lord! I am alone, solitary, and lowly, with no helper but Thee, no comforter but Thee. Sustain me in my service to Thee; help me with Thy heavenly hosts; bless me in promoting Thy word, that I may announce Thy mysteries among the people. Verily, Thou art the Powerful, the Glorious, and above all commands.
prayers.name: ຜິດ  ຢ່າງໜ້າລະອາຍ. (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Let the teacher and the friend recite this prayer daily.

O Thou Kind Lord!
Praise be to Thee, O my Lord! Thou hast shown unto us the way of guidance, opened the doors of Thy kingdom, and revealed Thyself through the sun of reality. Thou hast given sight to the blind, hearing to the deaf. Thou hast raised the dead, enriched the poor, guided the erring, and led the thirsty to the springs of life. Thou hast guided the fish in the sea and invited the wandering bird to the rose-gardens of Thy grace.

O Thou Almighty!
We are Thy servants, and are poor. We are far from Thee, and long to reach Thy presence; we thirst for the waters of Thy fountain. We are sick, and long for Thy healing. We walk in Thy paths, and have no aim or hope except to diffuse Thy fragrance, so that all souls may cry out: "O God, guide us unto the straight path." May their eyes be opened to see the light, and be freed from the darkness of ignorance. May they gather around the lamp of Thy guidance. May all those who have no portion share in the portion. May the unblest become the intimate friends of Thy mystery.

O Thou Omnipotent One!
Look upon us with the eyes of Thy mercy. Grant us the assurance of heaven. Bestow upon us the breath of the Holy Spirit, whereby we may be aided in Thy service, and like unto shining stars, illuminate these regions with the light of Thy guidance.

Verily Thou art the All-Powerful, the Mighty, the All-Knowing, the All-Seer.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

O my God! Thou knowest full well that calamities have rained down upon me from every direction and that no one can dispel or alleviate them but Thee. I know of a certainty, by virtue of my love for Thee, that Thou wilt never afflict any soul unless Thou desirest to exalt his station in Thy holy Paradise and to strengthen his heart for living this physical life. By Thy power, Thou providest this protective shield so that he may not incline toward the vain pride of the world. It is true, and Thou art well aware, that I find greater joy in the remembrance of Thy Name than in all the pleasures of the heavens and the earth.
O my God, strengthen my heart in Thy love and in obedience to Thy commandments, and grant that I may remain entirely free from the shadow of Thine adversaries. Truly, I swear by Thy glory that I desire no nearness but Thine, nor do I seek any mercy but Thy mercy, nor do I crave any justice but Thy justice.
Thou art the Most Generous, O Lord of the heavens and the earth, exalted above the praise of all men. Peace be upon Thy obedient servants, and glory be to God, the Lord of all the worlds!
