prayers.text:
He is the Glory of the Most Glorious
O kind Lord, make these Thy servants detached from stranger and kin, and make them intimate with the breezes of the garden of Thy Abhá Kingdom; illuminate the eyes with the vision of Thy lights, and make the souls a garden and meadow; make the hearts a garden of faithfulness, and the breasts a source of divine blessings from the Supreme Concourse. Bestow abundantly the grace of the Holy Spirit, and grant freely the power of the Faithful Spirit, so that Thy friends may arise in Thy service with heavenly strength, with divine power, with majestic might, with heavenly confirmation, with celestial hosts, with a divine breath, with a lordly gift, with the luminous hand of Moses, with the breath of Christ, with the intimacy of Abraham, with the love of the Beloved, with the beauty of Joseph, with the longing of Jacob, and the patience of Job.
O Lord, grant them success in this endeavor; verily, Thou art the Powerful, the All-Glorious.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O God! Draw us to Thy love, inflame us with Thy tenderness; satisfy us from the ocean of Thy bounty; assuage our hunger with the riches of Thy eternal grace; and clothe us with Thy glory. The birds of the air and the beasts of the field receive their sustenance each day from Thee, and all living things are nourished by Thy grace and tenderness.

Let not this frail creature be deprived of Thy favour, and strengthen through Thy power this helpless soul with Thy gift.

Give us our daily bread, and multiply Thy gifts for the sustenance of life, that we may not be dependent on aught else save Thee, may commune fully with Thee, may walk in all Thy ways, and declare all Thy mysteries.

Thou art the Almighty and Loving, and the Provider for all mankind.
prayers.name: PENYAUP (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
He is God

O Lord, this pure soul turned its attention unto Thy kingdom and made supplication and plea. While it was a captive of the material world, it intended to enter the kingdom of permanence and turned its attention toward the spiritual world, hoping for Thine infinite favours.

O Forgiver, forgive the sin and bestow pardon and forgiveness. Lead this thirsty one to the spring of life and plunge this fish into the boundless ocean of Thy forgiveness, that this bird may wing its way to the divine rose-garden and enter the infinite gardens of Thy pardon and forgiveness.

Thou art indeed the Forgiving, the Generous, the Merciful.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
I will now provide the translated text.## Servitude

O Thou Merciful One! O Thou Almighty! I am but a weak servant, helpless and powerless, yet I have been nurtured within the shelter of Thy grace and favor, nourished at the breast of Thy mercy, and reared in the lap of Thy loving-kindness. O Lord! I am indeed poor and needy, yet every needy one is made rich through Thy bounty, whereas every rich one is poor and desolate if Thy favor reacheth him not.

O Divine Providence! Grant me the strength to bear this heavy burden, and enable me to safeguard this supreme gift, for so overwhelming is the violence of the trials and so grievous the onslaught of the temptations that every mountain is scattered in dust, and the highest peak reduced to nothing. Thou knowest full well that in my heart I seek naught but Thy remembrance, and in my soul I desire nothing save Thy love. Raise me up to serve Thy loved ones, and let me abide forever in servitude at Thy Threshold. Thou art the Loving. Thou art the Lord of manifold bounties.
prayers.name: Dienstbarkeit (LLM translated)

prayers.text: O Thou Whose face is the object of my adoration, Whose beauty is my sanctuary, Whose habitation is my goal, Whose praise is my hope, Whose providence is my companion, Whose love is the cause of my being, Whose mention is my solace, Whose nearness is my desire, Whose presence is my dearest wish and highest aspiration, I entreat Thee not to withhold from me the things Thou didst ordain for the chosen ones among Thy servants. Supply me, then, with the good of this world and of the next. Thou, truly, art the King of all men. There is no God but Thee, the Ever-Forgiving, the Most Generous.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

He is the All-Glorious!

O Thou self-sufficient Lord! Attune these hearts to Thy mystery and make them independent of both stranger and kin. Give them to drink from the morning cup in the dawn of Thy oneness, and intoxicate them with the wine of the Covenant. These are Thy servants, enamored and enraptured by Thee; these are the longing ones, distracted and agitated for Thy sake. They are wanderers in Thy precinct, attracted by Thy beauty, and are as the gazelles of Thy flock.

In the desert of separation we are wandering and lost, and in the valley of deprivation we are distressed. Send forth the escort of Thy grace and the messenger of Thy guidance, that a fragrance from Thy court may reach our nostrils and a ray from Thy countenance may shine upon the dwelling of sorrows of these grief-stricken souls. Thus may the pitch-black darkness be turned into light, and the furnace of deprivation be transformed into the rose-garden of Thy bounty.

Thou art, verily, the Powerful, the All-Seeing, the All-Hearing.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O Merciful Lord, illuminate the hearts with the light of the Most Great Guidance, quicken the souls with the Most Great Glad-Tidings, brighten the eyes with the vision of Thy lights, make the ears attentive to the hearing of Thy call, cause us to enter Thy hallowed kingdom and to be revived by the breaths of the Holy Spirit, grant eternal life, bestow heavenly perfections.
O Lord, sacrifice our souls unto Thee, and bestow upon us a new spirit, grant heavenly strength, bestow eternal joy, make us successful in the service of the world of humanity, and be the cause of unity amongst hearts.
O Lord, awaken us from slumber and make us wise and vigilant, that we may comprehend the mysteries of Thy holy book and grasp the secrets of Thy words.
Thou art Powerful, Thou art the Giver, Thou art the Merciful.

He is the All-Glorious!

O Kind Lord! I thank Thee that Thou hast awakened me and made me conscious. Thou hast given me a seeing eye and favored me with a hearing ear; Thou hast guided me to Thy Kingdom and directed me to Thy Path. Thou hast shown me the right way and caused me to enter the Ark of Deliverance.

O God! Keep me steadfast and make me firm and staunch. Protect me from violent tests and preserve and shelter me in the strongly fortified fortress of Thy Covenant and Testament. Thou art the Powerful. Thou art the Seeing. Thou art the Hearing.

O Thou Kind God! Bestow upon me a heart which, like unto glass, may be illumined with the light of Thy love, and confer upon me a thought which may change this world into a rose garden through the outpourings of heavenly grace.

Thou art the Compassionate, the Merciful. Thou art the Great Beneficent God.

prayers.text:
O Merciful Lord! We are Your servants and have gathered in this meeting. We turn to Your kingdom and are in need of Your blessings. O Lord! Please reveal the sign of Your Oneness, which is in the essence of life, to be manifest. Please reveal the virtues that You bestow and are hidden in the essence of humanity.

O Lord! We are like trees and Your kindness is like the rain. Please make these trees refreshed and grow with Your blessings. We are Your servants. Please grant us freedom from the bonds of the material world. We lack wisdom, please grant us wisdom. We are lifeless, please revive us. We are worldly, please bestow spiritual power upon us. We are darkened, please let us reach Your mysteries. We

are poor and in need. Please bestow blessings and abundant riches from Your inexhaustible treasury upon us. O Lord! Please revive our lives. Please enlighten our eyes and ears. Please let us reach the mysteries of life, so that the mysteries of Your kingdom may be revealed to us in this world, and we may accept Your Oneness. All blessings come from You, and You are the Giver.

You are powerful, You are mighty, You are the Giver, and You are always the Merciful and Kind.
prayers.name: Prayer in a Meeting or Gathering (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Thou art the Almighty, the Forgiver, the Merciful! O my Lord, my Lord! Thou seest Thy servants in the depths of hell and error. O Desire of the world, where is the Light of Thy guidance? Thou knowest that they are weak and unable to help themselves. O Thou whose dominion over heaven and earth is in Thy hand, where is Thy dominion?

O my Master, my Lord! I beseech the light of the lamp of Thy grace, the waves of the ocean of Thy knowledge, the utterance with which Thou rulest the people in Thy kingdom. Grant that I may be among those who follow the command in Thy Book, decree for me that which Thou hast decreed for them whom Thou trustest, who have drunk the immortal water of inspiration from the cup of Thy favour. Hasten to do what pleaseth Thee, cling to Thy will and Thy decree. Thou art powerful to do what Thou wishest. There is no God but Thee, the All-knowing, the All-wise.

O my Master, let Thy favour manifest unto me that which will make me radiant in this world and the world to come, and bring me unto Thee. O Lord of mankind, there is no God but Thee, the One, the Almighty, the Praised One.
prayers.name: ເພື່ອຄວາມໝັ້ນຄົງໃນພຣະປະທິນຍາ (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
He is God.

O Lord, forgiving Lord! These are the broken-winged birds, grant them the wings of Thy favour and let them soar in the heavens of Thy mercy. These are the helpless creatures, give them the sustenance of Thy love and let them rest in the shadow of Thy forgiveness. These are the thirsty ones, bring them to the fount of Thy grace and let them drink. These are the needy ones, present them at the table of Thy bounty.

Deliver them from the dark night of separation and gladden their eyes with the dawn of reunion. Pardon their sins, bestow upon them Thy gifts, and fulfil what Thou hast promised.

Though we are sinners, we hope in Thy forgiveness; though we are without talent, we have the utmost trust in Thy justice.

Thou art the Bounteous, the Giver, the Forgiving, the Merciful.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
He is the Glory.
O Lord, Thy friends in every corner are under the threat of strangers and adversaries, weary of life and set aside from every thought, seeking Thy face and moving towards Thee, speaking Thy secret.

O kind Beloved, protect and guard the helpless from strangers in the fortress of Thy protection, and uplift the fallen by the hand of Thy power. Grant abundant portion to the deprived from Thy ancient bounty, make the dispossessed confidants of Thy secrets, and nurture and bestow upon the longing ones the gift of Thy vision, for they are captive to those musk-scented tresses, and at every breath they offer a hundred lives.

Thou art the Mighty and the Capable, and Thou art the Giver, the Bountiful, and the All-Seeing.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

O God, Thine countenance is the object of my worship! Thy beauty is sacred to me, Thy abode; what I seek, Thy praise my hope, Thy plan my companion, Thy love the reason why I am here, to call Thy name my happiness, to be near Thee is what I await, Thy presence my strongest desire and my highest longing. I beseech Thee with all my heart, deny me not what Thou hast ordained for the chosen of Thy servants, and provide me with the things that are good for me in this world and the next.

Thou art indeed the King of all mankind. There is no God besides Thee, the Ever-Forgiving, the Bountiful.
prayers.name: Hëllef a Bäistand (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O God, my God. Turn me not away from the heaven of Thy grace and the Daystar of Thy favour. I implore Thee by Thy Word which hath subdued all that are in the heavens and all that are on the earth. Aid me, and them that have chosen Thy path, to achieve that which Thou didst ordain for the exaltation of Thy Cause amongst Thy servants, and in all lands under Thy dominion. Appoint unto me all good that Thou hast revealed in Thy Holy Book.

Verily, Thou art the All-Powerful, the Ever-Forgiving, the Most Generous.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
He is the All-Glorious.

O my loving God! Through pure bounty Thou didst breathe the spirit of existence and bestow the garment of life. Thou didst cause [me] to drink from the fountain of guidance and didst cause the call of "Am I not [your Lord]?" to reach the ear of this servant. Thou didst cause the cry of "Glory be to my Lord, the All-Glorious" to be heard and didst grant shelter beneath the shade of the Tree of Anisa. Thou didst cause [me] to drink from the cup of the Covenant, didst distinguish [me] with special favor, and didst enable [me] to attain the supreme triumph.

Now, O Lord, make this poor one a manifest banner and illumine this sorrowful one in the radiant horizon. Thou art the Bounteous, the Compassionate.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O Thou, pure God! I am a small creature; suffer that the breast of Thy loving affection be that which I long for; grant that I may be nourished with the milk and honey of Thy love; make me grow in the bosom of Thy knowledge, and bestow upon me nobility and wisdom from infancy.

O Thou, the Self-Subsisting God! Make me loyal to the Kingdom of the Unseen.

Verily, Thou art the Strong, the Mighty.
prayers.name: Children (LLM translated)

prayers.text: Guide Your servants, O my Lord, to the court of Your favor and bounty, and let them not be deprived of the wonders of Your grace and Your abundant blessings. For they know not what Thou hast destined for them by virtue of Thy mercy which encompasseth all creation. O Lord, outwardly they are weak and helpless, and inwardly they are but souls. Thou art the Most Bountiful, the Beneficent, the Most Exalted, the Greatest. O my God, let not Thy fierce wrath descend upon them, and cause them to wait until the time when the wonders of Thy mercy shall be revealed, so that they may turn unto Thee and ask forgiveness for all that they have done unto Thee. Verily, Thou art the Forgiving, the Most Merciful.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text: O Compassionate God! O Lord of Hosts! Praised be Thou for Thou hast preferred these little children over the elders and adults, and hast bestowed upon them Thy special favours. Thou hast guided them. Thou hast been kind to them. Thou hast endowed them with enlightenment and spirituality. Confirm us, so that when we grow up, we may engage in the service of Thy Kingdom, become a cause of the education of others, burn as shining candles and shine as brilliant stars. Thou art the Bountiful, the Bestower, the Compassionate.
prayers.name: Children (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O Thou Holy Provider! Vouchsafe unto all the peoples of Bahá purity and holiness. Make them free from all uncleanness, and deliver them from all attachment. Save them from the commission of any defiling action, loosen them from the tie of every evil habit, that they may live in purity and freedom, good and clean, fit to serve at Thy Sacred Threshold and worthy to be connected with their Lord.

Deliver them from intoxicating drinks and tobacco; guard them, save them from this opium which causeth madness; cause them to savour the sweetness and fragrance of holiness, that they may drink their fill from the mystic cup of heavenly love and know the joy that draweth them nigh unto Thy Most Glorious Realm. For it is as Thou hast said: "All that thou hast in thy stores of wine will not satisfy the thirst of My love—bring unto me, O cupbearer, a cup of spiritual wine full as the ocean!"
prayers.name: GUIDANCE (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
The search did not yield direct results for this specific prayer. I will proceed with a direct translation request to the web search.

I will use the phrase "Translate Persian Bahá'í prayer" to ensure the context is maintained.
The phrase "هُواللّه" is an invocation that translates to "He is God". I will include this in the translation.
The prayer is a single block of text, so I will preserve it as a single block.

He is God!
O Thou Self-Sufficing One! Make Thy loved ones confidants of Thy mysteries and fashion them as revealing mirrors, that they may raise up a melody and song throughout the world and play a tune and a Shahnaz, that the world of existence may be set in motion, and the Beloved of human unity may cast away the veil and manifest in the assembly of the world.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
The friends of God ... should give as much as they are able, however modest their contribution may be. God does not burden any soul beyond its capacity. Such donations must come from everywhere and from every believer ... O friends of God! Be assured that for these donations, your agriculture, your trade, and your commerce will be blessed with manifold growth and abundant gifts. Whoever performs a good deed will receive tenfold reward. Without a doubt, the living God will abundantly confirm those who sacrifice their wealth in His path.
O God, my God! Enlighten the faces of those who truly love Thee, and assist them with the victorious army of Thine angels. Set their steps securely on Thy straight path and open to them, through Thine eternal bounty, the gates of Thy blessings; for on Thy path, for the protection of Thy faith, they spend of that which Thou hast bestowed upon them. In Thy remembrance they place their trust, out of love for Thee they give their hearts, and in prayer to Thy beauty, seeking Thy favour, they withhold not what they possess.
O my Lord! Appoint for them an abundant share, a precise recompense, a secure reward!
Verily, Thou art the Sustainer, the Helper, the Bountiful, the Generous, the Ever-Giver.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
For the Deceased Woman

My Lord, O Forgiver of sins and Remover of suffering! O Thou, the Pardoner, the Merciful! I supplicate Thee with my hands raised, with tears flowing, beseeching the Throne of Thy Divine Essence to forgive, through Thy grace and tenderness, Thy handmaid who hath ascended to the throne of truth. Cause her, O Lord, to be sheltered by the clouds of Thy mercy and Thy bounty, immerse her in the ocean of Thy forgiveness and Thy pardon, and suffer her to enter that hallowed abode, the Paradise of Thy divine presence.

Thou art, verily, the Almighty, the Loving, the Bountiful, the Merciful.
prayers.name: FOR THE DEPARTED SOUL (LLM translated)

O God! We are ignorant, bestow upon us knowledge.
O Lord! We are poor; make us rich.

If Thou dost help us, O Lord, we shall become like shining stars; if Thou dost not help us, we shall become less than dust.

O Lord! Free us from the servitude of the material world.

O Lord! Thou art the Powerful!
O God, Thou art the Forgiving!
O Lord, Thou art the Compassionate!

prayers.text:
O Lord! We are weak; give us strength. O Lord! We are ignorant; give us knowledge. O Master! We are poor; make us wealthy. O God! We are lifeless; give us consciousness. O Controller of the Universe! We are the embodiment of inferiority; give us glory in Your kingdom.

O Lord of the Universe! If You help us, we too will become bright stars; if You do not help, we will go to ruin. O Master! Make us strong. O Supreme God! Grant us victory. O Supreme Soul! Help us to conquer our ego and keep our desires under control. O Father! Grant us liberation from the bonds of the material world.

O Lord! May we be committed to Your service, be absorbed in Your devotion, and sincerely strive to reach Your kingdom; for that, infuse Your holy life-force and give us consciousness. O Master! You are almighty. O God! You are forgiving. O Lord! You are an ocean of compassion.
prayers.name: Spiritual Development (LLM translated)

lo.cat: ຊົງອໍານາດ.

prayers.text:
Glory be to Thee, O Lord, my God! I implore Thee by Thy Name by which He Who is Thy Beauty was established upon the throne of Thy Cause, Thy Name by which Thou dost change and gather together all things, and call them to account, and recompense them, and preserve them all, and sustain them all, ‒ I implore Thee to protect this handmaiden who hath sought refuge in Thee, sought the succour of Him in Whom Thou hast revealed Thyself, and hath fully trusted and relied upon Thee.
She is ailing, O my God, and hath entered into the shadow of Thy Tree of Healing; suffering she hath found sanctuary in Thy City of Refuge; sick she hath sought Thy Fount of Favours; distressed she hath hastened to attain Thy River of Peace; burdened with sins she hath turned her face toward the court of Thy forgiveness.
Bestow on her, by reason of Thy supreme dominion and gracious favour, O my God and my Beloved, the raiment of Thy comfort and healing, and make her to drink of the cup of Thy loving-kindness and Thy favours. Protect her also from all distress and tribulation, from all pain and sickness, from whatsoever may be displeasing unto Thee.
Thou art indeed most exalted above all, save Thyself. Thou art indeed the Healer, the Bestower, the Protector, the Forgiver, the Most Merciful.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
PROTECTION

In the Name of God, the King whose shelter is beyond beauty and magnificence, the Almighty.

The King reigns, in Whose hand is the beginning of dominion. He creates anything He willeth from His command, "Be," and it is. He is the power of rule until now, and will continue thus until times to come. He makes victorious whomever He willeth, by the power of His desire. He is indeed the Powerful, the Almighty. Above Him is all beauty and a shelter beyond all the dominions of Manifestation and the

Creation and all that abides between them. He is indeed the Powerful, the All-Powerful. From eternity He is the beginning of dominion, which cannot be comprehended and will continue thus until eternity. And He is indeed the Lord of might and power. All the worlds in heaven and earth and all that is between them are God's, and His power is above all things. All that is precious in the earth and the heavens and all that is between them belongs to Him, and His protection abides over all things. He is the Deliverer of the heavens and the earth and all that is between them, and He is indeed the Witness over all things. He is the Lord of Judgment for all those in the heavens and the earth and all that is between them, and indeed God is the Judge who does not err. He directs the distribution of things to all people in the heavens and the earth and all that is between them. He is indeed the Supreme Protector in the Kingdom. In His Hands are the keys of the heavens and the earth and all that is between

them. From His grace He bestows gifts, from the power of His command. Indeed His mercy encompasses all things, and He is the All-Knowing.

Say: God is sufficient for me; And He is the One in Whose hands are the dominions of all things. From the power of His might...
prayers.name: PUIPUIIGA (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Me

O God of gentleness! I thank Thee that Thou hast awakened me and made me to know Thee. Thou hast given me eyes to see and ears to hear. Thou hast guided me to Thy kingdom and led me to Thy path. Thou hast shown me the way of truth and brought me into the ark of salvation. O God! Make me firm and steadfast. Protect me from the fiercest of trials and make me safe within the strong citadel of Thy covenant. Thou art powerful. Thou art the Seer. Thou art the Hearer.
O God of gentleness! Grant me a heart like unto glass, that it may be illumined with the light of Thy love, and bestow upon me the thoughts that will cause the heavenly grace to stream down and transform this world into a rose garden.
Thou art gentle and merciful. Thou art God of great bounty.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
The `transliterate_bahai.py` script is designed for transliterating Persian and Arabic Baháʼí texts, not for translating Hindi to English.

I have used the `google_web_search` tool to translate the provided Hindi text into English. The translation preserves the section headers and paragraph breaks as requested.

## Service

I praise Thee, O my God, that the fragrance of Thy loving-kindness hath gladdened me, and the gentle breezes of Thy mercy have drawn me towards the court of Thy bountiful favors. Give me to drink, O my Lord, from the fingers of Thy generosity, of the living water which hath enabled every one who hath partaken thereof to rid himself of all attachment to any one but Thee, and to soar in the atmosphere of detachment from all Thy creatures, and to fix his gaze upon Thy manifold gifts and Thy loving-kindness.

Keep me ready, O my Lord, to serve Thee in every condition, and to advance towards the dawning-place of Thy manifestation and beauty. If it be Thy will, let me be as a tender branch in the pasture of Thy grace, so that the gentle breezes of Thy will may cause me to vibrate in such wise as to be well-pleasing unto Thee, that my motion and my stillness may be entirely directed by Thee.

Thou art He by Whose name the hidden secrets were revealed, and the guarded Name was disclosed, and the seals of the sealed wine-cup were broken, and its fragrance was shed over all creation, whether of the past or of the future. He who was thirsty, O my Lord, hath hastened to attain the living waters of Thy grace, and the unfortunate one hath longed to immerse himself in the depths of the ocean of Thy wealth.

I swear by Thy glory, O Beloved of the world and the Desire of all them that have recognized Thee! I am sore perplexed by the pain of my separation from Thee in these days when the Sun of Thy presence hath shed its radiance upon Thy people. Ordain, then, for me the recompense decreed for those who have gazed upon Thy face, and have, by Thy leave, entered the court of Thy throne, and have, at Thy bidding, attained Thy presence.

I beseech Thee, O my Lord, by Thy name whose splendor hath encompassed the earth and the heavens, to empower me to surrender my will to what Thou hast ordained in Thy Tablets, that I may find within me no desire except that which Thou, by the power of Thy sovereignty, hast willed for me...
prayers.name: सेवा (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
I found an existing English translation for the first part. I will continue to find the full translation. I will search for the next part of the Russian text to confirm the full translation.
The Russian text is:
"Славен Ты, о Господи мой Боже! Ты, воистину, Царь царей. Ты даруешь власть, кому пожелаешь, и лишаешь ее, кого пожелаешь. Ты возвышаешь, кого пожелаешь, и принижаешь, кого пожелаешь. Ты даруешь победу, кому пожелаешь, и приводишь к позору, кого пожелаешь. Ты даруешь богатство, кому пожелаешь, и ввергаешь в нищету, кого пожелаешь. Кому пожелаешь, даруешь Ты господство над кем пожелаешь. В длани Своей держишь Ты все мироздание, и силой Своего владычного повеления являешь Ты к жизни, кого пожелаешь. Воистину, Ты Всеведущий, Всемогущий, Господь силы."

I will search for the entire Russian text to find its English translation.
The search provided the full English translation. I will now output it according to the user's instructions.

Glorified art Thou, O Lord my God! Thou art in truth the King of kings. Thou dost confer sovereignty upon whomsoever Thou willest and dost seize it from whomsoever Thou willest. Thou dost exalt whomsoever Thou willest and dost abase whomsoever Thou willest. Thou dost render victorious whomsoever Thou willest and dost bring humiliation upon whomsoever Thou willest. Thou dost bestow wealth upon whomsoever Thou willest and dost reduce to poverty whomsoever Thou willest. Thou dost cause whomsoever Thou willest to prevail over whomsoever Thou willest. Within Thy grasp Thou dost hold the empire of all created things and through the potency of Thy sovereign behest Thou dost call into being whomsoever Thou willest. Verily Thou art the Omniscient, the Omnipotent, the Lord of power.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
fied art Thou, my compassionate Lord! I implore Thee by the world-encircling ocean of Thy holy Word, by the manifold signs of Thy supreme sovereignty, by the compelling tokens of Thy divinity, and by the hidden mysteries that abide in Thy knowledge, to grant me Thy grace to serve Thee and Thine chosen ones, and to enable me to offer up, in accordance with Thy Book, Thy Huqúqu'lláh.

It is I, O my Lord, who layeth his love upon Thy realm of glory and clingeth tenaciously to the hem of Thy bounty. O Thou Lord of all being, Thou Ruler in the realm of names! I implore Thee, deny me not what Thou possessest, nor withhold from me that which Thou hast destined for Thine chosen ones.

I fervently implore Thee, O Thou Lord of all names and Creator of the heavens, to aid me through Thy strengthening grace to be so steadfast in Thy Cause that the vanities of the world may not exclude me from Thee as a veil, nor the mighty disturbance, by which the evildoers have arisen to lead Thy people astray in Thy days, hinder me. Ordain for me, then, O Thou the object of my heart's desire, the good of this and of the future world. Verily, Thou art powerful to do as Thou wishest. There is none other God besides Thee, the Ever-Forgiving, the Most Gracious.
prayers.name: Ḥuqúqu’lláh (LLM translated)

lo.cat: ບົດອະທິຖານລະຫວ່າງຖືສິນອົດ

prayers.text:
In His Name, the Most Glorious, the Most High, the All-Glorious.

Praise be to Thee, O Lord my God! O Thou Who art my God, my Master, my Lord, my Helper, my Hope, my Refuge, and my Light.

I beseech Thee, through Thy Hidden and Treasured Name, which no one knoweth except Thyself, to protect the bearer of this Tablet from all calamities and pestilential diseases, and from every wicked person, whether male or female; from the evil wrought by the makers of mischief, and from the plots of the unbelievers.

Ward off from him also, O my God, all pain and suffering, O Thou Who holdest in Thy hand the dominion of all things. Thou art, indeed, the Almighty over all things. Thou doest whatsoever Thou wishest, and commandest as Thou pleasest.
prayers.name: Prayer for Protection (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
He is the Most Glorious!

O my merciful Lord! This is a hyacinth that hath sprung up in the garden of Thy favour, and a branch that hath appeared in the orchard of true knowledge. Grant, O Lord of bounty, that he may be refreshed by the invigorating breezes of Thy grace at all times, and cause him, O merciful Lord, to be verdant and blooming through the showers of the clouds of Thy favour!

Verily, Thou art the All-Glorious.
prayers.name: For the Children (LLM translated)

prayers.text: I have awakened in Thy shelter, O my God, and it behooves him who seeketh this shelter to abide within the tabernacle of Thy protection and the fortress of Thine invincibility.
prayers.name: For children (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
He is God!

O Lord! How shall we thank Thee! Thy bounties are limitless, and our gratitude but limited. How can the limited render thanks to the limitless? Incapable are we of offering thanks for Thy mercies. Utterly powerless, we turn unto Thy Kingdom, and beg Thee to increase Thy bestowal and bounty. Thou art the Giver, Thou art the Bestower, Thou art the Powerful.
prayers.name: Praise and Gratitude (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O God! Unto Thee I flee for refuge and to all Thy signs do I direct my heart.

O God! Whether in travel or at home, in my occupation or in my task, I place my whole trust in Thee.

Grant me, O my God, Thy sufficient help, that I may become independent of all things, O Thou Who art infinite in Thy mercy.

Bestow upon me, O my God, my share, as Thou hast decreed, and make me content with whatsoever Thou hast commanded me.

Thou art absolute sovereign to command.
prayers.name: Detachment (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
He is the Most Holy, the Most Glorious.

In the name of God, the Compasionate, the Merciful! Praise be to God, the Lord of all worlds!

O Thou my God, my Refuge and my Shelter! How can I fitly call upon Thee, when even the most wondrous words of glorification and the most eloquent praises are inadequate, O Thou Almighty and Forgiving, knowing that the tongue of every eloquent speaker stammereth, and every eulogy from human pen or tongue becometh confused in its endeavour to extol even one of the signs of Thy almighty power or to glorify a single Word which hath been created by Thee. Verily the wings of the birds of human intellect are broken in their attempt to soar to the spheres of Thy divine holiness, and the spiders of vain fancy are unable to weave their fragile web upon the loftiest pinnacles of the canopy of Thy knowledge. Thus there is no recourse for me but to acknowledge my powerlessness and shortcomings, and there is no abode for me save in the depths of poverty and destitution. Verily, powerlessness to comprehend Thee is the essence of comprehension, confession of shortcomings is the only means of reaching Thy presence, and acknowledgment of poverty is the source of true riches.

O Lord! Graciously assist me and Thy devoted servants in our servitude to Thy Exalted Threshold, strengthen us in our supplications to Thy divine holiness, and enable us to be humble and submissive at the door of Thy oneness. Make my steps powerful in Thy path, O my Lord, and enlighten my heart with the rays that shine from the heaven of Thy mysteries. Quicken my spirit with the animating breeze diffused from the paradise of Thy forgiveness and pardon, and gladden my soul through the refreshing breath conveyed from the meads of Thy holiness. Verl
prayers.name: Prayers, translated on the occasion of the centenary in 2021 of the passing of the Master (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
* Selections from the Tablet of Wisdom
# In the Name of the Creative, the All-Knowing, the Wise
A Book which the Most Merciful hath sent down from the realm of Revelation, and which is the spirit of life unto them that are in the world. Exalted be God, the Lord of all worlds.
Wherein he that remembereth God, his Lord, doth remember Him; verily it is the Most Noble in a Great Tablet.
O Muhammad! Hear the summons from the centre of glory, from the Lote-tree that is raised on saffron plains: Verily, there is none other God but Me, the All-Knowing, the All-Wise.
Be as the breezes of the Most Merciful unto the trees of existence and their nurturer, in the name of thy Lord, the Just, the All-Informed.
We desired to remind thee of that whereby men may be reminded, that they may relinquish what is with them and turn their faces toward God, the Lord of the sincere.

We do counsel the servants in these days wherein the face of justice is obscured, and the cheek of ignorance hath become radiant, and the veil of reason is rent asunder, and rest and faithfulness have vanished, and tribulation and affliction have overflowed; and in these days wherein covenants have been broken and agreements disregarded, no soul knoweth what doth guide him or what doth lead him astray.

Say: O people, relinquish ye vices and embrace virtues.
Be a good example unto men, and a page wherein they may be reminded.
Whosoever riseth to serve the Cause, it behoveth him to proclaim wisdom and strive to remove ignorance from amongst mankind.
Say: Unite in your word, and be of one accord in your opinion, and make your radiance better than your twilight, and your morrow better than your yesterday.
Man's preferment lieth in service and perfection, not in adornment, nor in riches and wealth.
Let your words be purified from all vain desires, and your deeds cleansed from hypocrisy.
Say: Spend not the precious moments of your lives in the gratification of your lower selves, nor limit your concerns to your own personal benefit.
Give when ye have, and be patient when ye have not; for verily, after every hardship cometh ease, and with every distress there is relief.
Avoid idleness and sloth, and cling to that which profiteth the world, whether it be small or great.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:

## Written to the Bahá'í friends in the United States and Canada

Written to the Bahá'í friends in the United States and Canada

Whoever travels to various places to teach, let him recite this supplication on the mountain, in the desert, on the land, and by the sea.

O God! O God! Thou seest my weakness, my lowliness, and my humility before Thy creatures; yet, I have believed in Thee and have arisen to spread Thy teachings amongst Thy steadfast servants, relying on Thy power and Thy might.

O God! I am a bird with a broken wing, longing to soar in Thy boundless space. How can I do this save through Thy grace and Thy bounty, Thy confirmation and Thy help.

O God! Have compassion on my weakness, and strengthen me with Thy power. O God! Have compassion on my helplessness, and aid me with Thy might and Thy majesty.

O God! If the breath of the Holy Spirit strengthen Thy weakest creatures, they will achieve all that they desire and will possess whatsoever they wish. Verily, Thou hast aided Thy servants in the past, and although they were Thy weakest creatures, Thy lowliest servants, and the most insignificant of them that dwell on earth, through Thy leave and Thy power they have overcome the noblest amongst Thy people and the most exalted of humankind. Whereas before they were as moths, now they have become eagles; whereas before they were as small streams, now they have become as oceans, through Thy bestowal and Thy grace. Through Thy great bounty, they have become brilliant stars upon the horizon of Thy guidance, birds singing in the eternal rose-gardens, lions roaring in the forests of knowledge and wisdom, and whales swimming in the oceans.
prayers.name: Dituliskan kepada rakan-rakan Bahá'í di Amerika Syarikat dan Kanada (LLM translated)

ms.cat: Dituliskan kepada rakan-rakan Bahá'í di Amerika Syarikat dan Kanada

prayers.text:
O Thou beloved of my heart and soul! I have no refuge save Thee. I raise no voice at dawn save in remembrance and praise of Thee. Thy love encompasses me and Thy grace perfecteth me. My hope is in Thee.

O God, renew my life constantly and provide me with the breaths of the Holy Spirit each moment, that I may remain firm in Thy love, attain great happiness, perceive the manifest light, and be found in the state of utmost peace and devotion.

Verily, Thou art the Giver, the Forgiver, the Merciful.
prayers.name: Gvidado, Asisto kaj Protekto (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
The Baháʼí prayer translated from Arabic to English, preserving paragraph breaks and translating section headers, is as follows:

# The Hidden Words
## He is the Glory of Glories
This is that which hath descended from the realm of glory, uttered by the tongue of power and might, and revealed unto the Prophets of old. We have taken the essence of these words and clothed them with the garment of brevity, as a favour unto men, that they may fulfil the covenant of God and discharge His trusts in their bosoms, and that they may be of the winners of the joy of the spirit in the land of souls.

## O Son of Spirit!
In the first place, know thou of a certainty that thou hast been created to know Me, and to worship Me.

## O Son of Spirit!
The best beloved of all things in My sight is Justice; turn not away therefrom if thou desirest Me, and neglect it not that I may confide in thee. By its aid thou shalt see with thine own eyes and not through the eyes of others, and shalt know of thine own knowledge and not through the knowledge of thy neighbor. Ponder this in thy heart; how it behooveth thee to be. Verily justice is My gift to thee and the sign of My loving-kindness. Set it then before thine eyes.

## O Son of Man!
Veiled in My immemorial being and in the ancient eternity of My essence, I knew My love for thee; therefore I created thee, have engraved on thee Mine image and revealed to thee My beauty.

## O Son of Man!
I loved thy creation, hence I created thee. Wherefore, do thou love Me, that I may name thy name and fill thy soul with the spirit of life.

## O Son of Being!
Love Me, that I may love thee. If thou lovest Me not, My love can in no wise reach thee. Know this, O servant.

## O Son of Being!
Thy Paradise is My love; thy heavenly home, reunion with Me. Enter therein and tarry not. This is that which hath been destined for thee in Our kingdom above and Our exalted dominion.

## O Son of Man!
If thou lovest Me, turn away from thyself; and if thou seekest My pleasure, regard not thine own; that thou mayest die in Me and I may eternally live in thee.

## O Son of Spirit!
There is no peace for thee save by renouncing thyself and turning unto Me; for it behooveth thee to glory in My name, not in thine own; to put thy trust in Me and not in thyself, since I desire to be loved alone and above all that is.

## O Son of Being!
My love is My stronghold; he that entereth therein is safe and secure, and he that turneth away shall surely stray and perish.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
I beseech Thee, O my God, by all the transcendent glory of Thy Name, to adorn Thy loved ones with the mantle of justice and to illumine their beings with the light of honesty.

Thou art the One Who hath power to do whatsoever He willeth and Who holdeth with His hand the reins of all things, visible and invisible.
prayers.name: Spiritual Qualities (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O my God, my Master, the Object of my longing. Thy servant wishes to sleep in the shelter of Thy mercy and rest in the tent of Thy grace. He supplicates Thee, that Thou mayest watch over him and that naught may harm him.

I beseech Thee, O my Lord, by Thy Eye, that sleepeth not, protect my eyes, that they may look upon naught but Thee. Make then their gaze keen, that they may discern Thy signs and behold the horizon of Thy Revelation. Thou art He, before Whose manifestations of highest power all power trembles in its very essence.

There is no God but Thou, the Almighty, the All-Subduing, the All-Perfect.
prayers.name: Owes (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
The Huqúqu'lláh is verily a weighty law. All men are under obligation to offer it, for it is the source of grace and abundance and all good. It is a generous gift from God, the Possessor of all, Who bestoweth freely in all His worlds, unto every soul. -Baháʼu'lláh

I beseech Thee, O my Lord, that Thou mayest confirm him.

O Thou Who art the Lord of all creation, and the Goal of all that hath been created, O my Lord, praise be unto Thee. By the inner and outer tongue I bear witness that Thou hast manifested Thyself and revealed Thy signs, hast sent down Thy proofs, and proclaimed Thy teachings. I bear witness that Thou art self-sufficing, needing none other than Thyself, and that Thou art sanctified above all that hath been created. By the transcendent glory of Thy Cause and the supreme power of Thy Word, I beseech Thee to confirm him. He is one who longeth to offer what Thou hast ordained in Thy Book, and by his observance of what is incumbent upon him, to enable the sweet savours of Thy acceptance to be diffused. Verily, Thou art the Most Powerful, the Most Gracious, the All-Forgiving, the Most Generous.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text: Glorified art Thou, O Lord my God! Thou art in truth the King of kings. Thou dost confer sovereignty upon whomsoever Thou willest and dost seize it from whomsoever Thou willest. Thou dost exalt whomsoever Thou willest and dost abase whomsoever Thou willest. Thou dost render victorious whomsoever Thou willest and dost bring humiliation upon whomsoever Thou willest. Thou dost bestow wealth upon whomsoever Thou willest and dost reduce to poverty whomsoever Thou willest. Thou dost cause whomsoever Thou willest to prevail over whomsoever Thou willest. Within Thy grasp Thou dost hold the whole of creation, and at Thy behest Thou dost call into being whomsoever Thou pleasest. Verily Thou art the All-Knowing, the All-Powerful, the Lord of Might.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text: We entreat God graciously to help them that have gone astray to become righteous and fair-minded, and to make them aware of that wherein they have been stubborn. He, verily, is the All-Bountiful, the Most Generous. Bar not Thy servants, O my Lord, from the door of Thy mercy, and drive them not away from the court of Thy presence. Help them to dispel the mists of aimless imaginings, and to tear asunder the veils of vain thoughts and hopes. Thou, verily, art the Possessor of all, the Most High. No God is there but Thee, the Almighty, the All-Loving.
prayers.name: Guidance (for the erring) (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
THE TABLET OF AHMAD

He is the King, the All-Knowing, the Wise!

     Lo, the Nightingale of Paradise singeth upon the twigs of the Tree of Eternity, with holy and sweet melodies, proclaiming to the sincere ones the glad tidings of the nearness of God, calling the believers in the Divine Unity to the court of the Presence of the Generous One, informing the severed ones of the message which hath been revealed by God, the King, the Glorious, the Peerless, guiding the lovers to the seat of sanctity and to this resplendent Beauty.

     Verily this is that Most Great Beauty, foretold in the Books of the Messengers, through Whom truth shall be distinguished from error and the wisdom of every command shall be tested. Verily He is the Tree of Life that bringeth forth the fruits of God, the Exalted, the Powerful, the Great.

     O Aḥmad! Bear thou witness that verily He is God and there is no God but Him, the King, the Protector, the Incomparable, the Omnipotent. And that the One Whom He hath sent forth by the name of ‘Alí was the true One from God, to Whose commands we are all conforming.

     Say: O people be obedient to the ordinances of God, which have been enjoined in the Bayán by the Glorious, the Wise One. Verily He is the King of the Messengers and His Book is the Mother Book did ye but know.

     Thus doth the Nightingale utter His call unto you from this prison. He hath but to deliver this clear message. Whosoever desireth, let him turn aside from this counsel and whosoever desireth let him choose the path to his Lord.

     O people, if ye deny these verses, by what proof have ye believed in God? Produce it, O assemblage of false ones.

     Nay, by the
prayers.name: THE TABLET OF AHMAD (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
(Naw-Rúz is the first day of the Bahá’í year)

Praise be to Thee, O my Lord! for Thou hast ordained
Naw-Rúz to be a festival for those who have observed the Fast because of their love for Thee and have abstained from all that hath been forbidden Thee. Confer, O my Lord, upon my soul the fire of Thy love and the heat of the Fast which Thou hast commanded, that it may be quickened in Thy cause and be occupied with Thy praise and remembrance.

For Thou hast, O my Lord, invested them with the raiment of the Fast which Thou hast appointed for them, and with the same beauty, invest them also with the raiment of Thine acceptance, through Thy bounty and the abundance of Thy grace. For the deeds of men are all dependent on Thee, and follow Thy command. If Thou dost accept him who hath broken the Fast as though he had observed it, such a one shall be accepted as though he had been of old, who had ever observed the Fast. And if Thou dost accept him who hath observed the Fast and hath broken it, such a one shall be joined with them who have caused the Robe of Revelation to be rent, and have been far removed from the crystal waters of this life.

Thou art He through Whom the cry of "Glory be to Thee in all Thy works" hath been raised, and the strain of "Obedient are we to Thy command" hath been sung. Let this Thy station, O my Lord, be as though it were unto Thy messengers, that they may know the dependence of all things on Thy plea and Thy word, and the perfection of every deed according to Thy pleasure and the measure of Thine own good-pleasure, and may perceive the reins of the affairs of men to be in the hands of Thy providence and Thy command. Let this be made known unto them, that naught can hinder them from Thy Beauty, on this day whereon Christ addressed: "All dominion is Thine, O Thou Creator of spirits"; and Thy Friend (Muhammad) cried: "Praised be Thou, O Thou the Desired, for Thou hast manifested Thy Beauty and hast written for the chosen ones..."
prayers.name: NAWR-RÚZ (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
* Naw-Rúz, March 21st, is the first day of the Bahá'í year.

Praised be Thou, O my God, that Thou hast set Naw-Rúz as a festival day for those who have observed the fast out of love for Thee and have abstained from all that is abhorrent to Thee. Grant, O my Lord, that the fire of Thy love and the heat produced by the fast commanded by Thee may cause them to be inflamed in Thy Cause, and make them to be engaged in Thy praise and in the remembrance of Thee.

Since Thou hast adorned them, O my Lord, with the adornment of the fast prescribed by Thee, adorn them also with the adornment of Thy acceptance, through Thy grace and tender mercy. For the deeds of men are all dependent on Thy pleasure, and have been determined by Thy command. Shouldst Thou regard him who hath broken the fast as one who hath observed it, such a one would be counted among them who have kept the fast unto eternity. And shouldst Thou determine that he who hath observed the fast hath broken it, that person would be counted among those who have stained the Robe of Thy Revelation with dust, and were far removed from the crystal waters of this living Fountain.

Thou art He by Whom the Standard, 'Praiseworthy art Thou in Thy deeds,' hath been hoisted, and the Banner, 'Obeyed art Thou in Thy command,' unfurled. Make this Thy Cause known unto Thy servants, O my God, that they may be apprised that the excellence of all things dependeth on Thy behest and Thy Word, and the virtue of every deed is determined by Thy leave and the pleasure of Thy will, and may recognize that the reins of men's actions are within the grasp of Thy acceptance and Thy command. Make it known unto them, that naught may shut them out from Thy Beauty, in this day whereon Christ crieth out: 'All dominion be unto Thee, O Thou the Best-Beloved, because Thou hast revealed Thy Beauty, and inscribed for Thy chosen ones who shall make them attain the seat of the revelation of Thy Most Great Name, whereby all nations have lamented except those who have detached themselves from all save Thee,
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Words of Wisdom
# HE IS THE MOST EXALTED, THE MOST HIGH

The root of all good
Is reliance upon God and submission to His command and contentment with His good pleasure.

The root of wisdom
Is the fear of God, glorified be His mention, and dread of His might and scourges, and apprehension of the manifestations of His justice and decree.

The summit of religion
Is the acknowledgment of that which hath been revealed from God and the observance of that which hath been ordained in His immutable Book.

The root of might
Is contentment with that which hath been bestowed and sufficiency with that which hath been decreed for him.

The root of love
Is the turning of the servant towards the Beloved and turning away from all else, and that his desire be naught but what his Lord hath desired.

The root of remembrance
Is to stand firm in the remembrance of the Remembred One and to forget all else beside Him.

The summit of reliance
Is the servant's striving and earning in this world, and his seeking refuge in God, and confining his gaze to the bounty of his Lord, for unto Him return all the affairs of the servant in his return and abode.

The summit of detachment
Is turning towards the direction of God and approaching Him and gazing upon Him and bearing witness before Him.

The summit of innate nature
Is the acknowledgment of poverty and voluntary submission before God, the King, the Mighty, the Chosen.

The summit of benevolence
Is the servant's manifesting that which God hath bestowed upon him and his gratitude in all circumstances and at all times.

The summit of commerce
Is My love, through which everything is rendered independent of everything else, and without it everything is in need of everything else. And this is what hath been inscribed by the Pen of Resplendent Glory.

The summit of faith
Is paucity of speech and abundance of action. And whoso's words exceed his deeds, know ye that his non-existence is better than his existence and his annihilation better than his survival.

The root of well-being
Is silence and foresight, and withdrawal from mankind.

The summit of aspiration
Is for one to spend upon himself and upon his family and the poor among his brethren in his religion.

The summit of power and courage
Is the exaltation of the Word of God and steadfastness
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
The English translation of the prayer is as follows:

### Tablet of Carmel (Loh Karmel)

**1.** All glory be to this Day, the Day in which the fragrances of mercy have been wafted over all created things, a Day so blest that past ages and centuries can never hope to rival it, a Day in which the countenance of the Ancient of Days hath turned towards His holy seat. Thereupon the voices of all created things, and beyond them those of the Concourse on High, were heard calling aloud: "Haste thee, O Carmel, for lo, the light of the countenance of God, the Ruler of the Kingdom of Names and Fashioner of the heavens, hath been lifted upon thee".

**2.** Seized with transports of joy, and raising high her voice, she thus exclaimed: "May my life be a sacrifice to Thee, inasmuch as Thou hast fixed Thy gaze upon me, hast bestowed upon me Thy bounty, and hast directed towards me Thy steps. Separation from Thee, O Thou Source of everlasting life, hath well nigh consumed me, and my remoteness from Thy presence hath burned away my soul. All praise be to Thee for having enabled me to hearken to Thy call, for having honoured me with Thy footsteps, and for having quickened my soul through the vitalizing fragrance of Thy Day and the shrilling voice of Thy Pen, a voice Thou didst ordain as Thy trumpet-call amidst Thy people. And when the hour at which Thy resistless Faith was to be made manifest did strike, Thou didst breathe a breath of Thy spirit into Thy Pen, and lo, the entire creation shook to its very foundations, unveiling to mankind such mysteries as lay hidden within the treasuries of Him Who is the Possessor of all created things".

**3.** No sooner had her voice reached that most exalted Spot than We made reply: "Render thanks unto thy Lord, O Carmel. The fire of thy separation from Me was fast consuming thee, when the ocean of My presence surged before thy face, cheering thine eyes and those of all creation, and filling with delight all things visible and invisible. Rejoice, for God hath in this Day established upon thee His throne, hath made thee the dawning-place of His signs and the dayspring of the evidences of His Revelation. Well is it with him that circleth around thee, that proclaimeth the revelation of thy glory, and recounteth that which the bounty of the Lord thy God hath showered upon thee.

**4.** Seize thou the Chalice of Immortality in the name of thy Lord, the All-Glorious, and give thanks unto Him, inasmuch as He, in token of His mercy unto thee, hath turned thy sorrow into gladness, and transmuted thy grief into blissful joy. He, verily, loveth the spot which hath been made the seat of His throne, which His footsteps have trodden, which hath been honoured by His presence, from which He raised His call, and upon which He shed His tears.

**5.** Call out to Zion, O Carmel, and announce the joyful tidings: He that was hidden from mortal eyes is come! His all-conquering sovereignty is manifest; His all-encompassing splendour is revealed. Beware lest thou hesitate or halt. Hasten forth and circumambulate the City of God that hath descended from heaven, the celestial Kaaba round which have circled in adoration the favoured of God, the pure in heart, and the company of the most exalted angels. Oh, how I long to announce unto every spot on the surface of the earth, and to carry to each one of its cities, the glad-tidings of this Revelation—a Revelation to which the heart of Sinai hath been attracted, and in whose name the Burning Bush is calling: 'Unto God, the Lord of Lords, belong the kingdoms of earth and heaven.' Verily this is the Day in which both land and sea rejoice at this announcement, the Day for which have been laid up those things which God, through a bounty beyond the ken of mortal mind or heart, hath destined for revelation. Ere long will God sail His Ark upon thee, and will manifest the people of Bahá who have been mentioned in the Book of Names."
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
All majesty and all glory, my knowing You,

My God, all majesty and glory, all sovereignty and splendor, and grandeur and brilliance are Yours alone. You give sovereignty to whomever You wish, and You take it away from whomever You will. There is no God but You, the Possessor of all, the Most High. You are the One who created the universe and all that dwells within it from nothingness. Nothing is worthy in Your sight but You, and everything else besides You is like refuse before Your holy throne, and nothing compared to Your glory. It is beyond my power to praise Your virtues. I have no other praise to offer than what You Yourself have said in Your Book (the Qur'an) praising Yourself: "No vision can grasp Him, but He grashes all vision. He is uncomprehending, yet All-Aware." My God, glory is Yours, and no matter how keen or sharp an eye or intellect, it cannot grasp even the smallest of Your signs. Truly, You are God, and there is no God but You. Only You can express Your attributes, and no praise but Yours reaches Your divine court, and no one but You can comprehend Your attributes. Glory be to You, and You are one whom none can describe but Yourself, for to adequately praise Your virtues or to comprehend the inner reality of Your essence transcends human capacity. If Your creatures were to describe You, or if anyone other than You claimed to know You, it would surely be far removed from Your glory. My God, my knowing You is solely because You revealed Yourself to me, and had You not revealed Yourself to me, I would not have known You. My worshipping You is because You called me to You, and had it not been for Your call, I would not have worshipped You.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Okay, I will translate the provided Baháʼí prayer from zh-Hans to English. I will ensure to output ONLY the translated text, with no introduction, commentary, or quotes, and preserve all paragraph breaks and section headers.
I was unable to find an existing translation through a direct web search. I will proceed with translating the prayer myself, focusing on accuracy, preserving paragraph breaks, and ensuring section headers are translated, while strictly adhering to your output format requirements.My Lord! To be near Thee is my desire, Thy presence is my hope, remembrance of Thee is my longing, Thy glorious heaven is my goal, Thy sanctuary is my dwelling, Thy Holy Name is my remedy, Thy love is the light of my soul, and service to Thee is my highest aspiration! Through Thy Name, let those who know Thee soar to the pinnacle of Thy knowledge, and let those who are devoted to Thee enter the heaven of Thy grace. By Thy Name, I beseech Thee to help me turn towards Thy Holy Face, to gaze upon Thee, and to glorify Thy majesty.

My Lord! Apart from Thee, I have forgotten everything, and turned my whole heart to the dawn of Thy bounty; I have cast aside all else, hoping to draw nigh unto Thy heaven. Look upon me! I have lifted my eyes to that Throne which radiates Thy brilliant countenance. My Beloved! Bestow upon me the quality of firm belief in Thy Cause, and let not the doubts of the unbelievers hinder my devotion to Thee.

Verily! Thou art the All-Powerful God, the Helper in peril, the Peerless in glory, the Almighty.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O my God, I know not what Fire Thou hast kindled in Thine own land. The dust of the earth cannot obscure its light, nor can water quench its flame. The peoples of the entire world are powerless to withstand its might. Happy is he who draweth nigh unto it and hearkeneth to its voice.

O my God, by Thy bounty Thou hast guided some unto it, whilst others Thou hast kept back from it on account of the works their hands have wrought. Those who hastened unto it and attained unto it, gave their lives in Thy path, so desirous were they of gazing on Thy beauty, and ascended unto Thee, detached from aught else in this world.

O my Lord, by the power of this Fire which is ablaze in the Kingdom of Creation, I implore Thee to remove all veils that have hindered me from drawing nigh to the throne of Thy grandeur and standing before the door of Thy gate. O my God, ordain for me all the blessings Thou hast destined in Thy Book, and remove me not far from the tabernacle of Thy mercy. It is in Thy power to do what Thou willest. Verily, Thou art the All-Powerful, the Most Generous.
prayers.name: DETACHMENT (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O Lord! Thou seest the hands of hope stretched forth towards the heaven of Thy grace and Thy bounty. It is my hope that Thou wilt fill them with Thy favors and Thy gifts.

    O my God! Ordain for us and for our parents... "the blessing" Thou hast sent down. Then, out of the ocean of Thy wealth and Thy gifts, bestow upon us what Thou hast willed.

    Then, O our Beloved! Accept from us what we have done in Thy path. Verily, Thou art the All-Befriending, the All-Wise, the All-Knowing, the Ever-Forgiving, the Most Merciful.
prayers.name: Parents (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O Lord, Thou art the eternal King, Creator of peoples, Builder of every bone and dissolver, Glory be to Thee! Count me among those who have turned away from all things other than Thyself, who have turned their faces towards Thee, and who, through the afflictions Thou hast ordained, have turned towards Thy gifts. I beseech Thee in Thy name, by which Thou didst call all the human race to the horizon of Thy majesty and glory, and Thy servants to the throne of Thy grace and Thy bounty.

My Lord, I have firmly grasped the hem of Thy loving-kindness, and have clung with constancy to the border of the robe of Thy bounty. Therefore, grant Thou that I may be enabled to turn to Him Whom those who have broken Thy covenant, and who believe not in Thee nor in Thy signs, have arrayed themselves in opposition—to Him Who is the object of adoration of all the human race—send down upon me from the clouds of Thy grace that which shall purge from my heart the remembrance of all save Thee.

My Lord, in Thy days, the fragrances of Thy raiment, and when the beauties of the light of Thy countenance have appeared, do not deny me the breathings of Thy revelation; for Thou art powerful to do that which is Thy pleasure; nothing can withstand Thy will, nor can anything frustrate what Thou hast decreed by Thy sovereignty.

There is no God other than Thee, the All-Powerful, the All-Wise.
He is the Mighty, the Forgiving, the Compassionate;

O God, my God! Thou seest Thy servants in the abyss of error and iniquity, O Thou, the Desire of the world, where is the light of Thy divine guidance? Thou knowest their helplessness and their weakness; O Thou, in Whose hand are all the powers of heaven and earth, where is Thy might?

My Lord, my God, I implore Thee by the beauty of the lights of Thy loving-kindness, and by the ocean of Thy knowledge and wisdom, that I may be among those who have honored the ordinances that are to be found in Thy Book, so that it be Thy will, and I beseech Thee by Thy word, through which Thou hast caused the peoples of Thy kingdom to believe. Ordain for me, O my Lord, what Thou hast ordained for Thy faithful ones, for those who, drinking the wine of the Divine Knowledge from the cup of Thy bounty, have hastened to fulfill Thy purpose, and to preserve Thy covenant and Thy new order; ordain for me also that which Thou hast ordained for them.

For Thou art powerful to do whatsoever Thou pleasest. There is no God other than Thee, the All-Knowing, the All-Wise.

O Lord, O Thou, the Lord of all mankind, ordain for me by Thy bounty that which shall enrich me in this world and in the world to come, and draw me nigh unto Thee. There is no God other than Thee, the Mighty, the Glorious.

O Lord, establish Thou our steps upon Thy path, and strengthen our hearts to obey Thee, turn our faces towards the beauty of Thy oneness, gladden our minds with the signs of Thy divine unity, that the existence of all living beings may sing praises unto Thee in the vision of Thy greatness, clothe Thou our bodies with the raiment of Thy grace, remove from our eyes the veil of sinfulness, and give us to drink the cup of Thy bounty. O Lord, let the holy, exceeding joy of prayer, a prayer that is exalted beyond all words and letters, higher than all speech and utterances, fill our souls, that all things may be transformed into naught before the manifestation of Thy beauty, through Thy merciful words and Thy divine life.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

lo.cat: ເພື່ອຄວາມໝັ້ນຄົງໃນພຣະປະທິນຍາ

prayers.text:
Praise be to You, O Lord my God. Here is Your servant, who in Your hands has drunk of the waters of Your mercy, and tasted the sweetness of Your love on this day, which is Yours.

I implore You, by the glory of the manifestation of Your Names, to protect him from every tribulation that would hinder him from Your love, or turn his face away from beholding You; protect him, so that the snares of the heedless may be unable to turn him away from the path of Your good pleasure. I implore You to bestow upon him Your bounty, and to exalt him so that he may behold the world and realize that it is a fleeting thing, like the blink of an eye. In Your limitless power, protect him from all that You abhor.

Verily, You are his Lord, and the Lord of all creation.
prayers.name: Prayer (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
My God, my God! Your maidservant calls upon You, turns her face towards You, entrusts her hope to You, desires Your celestial bounties upon him, wishes You to reveal Your spiritual mysteries to him, and humbly prays You to cast Your light upon him from Your Divine Countenance.

My Lord! Open the eyes of my husband. Gladden his heart with the light of Your knowledge. Draw his thoughts to Your radiant beauty, reveal Your manifest glory before him, and restore his spirit.

My Lord! Remove the veil that covers the eyes of my husband. Send down upon him the abundant rain of Your bounty, intoxicate him with the wine of Your love, and make him one of those angelic beings whose souls soar in the celestial realm even while their feet walk upon this earth. Make him a lamp of precious jewel, shining with the light of Your wisdom among Your people.

You are the All-Precious, the Ever-Bestowing, the Generous.
prayers.name: A Prayer for a Husband (LLM translated)

prayers.text: O Lord! Thou art the Remover of all anguish and the Dispeller of every grief. Thou art He Who banisheth every sorrow and setteth free every slave, the Redeemer of every soul. O Lord! Grant deliverance through Thy mercy, and reckon me among such servants of Thine as have gained salvation.
prayers.name: SUPPORT IN TIMES OF DIFFICULTY (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
**Translation in Progress**

I'm now using Google Search to translate the provided Herero text into English, ensuring all formatting and section headers are preserved.

# EVENING

O Lord, my God, my Master, the Goal of my desire! This servant of Thine seeketh to sleep in the shelter of Thy mercy, and to repose beneath the canopy of Thy grace, imploring Thy care and Thy protection.

I beg Thee, O my Lord, by Thine eye that sleepeth not, to guard mine eyes from beholding aught beside Thee. Sharpen, then, their sight that they may behold Thy signs, and gaze upon the Horizon of Thy Revelation. Thou art He before the revelations of Whose omnipotence the quintessence of power hath trembled.

No God is there but Thee, the Almighty, the All-Subduing, the Unconditioned.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Many a chilled heart, O my God, hath been set ablaze with the fire of Thy Cause, and many a slumberer hath been wakened by the sweetness of Thy voice. How many are the strangers who have sought shelter beneath the shadow of the tree of Thy oneness, and how numerous the thirsty ones who have panted after the fountain of Thy living waters in Thy days!

Blessed is he that hath set himself towards Thee, and hasted to attain the Dayspring of the lights of Thy face.

Blessed is he that hath expended in Thy path what Thou didst bestow upon him through Thy bounty and favor.

Blessed is he who, in his sore longing after Thee, hath cast away all else except Thyself.

Blessed is he who hath enjoyed intimate communion with Thee, and rid himself of all attachment to anyone save Thee.

I beseech Thee, O my Lord, by Him Who is Thy Name, Who, through the power of Thy sovereignty and might, hath risen above the horizon of His prison, to ordain for everyone what becometh Thee and beseemeth Thine exaltation.

Thy might, in truth, is equal to all things.
prayers.name: Prayer (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O my Lord! Let Thy beauty be my food, and Thy presence my drink, and Thy pleasure my hope, and the praise of Thee my work, and remembrance of Thee my companion, and the power of Thy dominion my helper, and Thy habitation my home, and my abode the seat Thou hast hallowed from the limitations Thou hast subjected those who are debarred from Thee as by a veil.

Thou art, in truth, the Almighty, the Most Glorious, the Most Powerful.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)
