prayers.text:
(This prayer is recited in the Shrine of 'Abdu'l-Bahá, and it can also be recited when one is alone.)

If this prayer is recited with utmost humility and sincere yearning, it will bring joy to the heart of this Servant, and it will be as if meeting Him face to face.

He is the Most Glorious!

My God, my God! I pray humbly, shedding tears, stretching forth my hands towards Thee in supplication, and bowing my face in the dust of Thy Threshold, which is greater than the wisdom of the learned and the praise of all who glorify Thee. Look upon this Servant, who is humbly kneeling before Thy gate, with the eye of Thy loving-kindness and grace, and immerse him in the Ocean of Thine eternal bounty and favor.

O Lord! He is a poor and lowly servant of Thine, attracted by Thy might, imploring Thee earnestly, captured by Thy hand, praying to Thee in supplication, trusting in Thee, shedding tears before Thee and calling upon Thee, begging thus:

O Lord, my God! Bestow Thy blessings upon me in serving Thy beloved ones, strengthen me in striving for Thee, and illumine my eyes with the light of worshipping Thy Holy Threshold and prostrating before the Abode of Thy Glory. Assist me in self-abnegation before Thy celestial gate, and grant me Thy grace to detach myself from all things in Thy holy world. O Lord! Bestow upon me from the chalice of the virtue of self-abnegation, envelop me with this robe of virtue, and immerse me in this ocean of virtue. Make me dust on the path trodden by Thy beloved ones, and bless me to offer my soul as a sacrifice on the earth where the feet of Thy chosen servants have trod, on the path of the Most Glorious Lord.

Thy servant thus prays, facing Thee at dawn and at dusk. Fulfill the desires of his heart, O Lord! Illumine his heart, and his breast.
prayers.name: TABLET OF VISITATION OF 'ABDU'L-BAHÁ (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O Lord! Grant victory befitting Thy servants who endure in Thy time, for they have sacrificed their lives in Thy path. Grant that which will soothe their hearts, gladden their spirits, comfort them, and bring peace to their bodies. Aid their souls to ascend unto the presence of the most glorious Lord, and to reach the loftiest paradise and the luminous abode which Thou hast ordained for the wise and true of heart. Verily,

Thou knowest all things, and we are but Thy servants, Thy dependents, and the object of Thy compassion. O Lord, our Lord, there is none to have mercy on us save Thee, Whom we beseech. O Lord of this world and the world to come, none save Thee, do we implore for blessings and mercy. We,

are but a symbol of poverty, annihilation, helplessness, and utter destitution, whilst Thou art

the symbol of riches, independence, glory, sovereignty, and unending mercy.

O Lord, recompense us with that which is meet for Thee, in this world and the world to come, with manifold blessings which from Thy presence may descend upon us.

Verily, Thou art the Lord, the Lord of all things. We surrender ourselves unto the hands of Thy might,

and seek that which is with Thee.
prayers.name: Prayer for the Victory of Religion (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
O Generous, O Merciful!

    I bear witness to Your oneness, Your singularity, and I ask from You that which is eternal and everlasting in the kingdom and the dominion. You are the Lord of the Kingdom. You are the King of both the unseen and the seen.

    O Provider! A poor one has turned to Your sea of wealth, and a beggar has sought Your generous shade. Do not deprive him. You are so bountiful that all particles of the universe bear witness to Your mercy, You are so munificent that all beings acknowledge Your generosity.

    This servant, having abandoned all else, clings to the rope of Your mercy and the hem of Your robe of generosity, and supplicates You, and in every state turns to You, and thanks You. If You bless, You are the refuge; if You condemn, Your decree is bowed to. You are so mighty that before Your presence, others appear powerless and weak.

    O Generous! Do not abandon this servant to his own will. You are the Powerful and Capable, You are the Lord of Compassion and Generosity.
prayers.name: Guidance (LLM translated)

prayers.text: O God, my Lord, my Beloved, the Desire of my heart.
prayers.name: Spiritual Development (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
All praise be unto Thee, O my God! I thank Thee for having made me to know the Manifestation of Thy mercy, the Day-Spring of Thy grace, and the Treasury of Thy Revelation. I supplicate by Thy Name, through which the faces of those nigh unto Thee have been made white, and the hearts of those who are devoted to Thee have soared towards Thee. Help me, I implore Thee, to cling to the cord of Thy grace in all times and circumstances, to detach myself from all save Thee, to fix my gaze upon the horizon of Thy Revelation, and to carry out that which Thou hast ordained for me in Thy Tablets.

O my Lord, clothe my body and my spirit with the raiment of Thy bounty and Thy tender mercies. Protect me from whatsoever Thou abhorrest, and assist me and my household to be obedient unto Thee, and to flee from whatsoever stirreth up evil and passion within me.

Verily, Thou art the Lord of all mankind, the Possessor of this world and the world to come. There is no God but Thee, the All-Knowing, the All-Wise.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

th.cat: บทอธิษฐานพื่อตัดความผูกพัน

prayers.text: He is God. O Thou forgiving Lord! These servants were noble souls, and these radiant hearts were made illumined and resplendent through the light of Thy guidance. They drank a brimming cup of the wine of Thy love, and gave ear to eternal mysteries imparted by the melodies of Thy knowledge. They bound their hearts to Thee, broke free from the snare of estrangement, and laid hold of Thy unity. Make these precious souls companions of the inmates of Heaven, and admit them into the circle of Thy chosen ones. Make them near to Thy mysteries in the sanctuaries of Thy Kingdom above, and immerse them in the sea of light. Thou art, in truth, the Forgiving, the Radiant, and the Generous.
prayers.name: Protection (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
The search results provided the English translation of the prayer. I will now output only the translated text as requested.

## Nearness to God

O my God, my God! Glory be to Thee for that Thou hast guided me to the horizon of Thy Revelation, and illumined me with the splendors of the light of Thy grace and mercy, and caused me to utter Thy praise, and made me to behold that which hath been revealed by Thy Pen.

I beseech Thee, O Thou Who art the Lord of the kingdom of names and the Fashioner of earth and heaven, by the rustling of the Divine Lote-Tree, and by Thy most sweet utterance which hath enthralled the realities of all created things, to raise me up in Thy Name amidst Thy servants. I am he who, by day and by night, hath desired to stand before the door of Thy bounty and to present himself before the throne of Thy justice.

O Lord! Cast not away from Thee him who hath clung to the cord of Thy nearness, nor debar him who hath directed his steps towards Thy most sublime station, the summit of glory and the supreme objective, that station wherein every atom crieth out in the most eloquent tongue: 'The earth and the heaven, the glory and the dominion are God’s, the Almighty, the All-Glorious, the Most Generous!'
prayers.name: Cercanía a Dios (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
The text found includes both Korean and English versions, presented side-by-side. The English version appears to be an authorized translation. I will extract the English text, including the translated header and preserving paragraph breaks, and output only that.

## Epistle to Ahmad

He is the King, the All-Knowing, the Wise!
Lo, the Nightingale of Paradise singeth upon the twigs of the Tree of Eternity, with holy and sweet melodies, proclaiming to the sincere ones the glad tidings of the nearness of God, calling the believers in the Divine Unity to the court of the Presence of the Generous One, informing the severed ones of the message which hath been revealed by God, the King, the Glorious, the Peerless, guiding the lovers to the seat of sanctity and to this resplendent Beauty.
Verily this is that Most Great Beauty, foretold in the Books of the Messengers, through Whom truth shall be distinguished from error and the wisdom of every command shall be tested. Verily He is the Tree of Life that bringeth forth the fruits of God, the Exalted, the Powerful, the Great.
O Ahmad! Bear thou witness that verily He is God and there is no God but Him, the King, the Protector, the Incomparable, the Omnipotent.
And that the One Whom He hath sent forth by the name of ‘Alí was the true One from God, to Whose commands we are all conforming.
Say: O people be obedient to the ordinances of God, which have been enjoined in the Bayán by the Glorious, the Wise One. Verily He is the King of the Messengers and His Book is the Mother Book did ye but know.
Thus doth the Nightingale utter His call unto you from this prison. He hath but to deliver this clear message. Whosoever desireth, let him turn aside from this counsel and whosoever desireth let him choose the path to his Lord.
O people, if ye deny these verses, by what proof have ye believed in God? Produce it, O assemblage of false ones.
Nay, by the One in Whose hand is My soul, they are not, and never shall be able to do this, even should they combine to assist one another.
O Ahmad! Forget not My bounties while I am absent. Remember My days during thy days, and My distress and banishment in this remote prison.
And be thou so steadfast in My love that thy heart shall not waver, even if the swords of the enemies rain blows upon thee and all the heavens and the earth arise against thee.
Be thou as a flame of fire to My enemies and a river of life eternal to My loved ones, and be not of those who doubt.
And if thou art overtaken by affliction in My path, or degradation for My sake, be not thou troubled thereby.
Rely upon God, thy God and the Lord of thy fathers. For the people are wandering in the paths of delusion, bereft of discernment to see God with their own eyes, or hear His Melody with their own ears.
Thus have We found them, as thou also dost witness. Thus have their superstitions become veils between them and their own hearts and kept them from the path of God, the Exalted, the Great.
Be thou assured in thyself that verily, he who turneth away from this Beauty hath also turned away from the Messengers of the past and showeth pride towards God from all eternity to all eternity.
Learn well this Tablet, O Ahmad. Chant it during thy days and withhold not thyself therefrom.
For verily, God hath ordained for the one who chanteth it, the reward of a hundred martyrs and a service in both worlds.
These favors have We bestowed upon thee as a bounty on Our part and a mercy from Our presence, that thou mayest be of those who are grateful.
By God! Should one who is in affliction or grief read this Tablet with absolute sincerity, God will dispel his sadness, solve his difficulties and remove his afflictions.
Verily, He is the Merciful, the Compassionate. Praise be to God, the Lord of all the worlds.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

lo.cat: ທັມມະຈາລຶກເຖິງອາໝັດ

prayers.text:
O Lord my God, praise be to Thy Name! Through which time hath ceased, and resurrection become possible, and all in heaven and on earth trembled in awe; I pray Thee to cause to rain from the clouds of Thy favour and the tokens of Thy tender mercy, that gladden the hearts of them that have turned unto Thee and aided Thy Cause.
O my Lord, keep Thy servants and handmaids safe from vain imaginings and illusory thoughts, and grant them to drink of the cup of Thy knowledge that floweth from the hands of Thy bounty.
Thou art, verily, Almighty, Most High, All-Forgiving, Most Generous.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text: O Thou kind Lord! Thou art generous and all-powerful! We are Thy servants sheltered beneath Thy providence. Cast Thy kindly glance upon us. Give light to our eyes, hearing to our ears, and understanding and love to our hearts. Render our souls joyous and happy with Thy glad tidings. O Lord! Point out to us the pathway of Thy kingdom and resuscitate all of us through the breaths of the Holy Spirit. Bestow upon us life everlasting and confer upon us never-ending honor. Unify mankind and illumine the world of humanity. May we all follow Thy pathway, desire Thy good pleasure and seek the mysteries of Thy kingdom. O God! Unite us and connect our hearts with Thy indissoluble bond. Verily, Thou art the Giver, Thou art the Kind One and Thou art the Almighty.
prayers.name: (LLM translated)

prayers.text:
Say: God sufficeth me. He is the One Who holdeth in His hand the kingdom of all things. Through the power of His hosts in the heavens and on earth and whatever lieth between them, He protecteth whomsoever among His servants He willeth. God, in truth, keepeth watch over all things.

         Immeasurable is Thy greatness, O Lord! Guard us from that which is before us and behind us, above our heads, on our right, on our left, beneath our feet and from every other side to which we are exposed. Verily, Thy protection of all things is unfailing.
prayers.name: Prayer for Protection (LLM translated)
